structure.title: Front Matter

nodes.title: Abstract
nodes.summary: Abstract overview of scope and document relationships for Link Services requirements. States that this standard describes the Link Services requirements, while Physical Interface requirements are in `Fibre Channel-Physical Interfaces - 2(FC-PI-2)` and Framing and Signaling requirements are in `Fibre Channel-Physical Framing and Signaling - 2 (FC-FS-2)`. Notes it is recommended for new implementations but does not obsolete the existing Fibre Channel standard. Core topic: high-level abstract and cross-reference to related standards, not protocol detail. Exact named anchors present: `Abstract`, `Link Services requirements`, `Fibre Channel-Physical Interfaces - 2(FC-PI-2)`, `Fibre Channel-Physical Framing and Signaling - 2 (FC-FS-2)`. No tables. No figures. No procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling, state machine, timer/negotiation, or completion/exception conditions.

structure.summary: Front Matter (pp. 1-19): administrative and orientation material for `Fibre Channel Link Services (FC-LS)` `Rev 1.61` / `INCITS xxx-200x`, not protocol behavior. Contains working-draft/approval notices, points of contact, release notes for versions `1.61`, `1.6`, `1.51`, `1.5`, `1.4`, `1.3`, `1.2`, `1.1`, `1.0`, `0.3`, `0.2`, abstract, foreword, committee/task-group rosters, acknowledgements, plus the document map. Key anchors named here: sections `1 Scope`, `2 Normative References`, `3 Definitions and conventions`, `4 Extended Link Services`, `5 FC-4 Link Service`, `6 Login and Service Parameters`, `7 Process Login/Logout`, `8 Virtual Fabrics N_Port Support`; commands/messages including `FLOGI`, `PLOGI`, `LOGO`, `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `PRLI`, `PRLO`, `FDISC`, `ADISC`, `EVFP`, `LKA`. Content types: procedure/flow absent; message/frame format only via TOC/listings; state machine listed (`Figure 1`); response/branch handling listed; completion/exception conditions listed. Figures/tables physically present here: `Figure 1 – State Machine Example` shows notation example; `Figure 2 – Illustration of parameters` shows parameters; `Figure 3 – LS_RJT format` shows reject format; `Figure 4 – Image/Group of Related Processes` shows process grouping; `Figure 5 – Image pairs` shows pair relationships; `Figure 6 – Logical N_Port Initialization` shows initialization; `Figure 7 – A Generic EVFP Transaction` shows EVFP flow; `Table 1 – ISO and American Conventions` defines conventions; `Table 2` through `Table 177` are listed by caption in the contents/tables pages.

structure.title: 1 Scope
structure.summary: Defines the document scope for `FC-LS` and immediately lists `Normative References` relevant to this standard. Core topic: `1 Scope` states that `FC-LS` describes in detail the `Fibre Channel Extended Link Services`. Named anchors present: `FC-LS`, `Fibre Channel Extended Link Services`, `2 Normative References`, `2.1 Overview`, `2.2 Approved references`, `2.3 References under development`, `ANSI INCITS 332-1999`, `ANSI INCITS 332-1999/AM1-2003`, `ANSI INCITS 373-2003`, `ANSI INCITS 387-2004`, `ANSI INCITS 384-2004`, `ANSI INCITS 374-2003`, `ANSI INCITS Project 1619-D`, `FC-AL-2`, `FC-FS`, `FC-GS-4`, `FC-SW-3`, `FC-SB-3`, `FC-FS-2`. No tables. No figures. Content types: references/catalog only; no procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling, state machine, timer/negotiation, or completion/exception conditions.

structure.title: 2 Normative References

nodes.title: 2.1 Overview
nodes.summary: Covers `2.1 Overview` within `2 Normative References`: explains that referenced standards “constitute provisions of this standard,” editions listed were valid at publication, standards may be revised, and users should check for newer editions. Includes availability/source pointers: `ANSI’s Electronic Standards Store (ESS)` at `www.ansi.org`, `Global Engineering Documents`, `15 Inverness Way East, Englewood, CO; 80112-5704`, `(800) 854-7179`. Names adjacent normative-reference anchors appearing in this section block: `2.2 Approved references`, `2.3 References under development`, `ANSI INCITS 332-1999`, `ANSI INCITS 332-1999/AM1-2003`, `ANSI INCITS 373-2003`, `ANSI INCITS 387-2004`, `ANSI INCITS 384-2004`, `ANSI INCITS 374-2003`, `ANSI INCITS Project 1619-D`. No tables. No figures. Content types: none of procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling, state machine, timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 2.2 Approved references
nodes.summary: Core topic: approved normative references for FC-LS, within `2 Normative References`, specifically `2.2 Approved references`, with surrounding context from `2.1 Overview` and `2.3 References under development`. Exact anchors/identifiers listed: `ANSI INCITS 332-1999`, `FC-AL-2`, `ANSI INCITS 332-1999/AM1-2003`, `Amendment 1`, `ANSI INCITS 373-2003`, `FC-FS`, `ANSI INCITS 387-2004`, `FC-GS-4`, `ANSI INCITS 384-2004`, `FC-SW-3`, `ANSI INCITS 374-2003`, `FC-SB-3`, and under development `ANSI INCITS Project 1619-D`, `FC-FS-2`. Also notes ANSI ESS (`www.ansi.org`) and Global Engineering Documents availability contact. Tables: none. Figures: none. nodes.title: 2.3 References under development
nodes.summary: Section `2.3 References under development` indexes in-development normative references for `FC-LS` and tells readers where to check current status or availability. Core content: a publication-time notice that some referenced standards were still under development, with contact via the relevant standards body or organization. Exact identifiers/names present: `ANSI INCITS Project 1619-D`, `Fibre Channel - Framing and Signaling (FC-FS-2)`, `FC-LS`, `2.3 References under development`. It also contextually follows `2 Normative References`, `2.1 Overview`, and `2.2 Approved references`, but this subsection itself lists only the under-development reference above. No tables. No figures. Content types: normative-reference listing; availability/status note. No procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling, state machine, timer/negotiation, or completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 2.4 Other references
nodes.summary: Lists external normative/informative references used by the spec, mainly IETF RFCs plus related Fibre Channel project documents. Core topic: “2.4 Other references” identifies where RFCs may be obtained (`http://www.ietf.org/rfc.html`) and enumerates cited network/transport/application references: `RFC 768` “User Datagram Protocol,” `RFC 791` “Internet Protocol,” `RFC 793` “Transmission Control Protocol,” `RFC 854` “Telnet Protocol Specification,” `RFC 1157` “A Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP),” `RFC 1901` “Introduction to Community-based SNMPv2,” `RFC 2373` “IP Version 6 Addressing Architecture,” `RFC 2460` “Internet Protocol, Version 6 (IPv6) Specification,” `RFC 2616` “Hypertext Transfer Protocol -- HTTP/1.1,” `RFC 2818` “HTTP Over TLS,” `RFC 4338` “Transmission of IPv6, IPv4, and Address Resolution Protocol (ARP) Packets over Fibre Channel.” Also names `FC-GS-5`, `FC-SW-4`, `FCP-3`. No tables, no figures. Content type: references list only.

structure.summary: Normative references section for FC-LS: identifies external standards incorporated by reference and where to obtain them. Anchors: `2 Normative References`, `2.1 Overview`, `2.2 Approved references`, `2.3 References under development`; `ANSI INCITS 332-1999`, `FC-AL-2`; `ANSI INCITS 332-1999/AM1-2003`, `Amendment 1`; `ANSI INCITS 373-2003`, `FC-FS`; `ANSI INCITS 387-2004`, `FC-GS-4`; `ANSI INCITS 384-2004`, `FC-SW-3`; `ANSI INCITS 374-2003`, `FC-SB-3`; `ANSI INCITS Project 1619-D`, `FC-FS-2`; `ANSI’s Electronic Standards Store (ESS)`; `Global Engineering Documents`. No tables. No figures. Content types: reference catalog only; no procedure/flow, no message/frame format, no response/branch handling, no state machine, no timer/negotiation, no completion/exception conditions.

structure.title: 3 Definitions and conventions

nodes.title: 3.1 Overview
nodes.summary: Section `3.1 Overview` is the entry point for Section 3 terminology: it states that, for `FC-LS`, the following definitions, conventions, abbreviations, acronyms, and symbols apply, leading into `3.2 Definitions`. Named anchors visible on this page include `acknowledged class`, `address identifier`, `S_ID`, `D_ID`, `AE Principal Switch`, `AE Secondary Principal Switch`, `AE Switch`, `Alias_ID`, `Alias_Token`, `Arbitrated Loop topology`, `Association_Header`, `Avionics Fabric`, `buffer-to-buffer credit (BB_Credit)`, `buffer-to-buffer Credit_Count (BB_Credit_CNT)`, `B_Port`, `buffer`, `circuit`, and `class of service`, with references to `FC-FS-2`, `FC-SW-4`, `FC-GS-5`, `FC-AL-2`, and `FC-SW-3`. No tables or figures. Content type: terminology/identifier definitions only; no procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling, state machine, timer/negotiation, or completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 3.2 Definitions
nodes.summary: Defines FC-LS terminology and naming conventions used across the spec: service classes, ports, addressing, exchanges/sequences, flow control, Fabric entities, Avionics Fabric/FFI, virtualization, and network protocol terms. Named anchors include `S_ID`, `D_ID`, `Alias_ID`, `Alias_Token`, `BB_Credit`, `BB_Credit_CNT`, `EE_Credit`, `EE_Credit_CNT`, `Domain_ID`, `OX_ID`, `RX_ID`, `X_ID`, `SEQ_ID`, `N_Port_ID`, `N_Port_Name`, `Node_Name`, `Fabric_Name`, `F_Port_Name`, `VF_ID`, `Port VF_ID`, `Process_Associator`, `VFT_Header`, `FFFFFEh`, `FFFFFDh`, `FFFFFFEh`, `SOFc1`, `0000b`, `0010b`, `0011b`, `0100b`, `1111b`, `ROI`, `S_ID||X_ID`. Covers exact term definitions for `AE Principal Switch`, `AE Secondary Principal Switch`, `AE Switch`, `Fast Fabric Initialization (FFI)`, `FFI SW_ILS`, `FFI Link State Record`, `Loop Fabric Address (LFA)`, `Well-known addresses`. Content types: definitions/glossary only; no procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling, state machine, timer/negotiation, or completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 3.3 Editorial Conventions
nodes.summary: Defines editorial rules for interpreting this standard: special capitalization marks terms with defined meanings; lowercase uses normal English meaning. Lists by letters indicate no priority, numbered lists indicate priority order. Uses ISO numbering (space for thousands, comma decimal) and precedence rules: text over tables over figures; most significant bit shown on the left. Reserved frame fields/control bits: sender sets to zero, receiver does not check; `x`/`xx` mean value not relevant. Numeric notation: plain numbers are decimal, suffix `b` is binary, suffix `h` is hexadecimal. Includes `Table 1 – ISO and American Conventions`, showing ISO vs American numeric formatting examples. Content types: message/frame format; completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 3.4 State Machine notation
nodes.summary: Section `3.4 State Machine notation` defines the notation/style used for state machines in this standard, anchored by `figure 1` (“style shown in figure 1”) and the explicit assumption `a) Time elapses only within discrete states.` Use this section when questions ask how standard state machines are represented or what baseline assumptions apply to their execution. Related page content also includes `Table 1 – ISO and American Conventions`, which shows numeric formatting conventions (`0,6` vs `0.6`, `1 000` vs `1,000`) used elsewhere in the document. Content types: `state machine`; limited `procedure/flow` context via notation assumptions. No message/frame format, timer names, response branches, or completion/exception rules are defined here.

nodes.title: 3.5 Abbreviations and acronyms
nodes.summary: Section `3.5 Abbreviations and acronyms` is the glossary/index of abbreviations used by the standard, with note that definitions of several items are included in `3.2`. Contains acronym expansions for protocol, service, timer, identifier, and primitive names such as `ABTS`, `ACK`, `ADVC`, `BB_Credit`, `BB_Credit_CNT`, `CR_TOV`, `E_D_TOV`, `EE_Credit`, `EE_Credit_CNT`, `ELS`, `ELS_Command`, `FLOGI`, `LOGO`, `PLOGI`, `PRLI`, `PRLO`, `R_A_TOV`, `R_T_TOV`, `RTV`, `D_ID`, `S_ID`, `OX_ID`, `RX_ID`, `X_ID`, `WWN`, `SOF`, `EOFdt`, `EOFt`, `NOS`, `OLS`, `LR`, `LRR`, `R_RDY`, `TCP`, `UDP`, `HTTP`, `HTTPS`, `SNMP`. Nearby anchors on same pages: `Figure 1 – State Machine Example` (shows state machine with `S0`, `S1`, transitions `S0:S1`, `S0:S0`, `S1:S0`), `3.6 Symbols`, `3.7 Keywords`. Content types: `timer/negotiation`.

nodes.title: 3.6 Symbols
nodes.summary: Defines the notation used in `3.6 Symbols`: symbol meanings for `||` (concatenation), `m` (micro, e.g., `μm = micrometer`), and `L >>` (`Received from Link`). Core topic is symbol/notation interpretation, not protocol behavior. Useful when a question asks how expressions or directional annotations in this specification should be read. Adjacent text begins `3.7 Keywords` and includes `expected`, but that is outside the symbol definitions. Tables: none. Figures: none. Content types present: notation/definitions only; no procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling, state machine, timer/negotiation, or completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 3.7 Keywords
nodes.summary: Defines normative keyword semantics for the specification’s requirements language in `3.7 Keywords`: `expected`, `ignored`, `invalid`, `mandatory`, `may`, `may not`, `meaningful`, `not meaningful`, `obsolete`, `optional`, `reserved`, `restricted`, `shall`, `should`, `x or xx`. Core topic: how these words classify implementation requirements, unsupported/unused values, applicability of fields/bits, and error/reporting expectations. Exact anchors present: `3.7.1 expected`, `3.7.2 ignored`, `3.7.3 invalid`, `3.7.4 mandatory`, `3.7.5 may`, `3.7.6 may not`, `3.7.7 meaningful`, `3.7.8 not meaningful`, `3.7.9 obsolete`, `3.7.10 optional`, `3.7.11 reserved`, `3.7.12 restricted`, `3.7.13 shall`, `3.7.14 should`, `3.7.15 x or xx`. Content types: completion/exception conditions (e.g., invalid/reserved code values reported as error). Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 3.8 T10 Vendor ID fields
nodes.summary: Defines `3.8 T10 Vendor ID fields`: encoding and padding rules for a `T10 Vendor ID` placed into a field. A `T10 Vendor ID` shall be a string of one to eight characters, recorded in the informal list maintained by `INCITS Technical Committee T10` at `http://www.t10.org`. Byte placement rule: the first character goes in the most significant byte, with successive characters in successively less significant bytes. Unused bytes in the field shall be filled with ASCII space characters `20h`. Exact anchors present: `T10 Vendor ID`, `INCITS Technical Committee T10`, `20h`. No tables. No figures. Content types: message/frame format (field byte layout and padding); completion/exception conditions not present; procedure/flow not present; response/branch handling not present; state machine not present; timer/negotiation not present.

structure.summary: Defines FC-LS section-wide terminology and conventions used by later clauses. Covers 3.1 Overview and 3.2 Definitions, including exact identifiers and fields: acknowledged class, address identifier, `S_ID`, `D_ID`, `AE Principal Switch`, `AE Secondary Principal Switch`, `AE Switch`, `AE_Port`, `Alias_ID`, `Alias_Token`, `Arbitrated Loop topology`, `Association_Header`, `Avionics Fabric`, `BB_Credit`, `BB_Credit_CNT`, `B_Port`, `E_Ports`, `Nx_Ports`, `L_Ports`, and `Fast Fabric Intialization`. Also notes references to FC-FS-2, FC-SW-4, FC-GS-5, FC-AL-2, and FC-SW-3. No tables. No figures. Content type: definitions/glossary only; no procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling, state machine, timer/negotiation, or completion/exception conditions.

structure.title: 4 Extended Link Services

nodes.title: 4.1 Introduction
nodes.summary: Overview of `4.1 Introduction` for `Extended Link Services (ELS)`: defines the generic ELS request/reply model and framing rules before `4.2`. An `ELS` request solicits a destination `Nx_Port`; an `ELS` reply is required unless otherwise specified. Request/reply are a single `Sequence`; `ELS_Command` code is in the first word / first byte of the `Payload` of the first frame only; later frames carry additional `Payload` only. `LS_ACC` terminates the `Exchange` by setting `Last Sequence bit` to one on the last reply frame. Request and reply occur within one `Exchange`; normal `Exchange`/`Sequence` rules from `FC-FS-2` apply. `TYPE` for ELS frames = `01h`; `R_CTL` as in `Table 2 – Extended Link Services Routing Bits and Information Categories` (defines `R_CTL` values: `0001b` `Solicited Data`, `0010b` `Request`, `0011b` `Reply`; others reserved; `CSU` note). Content types: message/frame format, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 4.2 Extended Link Service requests
4.2.1 Introduction
nodes.summary: Core topic: `4.2.1 Introduction` for Extended Link Service requests: when an Nx_Port may send an ELS Sequence, addressing/login prerequisites, `ELS_Command` identification, reply/`Sequence Initiative` rules, and minimum mandatory ELS support. `TYPE=01h`; `R_CTL` uses `0001b` Solicited Data and `0010b` Request / `0011b` Reply. `D_ID`, `S_ID`, `LFA`, `Nx_Port`, `N_Port Login`, `FLOGI`, `PLOGI`, `LOGO`, `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `LINIT`, `LSTS` are explicitly covered. `Table 2 – Extended Link Services Routing Bits and Information Categories`: defines `R_CTL` routing/information categories. `Table 3 – ELS_Command codes`: maps command hex values (`01h`-`A6h`, `7Eh`, `7Fh`, `80h`, `90h`, `97h`, `14h` reserved, others reserved) to description, abbreviation, reference, and `N_Port Login Required`. Contains: [procedure/flow], [message/frame format], [response/branch handling], [completion/exception conditions].

nodes.title: 4.2.2 Sequence and Exchange management
nodes.summary: Section `4.2.2 Sequence and Exchange management` covers ELS Sequence/Exchange rules and adjacent response handling for received ELSs. `Table 4 – Responses to Received ELSs` defines response branching by `N_Port Login` required/not required and whether logged in with the source `N_Port`; outcomes include `LOGO ELS Exchange`, `LS_RJT ELS Sequence`, discard, or normal response, with reason code `"Unable to perform command request"` and explanation `"N_Port Login required"`. Also states `PLOGI ELS request` must be answered with `LS_ACC Link Service Reply` or `LS_RJT`, and `LS_RJT` shall not use `"Command not supported"` for `PLOGI`. Covers `RSI` collision handling with `LS_RJT` reason `"command already in progress"`. Sequence rules: all ELS except `ESTS`, `ESTC`, `ADVC` use a single Exchange; reply terminates by setting `Last_Sequence` bit `(bit 20)` in `F_CTL`. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 4.2.3 Advise Credit (ADVC)
4.2.3.1 Description
nodes.summary: Core topic: `4.2.3.1 Description` defines the purpose and usage constraints of the `ADVC` ELS (`Advise Credit`). `ADVC` is used to advise the destination `Nx_Port` of the estimated end-to-end `Credit` the source `Nx_Port` requests to be allocated. The `ADVC` ELS request shall be a separate `Sequence`; it may also be requested in a separate `Exchange`. Usage reference: `FC-FS-2`. The `ADVC` request may also be used independently from the `Estimate Credit` procedure (`FC-FS-2`). Exact anchors present: `ADVC`, `Advise Credit`, `Nx_Port`, `Credit`, `Sequence`, `Exchange`, `FC-FS-2`, `Estimate Credit`. Content types: procedure/flow, timer/negotiation. No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 4.2.3.2 Protocol
nodes.summary: Core topic: ELS protocol rules for Sequence/Exchange management plus `4.2.3.2 Protocol` for `Advise Credit (ADVC)`. General ELS rules: Request and Reply Sequences may use any class of service; reply uses same class as request; before successful Login use default Login values; after successful Login Originator uses Discard multiple Sequences Error Policy; detect missing Reply Sequence after Sequence Initiative transfer at `2 X R_A_TOV`; on Exchange error abort with `ABTS-LS` and retry with a different Exchange; Sequence abort/retry behavior covers `ABTS`, `BA_ACC`, `ACK`, Abort Sequence bits `01b`, and `E_D_TOV`. ADVC protocol anchor points: `Advise Credit Request Sequence`; `LS_ACC` or `LS_RJT Reply Sequence`. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 4.2.3.3 Request Sequence
nodes.summary: ADVC `4.2.3.3 Request Sequence`: defines ADVC request addressing for Credit revision—`S_ID` identifies the source `Nx_Port` requesting Credit revision, `D_ID` identifies the destination `Nx_Port`. Nearby ELS request/reply sequence management rules on this page include class-of-service rules for Request/Reply Sequences, same-class reply requirement, default Login values before successful Login, `Discard multiple Sequences Error Policy` after Login, timeout/error handling with `2 X R_A_TOV`, `E_D_TOV`, `ABTS`, `ABTS-LS`, `BA_ACC`, `ACK`, and `Abort Sequence bits` = `01b`, including retry/abort behavior with a different Exchange. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions. Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 4.2.3.4 Reply Sequence
nodes.summary: Defines ADVC reply handling for `4.2.3.4 Reply Sequence`: `LS_RJT` signifies rejection of the `ADVC` command; `LS_ACC` signifies successful completion of the `ADVC` function and permanently replaces end-to-end `Credit` for the current `N_Port Login`. Covers `LS_ACC` Payload requirements: revised end-to-end `Credit` in the appropriate Class Service Parameters indicated by `Class Validity bit`; recipient ignores other Service Parameter fields; revised value applies to current `Login` for the `Nx_Port` transmitting the `LS_ACC` Sequence. Includes `Table 6 – ADVC LS_ACC Payload`, showing the `LS_ACC` Payload format, including word `0 = 02h 00h 00h 00h`, `Common Service Parameters`, `N_Port_Name`, `Node_Name`, `Class 1 Service Parameters`, `Class 2 Service Parameters`, `Class 3 Service Parameters`, `Reserved`, `Vendor Version Level`. Content types: message/frame format, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

Section `4.2.3 Advise Credit (ADVC)` covers the `ADVC` ELS: advising a destination `Nx_Port` of the estimated end-to-end Credit a source `Nx_Port` requests to be allocated, including independent use from the Estimate Credit procedure. Named anchors: `ADVC`, `Advise Credit (ADVC)`, `FC-FS-2`, `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `S_ID`, `D_ID`, `Nx_Port`, `Advise Credit Request Sequence`, `LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`. Protocol content is minimal but specific: request is a separate `Sequence`, may be a separate `Exchange`; protocol consists of request then `LS_ACC`/`LS_RJT` reply; addressing defines `S_ID` as source `Nx_Port` requesting Credit revision and `D_ID` as destination `Nx_Port`. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, timer/negotiation. No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 4.2.4 Echo (ECHO)
4.2.4.1 Description
nodes.summary: Echo (ECHO) ELS description and basic protocol scope: `Echo ELS` requests the Recipient to return Payload following `ELS_Command` to the Initiator via `LS_ACC Reply Sequence`, as a simple loopback diagnostic. Covers one-frame `Echo` Request Sequence and one-frame `LS_ACC Reply Sequence`. Names and codes present: `ECHO (10h)`, `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `D_ID`, `S_ID`, `Nx_Port`, `Buffer-to-buffer Receive Data_Field Size - 4`, `Receive Data_Field Size`, `class of service`, `124 bytes`. `Table 7 – ECHO Payload` defines the `ECHO` Payload/message format: Word 0=`ECHO (10h) 00h 00h 00h`, Word 1..n=`ECHO data`, `MSB`, `LSB`, up to max frame length - 4. `LS_RJT` signifies rejection of the `ECHO` command. Content types: message/frame format, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 4.2.4.2 Protocol
nodes.summary: Defines `4.2.4.2 Protocol` for `Echo (ECHO)`: the protocol consists of `a) Echo Request Sequence` and `b) LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence` for the `ECHO` exchange. Core named items present in this section context: `ECHO`, `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `ELS_Command`, `Payload`, `D_ID`, `S_ID`, `Nx_Port`, `Receive Data_Field Size`, `Buffer-to-buffer Receive Data_Field Size`, `Table 7 – ECHO Payload`, code `ECHO (10h)`, value `124 bytes`, and `Word 0`. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, message/frame format, completion/exception conditions. `LS_ACC` returns payload in the same order as received; `LS_RJT` signifies rejection. Table present: `Table 5 – ADVC Payload` — defines `ADVC` payload layout (listed with this section’s extracted tables).

nodes.title: 4.2.4.3 Request Sequence
nodes.summary: Defines `ECHO` Request Sequence for `4.2.4 Echo (ECHO)`: addressing and payload limits for the one-frame request. Core fields: `D_ID` designates destination, `S_ID` designates source; Payload format is in `Table 7 – ECHO Payload`. `Word 0` contains `ECHO (10h) 00h 00h 00h`; `Word 1` starts `MSB ECHO data`; `Word n` ends `LSB`, up to max frame length `- 4`. Size rules branch on whether a Login with the destination `Nx_Port` exists: default `Buffer-to-buffer Receive Data_Field Size - 4` (`124 bytes`) if no Login; otherwise limited by smallest supported `Receive Data_Field Size` of destination `Nx_Port`, the `Fabric`, and source `Nx_Port` for the class of service, minus `4`. Content types: procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 4.2.4.4 Reply Sequence
nodes.summary: Section `4.2.4.4 Reply Sequence` covers `ECHO` reply behavior, specifically rejection handling for the Echo Extended Link Service. Core rule: `LS_RJT` signifies rejection of the `ECHO` command. Named anchors present from the section context: `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `ECHO (10h)`, `D_ID`, `S_ID`, `Nx_Port`, `Receive Data_Field Size`, `Buffer-to-buffer Receive Data_Field Size - 4`, `124 bytes`, `table 7`, `Table 5 – ADVC Payload`. Content types: response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions. Related message/frame format context is referenced via `table 7` for `ECHO Payload` (defines payload layout, including `Word 0 ECHO (10h) 00h 00h 00h` and ECHO data bytes). Included table: `Table 5 – ADVC Payload` — shows `ADVC` payload field layout.

Echo (`ECHO`) Extended Link Service loopback diagnostic: Recipient returns the Payload following `ELS_Command` to the Initiator in the same order using an `LS_ACC` Reply Sequence; both the `ECHO` Request Sequence and `LS_ACC` Reply Sequence shall be one-frame Sequences. Covers `LS_ACC` / `LS_RJT` reply handling, Request Sequence addressing via `D_ID` and `S_ID`, `S_ID`, and Payload sizing rules based on Login existence and `Buffer-to-buffer Receive Data_Field Size`; size limit is `- 4`, default example `124 bytes`. Payload format references `Table 7 – ECHO Payload`: Word 0 = `ECHO (10h) 00h 00h 00h`, followed by `ECHO data`. Also contains `Table 5 – ADVC Payload`, which defines the `ADVC` Payload layout. Content types: procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 4.2.5 Estimate Credit (ESTC)
4.2.5.1 Description
nodes.summary: ESTC ELS description and request framing for estimating minimum Credit to achieve maximum bandwidth between an Nx_Port pair at a given distance. Names/anchors: `ESTC`, `Estimate Credit (ESTC)`, `Nx_Port`, `SOF`, `ESTC Request`, `Data frames`, `Login parameters`, `FC-FS-2`, `S_ID`, `D_ID`, `Establish Streaming frame`, `Payload`, `ELS_Command`, `ESTC (0Ch)`, `00h`. Specifies that the class of the `SOF` identifies the class being estimated, and the destination `Nx_Port` acknowledges `Data frames` per `Login parameters`. Content types: message/frame format, procedure/flow, response/branch handling. Procedures listed: `Estimate Credit Request Sequence`; `No Reply Sequence`. nodes.title: 4.2.15 Fabric Address Notification (FAN)
4.2.15.1 Description
nodes.summary: Core topic: `4.2.15.1 Description` defines `Fabric Address Notification (FAN)` behavior: the `FAN ELS` is sent by Fabric `F_Port (FFFFFEh)` to all previously logged in (via `FLOGI`) attached `NL_Ports` after an initialization event, typically `Loop Initialization`, or other events that may change a port `ID`. Specifies that the `F_Port Controller` reports `F_Port_Name`, `Fabric_Name`, and current `Loop Fabric Address`, using default login parameters in effect before a `FLOGI Request`. Attached ports may authenticate active `Exchanges` and operating parameters such as `BB_Credit`. Requires identical information to all attached `NL_Ports`; if information changes before all are notified, a new initialization event begins. `NL_Ports` shall not initiate a `Reply Sequence`. Content types: response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions. Table `24 – RSI LS_ACC Payload`: defines `RSI LS_ACC Payload` format.

nodes.title: 4.2.16 Loop Initialize (LINIT)

Named anchors present in nearby protocol context: `LSTS`, `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `S_ID`, `D_ID`, `Loop Fabric Address (LFA)`, `Nx_Port`, `LSTS Payload`, `LSTS (72h)`, `00h`. `Table 30 – LINIT Status` shows `Status` field values/states: `Reserved 0`, `Success - The requested function was completed. 1`, `Failure - The requested function could not be completed. 2`, `Reserved 3-255`. `Table 31 – LSTS Payload` defines the request payload format. nodes.title: 4.2.17.3 Request Sequence

Payload uses `Table 31 – LSTS Payload`, which shows the request message/frame format: Word `0` = `LSTS (72h) 00h 00h 00h`. Context on the same page also includes `Table 30 – LINIT Status`, which defines `Status` field state values: `Reserved 0`, `Success - The requested function was completed. 1`, `Failure - The requested function could not be completed. 2`, `Reserved 3-255`. nodes.title: 4.2.17.4 Reply Sequence

Content types: response/branch handling, message/frame format, completion/exception conditions. `LS_ACC Payload` format is given in `Table 32 – LSTS LS_ACC Payload`, showing `Word 0 = 02h 00h 00h 00h`, `FC-FLA Compliance Level - obsolete`, `Reserved`, `Failed Receiver`, `Loop State`, `Current Public Loop Devices bit map`, `Current Private Loop Devices bit map`, and `AL_PA Position Map`. Field rules included: `Failed Receiver field` uses `AL_PA`; valid only when `Loop State` indicates `Loop Failure`; set to `00h` if `FL_Port` detected failure, `F7h` if `Fabric Controller` cannot determine the port.

Section `4.2.17 Loop Status (LSTS)` defines the `LSTS` ELS used to request the `Fabric Controller` to report the state of a specified loop. Payload format is in `Table 31 – LSTS Payload`, which shows the request frame fields: Word `0` = `LSTS (72h) 00h 00h 00h`, with bits `31 .. 24`, `23 .. 16`, `15 .. 08`, `07 .. 00`. Reply Sequence: `LS_RJT` signifies rejection of the `LSTS` command. nodes.title: 4.2.18 Registered State Change Notification (RSCN)
4.2.18.1 Introduction
nodes.summary: 4.2.18.1 Introduction: introduces `Registered State Change Notification (RSCN)` and when a `RSCN ELS` shall be sent to registered `Nx_Ports`—on events affecting the state of one or more `Nx_Ports` or `ULP state` within an `Nx_Port`. Core purpose: timely notification of important state changes (notably `Login state` or `Link state`) to reduce polling traffic, indicate failed nodes, release resources, and announce new devices or operational changes in an online node. Related state context on this page includes `Loop State field`, `Current Public Loop Devices`, `Current Private Loop Devices`, and `AL_PA Position Map`, with states `Reserved`, `Online`, `Loop Failure`, `Initialization Failure`, `Initializing`. `Table 33 – Loop State`: defines Loop State values/status meanings. Content types: state machine, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 4.2.18.2 RSCNs issued by the Fabric Controller
nodes.display_title: 4.2.18.2 RSCNs issued by the Fabric Controller
nodes.raw_title: RSCNs issued by the Fabric Controller
nodes.full_title: 4.2.18.2 RSCNs issued by the Fabric Controller
nodes.summary: Core topic: `4.2.18.2 RSCNs issued by the Fabric Controller` — when the `Fabric Controller` (`FFFFFDh`) shall issue an `RSCN Request` to all registered `Nx_Ports` for an affected `Nx_Port`, and required precondition that Fabric-provided resources such as the `Name Service` are updated before issuance. Enumerates triggering events: implicit `Fabric Logout`, `Loss-of-Signal`, `NOS`, `OLS`, receipt of `FLOGI` with new/different information, loop initialization of affected `L_Port` with `L_bit` set in `LISA Sequence`, new `Fabric Login`, Fabric path/routing changes, other Fabric-detected state changes, and affected `Nx_Port`-issued `RSCN Request` to the `Fabric Controller`. Receiver follow-up operations: `PDISC ELS`, `ADISC ELS`, `Name Service` query, `ULP` query. Content types: procedure/flow; completion/exception conditions. Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 4.2.18.3 RSCN issued by the affected Nx_Port
nodes.display_title: 4.2.18.3 RSCN issued by the affected Nx_Port
nodes.raw_title: RSCN issued by the affected Nx_Port
nodes.full_title: 4.2.18.3 RSCN issued by the affected Nx_Port
nodes.summary: Core topic: when the affected `Nx_Port` itself shall issue an `RSCN Request`, who receives it, and allowed event qualifiers. `4.2.18.3 RSCN issued by the affected Nx_Port` says an `Nx_Port` shall send an `RSCN Request` to the `Fabric Controller` or an `Nx_Port` registered for `RSCN Requests` or both when it detects an event. `Fabric Controllers` are implicitly registered after successful implicit or explicit `FLOGI`; non-`Fabric Controller` requesters are `registered Nx_Ports`. Unless providing a service associated with a `well known address`, an `Nx_Port` shall only issue `RSCNs` with event qualifier `2h` or `0h`. Events listed: failure within the affected `Nx_Port`; other important state change of the affected `Nx_Port` (e.g., defined by an `FC-4`). Content types: procedure/flow; completion/exception conditions. Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 4.2.18.4 RSCN initiative
nodes.summary: Covers `4.2.18.4 RSCN initiative` and adjacent `4.2.18.5 RSCN registration`, `4.2.18.6 Protocol`, `4.2.18.7 Request Sequence`: when an affected `Nx_Port` may initiate one `RSCN Request` for accumulated state changes, prohibition on queuing multiple `RSCN Requests`, and initiation point at transmission of the `SOF` of the first frame of the `RSCN Request Sequence`. Registration rule: only notify `Nx_Ports` registered via `State Change Registration (SCR) ELS`. Protocol anchors: `RSCN Request Sequence`, `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT Reply Sequence`. Addressing/identifiers: `S_ID`, `D_ID`, `Fabric Controller`, `FFFFFDh`. Message/frame format: `Table 34 – RSCN Payload`, shows `RSCN (61h)`, `Page Length (04h)`, `Payload Length`, `affected Port_ID pages`; field constraints include minimum `8 bytes`, maximum `1024 bytes`, multiple of `4 bytes`. Content types: procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 4.2.18.5 RSCN registration
nodes.summary: Covers `4.2.18.5 RSCN registration`: which nodes may issue `RSCN Request`s and the prerequisite registration rule. Core rule: a `Fabric Controller`, service, or `Nx_Port` shall only issue `RSCN` requests to `Nx_Ports` that have registered with the node to be notified of state changes; registration uses the `State Change Registration (SCR) ELS`. Adjacent protocol context present: `RSCN Request Sequence`, `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT Reply Sequence`; addressing identifiers `S_ID`, `D_ID`, `Fabric Controller`, `FFFFFDh`; payload identifiers `RSCN (61h)`, `Page Length (04h)`, `Payload Length`, `affected Port_ID pages`, `Affected Port_ID Pages`. Table: `Table 34 – RSCN Payload`, shows/defines the `RSCN Request Payload` layout and fields. Content types: procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling.

Field rules stated: `Payload length` shall be `20`; `Logout Parameter page` in `Words 1:N` covers a single image pair and may be per single `FC-4 TYPE` or common to all `FC-4 TYPE` codes.

nodes.title: 4.2.22 Test Process Login State (TPLS)
4.2.22.1 Description
nodes.summary: Section `4.2.22.1 Description` covers `Test Process Login State (TPLS)` ELS purpose and request/reply behavior: determining whether image pairs are established for `S_ID || Originator Process_Associator || D_ID || Responder Process_Associator`; the receiving `Nx_Port` checks each specified image and verifies `Login state` for the source `Nx_Port` at the destination `Nx_Port`. `LS_ACC Reply Sequence` confirms successful completion and reports whether an image pair is established; `LS_RJT Reply Sequence` is named in protocol. Includes content types: response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions, message/frame format. `Table 52 – TPLS Payload` defines the `TPLS Payload` layout: `TPLS (23h)`, `Obsolete (10h)`, `Payload Length`, and `Image Pair ID page`; `S_ID` designates source `Nx_Port`, `D_ID` designates destination `Nx_Port`; payload length shall be `20`.

nodes.summary: TPLS `4.2.22.2 Protocol` index: defines the protocol outline for `Test Process Login State (TPLS)` as a minimal exchange: `a) Test Process Login State Request Sequence` followed by `b) LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`. Core topic: message-level protocol steps for verifying login/image-pair state between Nx_Ports. Named anchors present: `TPLS`, `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `Test Process Login State Request Sequence`, `Reply Sequence`. Content types: procedure/flow; response/branch handling; completion/exception conditions. Related format material immediately referenced from the same protocol context: `Table 52 – TPLS Payload`, which defines/shows the `TPLS Payload` layout with fields `TPLS (23h)`, `Obsolete (10h)`, `Payload Length`, and `Image Pair ID page`. No figures.

nodes.title: 4.2.22.3 Request Sequence
nodes.summary: Core topic: `4.2.22.3 Request Sequence` for `Test Process Login State (TPLS)` request formatting and addressing within `4.2.22 Test Process Login State (TPLS)`. Content types: `message/frame format`, `procedure/flow`. Defines Request Sequence `Addressing` and `Payload` for `TPLS Request`: `S_ID` designates the source `Nx_Port` associated with the image pair; `D_ID` designates the destination `Nx_Port` associated with the image pair. `Table 52 – TPLS Payload` shows the `TPLS Payload` layout: `Word 0` contains `TPLS (23h)`, `Obsolete (10h)a`, `Payload Length`; `Words 1:N` contain `Image Pair ID page`. Exact constraints/codes: `Payload length` shall be `20`; obsolete field shall be set to `10h` for compatibility. Mentions `Image Pair ID page` format is described in `table 53`.

nodes.title: 4.2.22.4 Reply sequence
nodes.summary: Core topic: `4.2.22.4 Reply sequence` for TPLS request completion/rejection. Contains **procedure/flow**, **message/frame format**, **response/branch handling**, **completion/exception conditions**. `LS_RJT` signifies rejection of the `TPLS Request`; `LS_ACC` signifies successful completion. `Table 54 – TPLS LS_ACC Payload` shows the `LS_ACC Payload` format: Word 0 fields `(02h)`, `Obsolete (10h)` (obsolete, shall be set to `10h`), `Payload Length`; Words `1..N` carry `TPLS Response page`; `Payload length` shall be `20`. `Table 53 – TPLS image pair ID page format` defines `Originator Process_Associator Validity`, `Responder Process_Associator Validity`, `Originator Process_Associator`, `Responder Process_Associator`. `Table 55 – TPLS response page format` defines `Response Code (see table 56)` and `Image Pair State` plus the same Process_Associator validity/identifier fields.

Section 4.2.22 defines `Test Process Login State (TPLS)`: an ELS used to determine whether image pairs are established for the combination `S_ID || Originator Process_Associator || D_ID || Responder Process_Associator`, and to verify `Login state` for the source `Nx_Port` at the destination `Nx_Port`. Protocol content includes `Test Process Login State Request Sequence` and `LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`; `LS_ACC Reply Sequence` confirms completion and reports whether an image pair is established, with Payload indicating image-pair state. Request Sequence specifies addressing via `S_ID` and `D_ID`, and Payload fields. `Table 52 – TPLS Payload` defines the request payload/message format: `TPLS (23h)`, `Obsolete (10h)`, `Payload Length`, `Image Pair ID page`; `Payload length shall be 20`; `Words 1:N`. Content types: procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 4.2.23 Request Node Identification Data (RNID)
4.2.23.1 Introduction
nodes.summary: RNID ELS introduction and reply behavior for acquiring Node Identification Data. Covers `Request Node Identification Data (RNID)`, normal `Accept (ACC) ELS Sequence` with `Node Identification Data`, and reject cases via `LS_RJT ELS Sequence` using reason code `Command not supported`, or `Unable to perform command request` with reason code explanation `Unable to supply requested data`. Notes alternate accept reply containing only `Common Identification Data` (see `Table 61`), ULP retry with different `Node Identification Data Format request`, and scope limits for further discovery processing. Addressing rules include `Nx_Port`, `Fx_Port`, `Fabric Controller (FFFFFDh)`, and when replies represent the Fabric Controller node or a target `Nx_Port`. Also includes carryover context from `Table 56 – TPLS accept response code`, defining `Response code` values and `Image pair state` (`image pair established`, `image pair not established`). Content types: response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 4.2.23.2 Protocol
nodes.summary: Core topic: `4.2.23.2 Protocol` for `Request Node Identification Data (RNID)`; begins `a) Request Node-Identification (RNID) request Sequence` (`63`). Context immediately preceding protocol defines RNID behavior: normal response is `Accept (ACC) ELS Sequence` with `Node Identification Data`; unsupported `RNID ELS` returns `LS_RJT ELS Sequence` with reason code `Command not supported`; unsupported format returns `LS_RJT ELS Sequence` with reason code `Unable to perform command request` and reason code explanation `Unable to supply requested data`, or `Accept ELS Payload` containing only `Common Identification Data`. Addressing/identifiers present: `Nx_Port`, `Fx_Port`, `Fabric Controller (FFFFFDh)`. [content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions] Table `56 – TPLS accept response code`: defines `Response code` encoded values in `Word 0, Bits 11-8` (`0001b Request executed`, others `Reserved`, `0111b Obsolete`) and `Image pair state` (`image pair established` / `image pair not established`).

nodes.title: 4.2.23.3 Request Sequence
nodes.summary: RNID request sequence for Link Services, covering addressing and payload contents before `4.2.23.4 Reply Sequence`. Content types: procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling. `S_ID` designates requesting source port or Fabric Controller (`FFFFFDh`); `D_ID` designates receiving `Nx_Port` or `Fx_Port` or Fabric Controller (`FFFFFDh`). `RNID Payload` uses `RNID (78h)` with `00h 00h 00h`, plus `Node Identification Data Format`. `Table 57 – RNID Payload` defines the RNID payload word/byte layout. `Table 58 – Node Identification Data Format` defines values and meanings: `00h` Common Identification Data only, `01h – DEh` specific ULP `FC-4 TYPE`, `DFh` General Topology Discovery format, `E0h – FFh` vendor specific format. Reply branch names referenced: `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`.

nodes.title: 4.2.23.4 Reply Sequence
nodes.summary: RNID `4.2.23.4 Reply Sequence`: reply handling for `LS_ACC` / `LS_RJT` after RNID request; core content is RNID accept/reject semantics and `RNID Accept Payload` layout. `LS_RJT` signifies rejection of the RNID request; `LS_ACC` signifies acceptance and presents `Node Identification Data Format`. `Table 59 – RNID Accept Payload` shows accept payload/message format: word 0 `02h 00h 00h 00h`, fields `Common Identification Data Length (0 or 16)`, `Specific Identification Data Length`, `Common Identification Data`, `Specific Identification Data (m)`. `Table 60 – Node Identification Data Format` defines reply-format values: `00h`, `01h – DEh`, `DFh`, `E0h – FFh`. Length rules: common length `16` or `zero`; specific length multiple of `4`, min `zero`, max `252`. Content types: message/frame format, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 4.2.23.5 General Topology Discovery format:
nodes.summary: Defines the RNID `LS_ACC` payload when `Node Identification Data Format` = `DFh` (`4.2.23.5 General Topology Discovery format`): required lengths (`Common Identification-Data length` = 16, `Specific Identification-Data length` = 52), inclusion of `Common Node Identification Data` and `Specific Identification Data`, and field semantics for `Vendor Unique`, `Associated Type`, `Physical Port Number`, `Number of Attached Nodes`, `Node Management`, `IP Version`, `UDP/TCP Port Number`, `IP Address`. Includes `N_Port_Name`, `Node_Name`, `Nx_Port`, `Fx_Port`, `FL_Port`, `DFh`, `00h`-`05h`, `FFh`. `Table 61 – Common Identification Data`: layout of `N_Port_Name`/`Node_Name`. `Table 62 – General Topology Specific Identification Data`: field layout for general topology discovery. `Table 63 – Associated Type`: node/function type codes. `Table 64 – Multi-function device bit definitions`: bit meanings for multifunction devices. `Table 65 – Node Management`: protocol code values. `Table 66 – IP Version`: IP version codes and meaning. Content types: message/frame format, response/branch handling.

Covers `4.2.23 Request Node Identification Data (RNID)` introduction and start of protocol for the `RNID ELS`, used to acquire `Node Identification Data`. Normal reply is `Accept (ACC) ELS Sequence` with `Node Identification Data` payload. Unsupported `RNID ELS` returns `LS_RJT ELS Sequence` with reason code `Command not supported`. Unsupported requested format returns `LS_RJT ELS Sequence` with reason code `Unable to perform command request` and reason code explanation `Unable to supply requested data`, or `Accept ELS Payload` containing only `Common Identification Data` (see `Table 61`). Addresses/identifiers include `Nx_Port`, `Fx_Port`, `ULP`, `Fabric Controller (FFFFFDh)`. Destination handling distinguishes known `Fx_Port`, receiving `Nx_Port`, and reply representing the Fabric Controller node. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions. `Table 56 – TPLS accept response code`: defines `Response code` encoded values and `Image pair state`.

nodes.title: 4.2.24 Registered Link Incident Report (RLIR)
4.2.24.1 Description
nodes.summary: Defines `4.2.24 Registered Link Incident Report (RLIR)` purpose and terminology for link incident reporting: `incident port`, `connected port`, `connected node`, `reporting port`, `registered port`, `reporting node`. Core behavior: `RLIR ELS` lets a reporting `Nx_Port` send a `Link Incident Record` to a registered `Nx_Port`; normal response is `LS_ACC ELS Sequence` with no Payload. Response/branch handling: unsupported recipient returns `LS_RJT ELS Sequence` with reason code `Command not supported`; unsupported record type returns `LS_RJT ELS Sequence` with reason code `Unable to perform command request`. Mentions incident recognition via incident code in `table 72`; if a registered recipient exists, a `Link Incident Record` is created and reported. Exception/completion conditions: delayed reporting after `Link Failure`; some `FC-4` recovery may suppress reporting unless timeout persists beyond FC-4 specified period. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 4.2.24.2 Link Incident reporting procedure
nodes.summary: Procedure/flow for `4.2.24.2 Link Incident reporting procedure`: how a reporting node and reporting port handle creation, retention, transmission, and discard of `Link Incident Record` objects after a link incident. Core rules: the incident port’s reporting node generates one `Link Incident Record` per link incident; record capacity is vendor specific; on resource exhaustion, the oldest `Link Incident Record` is discarded and the new record retained. The selected reporting port presents records by sending `RLIR ELSs` to ports registered as valid recipients of `Link Incident Records`; after sending all required `RLIR ELSs` containing the `Link Incident Record`, the port may discard the record. Content types: procedure/flow; completion/exception conditions (resource exhaustion, post-send discard). Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 4.2.24.3 Protocol
nodes.summary: Section `4.2.24.3 Protocol` covers RLIR delivery protocol selection and the `RLIR Request Sequence` / `LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence` for sending a `RLIR ELS` from reporting port to registered port. Includes procedure/flow, response/branch handling, timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions, and message/frame format. Key rules: send first to ports registered to always receive `RLIR`; on failure, try ports registered to conditionally receive `RLIR`; `RLIR ELS` is successfully transmitted only if `LS_ACC` is received within `2xR_A_TOV`; registration may be discarded after unsuccessful delivery; vendor specific behavior if no registration exists. `S_ID` = reporting port, `D_ID` = registered port. Table 67 – RLIR Payload: defines `RLIR Payload` layout, including `RLIR (79h)`, `Common Link Incident Record Length`, `Common Link Incident-Format Descriptor Length`, `Common Link Incident Descriptor`, `IQ`, `IC`, `EPAI (domain/area of ISL)`, and `Specific Link Incident Record`.

nodes.title: 4.2.24.4 Request Sequence
nodes.summary: RLIR Request Sequence message/frame format for `RLIR` ELS payload and field semantics: addressing uses `S_ID` = reporting port, `D_ID` = registered port; payload starts `RLIR (79h)`. Covers `Link Incident Record Format`, `Common Link Incident Record Length`, `Common Link Incident Descriptor Length`, `Specific Link Incident Record Length`, `Common Link Incident Record`, `Common Link Incident Descriptor`, `Incident Qualifier (IQ)`, `Incident Code (IC)`, `Expansion Port address identifier (EPAI)`, `Transaction ID`, `Time Stamp Format`, `Time Stamp`; identifiers include `N_Port_Name`, `Node_Name`, `Port_ID`, `Fabric_Name`; values/codes include `00h`, `01h-FFh`, `4h`, `16`, `64`, `0`, `252`; states/conditions mention `Link-Failure`, `Offline State`, `OL3 State`, `LF1 State`, `R_T_TOV`. Tables: Table 67 – RLIR Payload (payload layout); Table 68 – Link Incident Record Format (format codes); Table 69 – Common Link Incident Record Data (record fields); Table 70 – Time Stamp Format values (timestamp encodings); Table 71 – Incident Qualifier / Continued (IQ bit meanings, severity/FRU/E_Port flags); Table 72 – Incident Code values (incident types). Content types: message/frame format, timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 4.2.24.5 Reply Sequence
nodes.summary: Section `4.2.24.5 Reply Sequence` covers RLIR reply handling: `LS_RJT` rejects the `RLIR Request`; `LS_ACC` accepts the `RLIR Request` and its `Link Incident Record`. Includes `Table 73 – RLIR LS_ACC Payload`, which defines the `LS_ACC Payload` message/frame format: `Bits 31 .. 24`, `23 .. 16`, `15 .. 08`, `07 .. 00`; `Word 0 = 02h 00h 00h 00h`. Contained content types: response/branch handling, message/frame format, completion/exception conditions. Page text immediately continues into `4.2.25 Link Incident Record Registration (LIRR)`: `LIRR ELS` registration/de-registration procedure/flow, normal response `LS_ACC` with no Payload, exception `LS_RJT` with reason code `Command not supported` or `Unable to perform command request`, and destination identifier `Management Server (FFFFFAh)`.

Section 4.2.24 defines the Registered Link Incident Report (`RLIR`) `ELS`: how a reporting `Nx_Port` sends a `Link Incident Record` for an incident port to a registered `Nx_Port`, with roles/terms `incident port`, `connected port`, `connected node`, `reporting port`, `registered port`, and `reporting node`. Response/branch handling: normal reply is `LS_ACC` `ELS Sequence` with no Payload; unsupported `RLIR` returns `LS_RJT` with reason code `"Command not supported"`; unacceptable record type returns `LS_RJT` with `"Unable to perform command request"`. Procedure/flow: recognize incidents when an incident code is defined in `table 72`, generate one record per incident, send `RLIR ELSs` to registered recipients, then discard after all required sends. Completion/exception conditions: vendor-specific record capacity, discard oldest record on overflow, optional delay after `Link Failure`, report only if failure persists past FC-4 timeout. Tables/Figures: `table 72` (incident codes referenced for recognition); no figures.

nodes.title: 4.2.25 Link Incident Record Registration (LIRR)
4.2.25.1 Description
nodes.summary: 4.2.25.1 Description — Link Incident Record Registration (LIRR) ELS: core topic is registering or de-registering a source Nx_Port to receive Registered Link Incident Report (RLIR) ELS notifications, including Fabric registration via Management Server (`FFFFFAh`). Refers to 4.2.24.1 for terms. Normal response to a `LIRR ELS Sequence` is `LS_ACC ELS Sequence` with no Payload. If unsupported, recipient returns `LS_RJT ELS Sequence` with reason code `Command not supported`; if unable to perform registration function or format unsupported, returns `LS_RJT ELS Sequence` with reason code `Unable to perform command request`. Contains: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions. Table 73 – RLIR LS_ACC Payload: defines `LS_ACC Payload` format for `RLIR Request`; value shown is Word 0 = `02h 00h 00h 00h`.

nodes.title: 4.2.25.2 Registration for Link Incident Records
nodes.summary: Section `4.2.25.2 Registration for Link Incident Records` covers procedure/flow and response/branch handling for `LIRR ELS` registration/de-registration of an `Nx_Port` to receive `Link Incident Records` from `ports of interest`. A `ULP` registers after determining its address identifier and the address identifiers of ports of interest, indicating the registration format; recipient returns `LS_ACC ELS` with no Payload. De-registration also uses `LIRR ELS` with indicated format. Completion/exception conditions: de-registering a port not registered returns `LS_ACC ELS` and no further action; registering a port already registered for the specified format returns `LS_ACC ELS` and no further action. Recipient maintains separate established registration lists for each registration format. Includes `Table 73 – RLIR LS_ACC Payload`, showing the `LS_ACC Payload` format with `Word 0 = 02h 00h 00h 00h` (message/frame format).

nodes.title: 4.2.25.3 Responsibilities of Valid-Registered Recipients
nodes.summary: Core topic: responsibilities of valid-registered recipients for Link Incident Records and mismatch handling. A valid-registered recipient shall receive/process Link Incident Records generated per Link Incident reporting procedure (`4.2.24.2`); ULP coordination is out of scope. If a valid registered recipient receives a Link Incident Record whose format does not match its registration format, the recipient port shall reject the `RLIR ELS` with reason code `Unable to perform command request`. Related surrounding conditions on this page: Logout between a node and a valid-registered-recipient node causes de-registration; full registration list causes rejection with `unable to perform command request`. Table present: `Table 73 – RLIR LS_ACC Payload` — defines `RLIR LS_ACC Payload`. Named anchors: `RLIR ELS`, `Link Incident Records`, `Link Incident reporting procedure`, `Unable to perform command request`, `Table 73 – RLIR LS_ACC Payload`. Content types: response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 4.2.25.4 Protocol
nodes.summary: 4.2.25.4 Protocol: defines the protocol outline for Link Incident Record registration, specifically `LIRR Request Sequence` and `LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`. Core anchors and identifiers present in the provided text: `LIRR`, `RLIR`, `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `S_ID`, `D_ID`, `LIRR Payload`, `Link Incident Record Registration Function`, `Registration Format`, `7Ah`, `02h`, reason code `Unable to perform command request`. Related behavioral conditions referenced in adjacent protocol text: add/remove registration, de-registration on `Logout`, rejection when registration list is full, rejection for format mismatch. Table present: `Table 73 – RLIR LS_ACC Payload`, which defines/shows the `RLIR LS_ACC Payload` layout. Content types: procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 4.2.25.5 Request Sequence
nodes.summary: Covers `4.2.25.5 Request Sequence` for `LIRR` registration requests for Link Incident Records: addressing and payload definition for the request message. Content types: `procedure/flow`, `message/frame format`. `Addressing`: `S_ID` = source port requesting registration for Link Incident Records; `D_ID` = port receiving the registration request. `Payload`: `LIRR Payload` format shown in `Table 74 – LIRR Payload`, including `Word 0` = `LIRR (7Ah) 00h 00h 00h`, and `Word 1` fields `Link Incident Record-Registration Function`, `Reserved`, `Registration Format`. Field rule present: `Registration Function` specifies mode of registration, i.e., whether the recipient node adds or removes the sending `Nx_Port` to or from its list of registered recipients. No figures. No explicit response/branch handling, state machine, timer/negotiation, or completion/exception conditions in this subsection.

nodes.title: 4.2.25.6 Reply Sequence
nodes.summary: Section `4.2.25.6 Reply Sequence` covers response/branch handling for `LIRR Request`: `LS_RJT` signifies rejection of the `LIRR Request`. Nearby context on the same page defines request parameters used by this exchange: field `Registration Function` in `Table 75 – Registration Function` (defines registration actions/codes `00h Reserved`, `01h Set registration – conditionally receive`, `02h Set registration – always receive`, `FFh Clear registration`, `03h - FEh Reserved`) and `Link Incident Record-Registration Format` / `Link Incident Record-Registration Type` in `Table 76 – Link Incident Record-Registration Format` (defines format codes `00h Common Format`, `01h - FFh Specific-coded value`). Content types present: response/branch handling, message/frame format, completion/exception conditions.

Section `4.2.25 Link Incident Record Registration (LIRR)` covers `LIRR ELS` registration/de-registration of an `Nx_Port` to receive `Registered Link Incident Report (RLIR) ELS`, including Fabric registration to `Management Server (FFFFFAh)`. Describes `4.2.25.1 Description` and `4.2.25.2 Registration for Link Incident Records`: when to send `LIRR ELS`, use of ports of interest, required determination of address identifier(s), and per-format recipient registration lists. Response/branch handling: normal response is `LS_ACC ELS Sequence` with no Payload; unsupported `LIRR ELS` returns `LS_RJT ELS Sequence` with reason code `Command not supported`; failures or unsupported format return `Unable to perform command request`; duplicate register/de-register and unregistered de-registration both accept with `LS_ACC` and no further action. Table `73 – RLIR LS_ACC Payload` shows the `LS_ACC Payload` format/value `02h 00h 00h 00h`. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions, message/frame format.

nodes.title: 4.2.27.3 Request Sequence
nodes.summary: Core topic: `4.2.27.3 Request Sequence` for `FACT Request Sequence` (Fabric Activate Alias_ID), defining addressing and request payload layout for activating an `Alias_ID` for listed `Nx_Ports`. nodes.title: 4.2.32.3 Request Sequence
nodes.summary: Covers `FDISC` request behavior and its immediate branching into reply handling context for additional Fabric login. Core rules: `Payload` in `FDISC` and corresponding `LS_ACC` matches `FLOGI`/`LS_ACC` except `ELS_Command`; behavior depends on `S_ID` and `N_Port_ID`. If `S_ID` = zero, `FDISC` transfers `N_Port Name` and `node name` to request assignment/login of an additional `N_Port_ID`; sender must already have one `N_Port_ID` logged in, and `Service Parameters` shall not be modified. If `S_ID` is a previously-assigned `N_Port_ID`, `FDISC` carries matching `N_Port_Name` and `node name`; `Service Parameters` are ignored and no error reported. Response/branch handling and completion/exception conditions include `D_ID`, `F_RJT`, reason code `Login required`, class-specific handling for `Class 1`, `Class 2`, `Class 3`. No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 4.2.32.4 Reply Sequence
nodes.summary: FDISC `Reply Sequence` response/branch handling and completion/exception conditions: defines `LS_ACC`/`F_RJT` behavior based on `S_ID` in the `FDISC ELS`. If `S_ID` is zero, `D_ID` in `LS_ACC` is the additional `N_Port_ID` being assigned, and the Fabric considers `Fabric Login` complete for that additional `N_Port_ID`. If `S_ID` is not zero and is currently logged in, `D_ID` of `LS_ACC` is set to the `S_ID` of the `FDISC ELS`. Error branch: if `S_ID` = zero and no `N_Port ID` is logged in, or `S_ID` is a non-zero `N_Port_ID` not currently logged, return `F_RJT` with reason code `Login required` to the `S_ID` for `Class 1`/`Class 2`; discard for `Class 3`. No tables. No figures.

nodes.summary: Core topic: `4.2.33.2 Protocol` for `Discover Address (ADISC)`; defines the exchange structure for communicating `Nx_Ports`. Content is minimal and procedural: step `a)` identifies the `ADISC Sequence`; step `b)` identifies the reply path as `LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`. Use this section when a question asks which command/reply sequences govern `ADISC`, or whether the response branch is acceptance (`LS_ACC`) versus rejection (`LS_RJT`). Content types present: procedure/flow, response/branch handling. Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 4.2.33.3 Request Sequence
nodes.summary: Defines the ADISC request message/frame format and request sequence contents for `ADISC Request`; contains `message/frame format` (no figures, no state machine, no timer/negotiation). `Table 89 – ADISC Request payload` shows the `ADISC Request payload` word/bit layout and originator-identification fields. Exact anchors: `ADISC (52h)`, `Hard Address of Originator`, `N_Port_Name of Originator`, `Node_Name of Originator`, `N_Port_ID of Originator`, `Reserved`, `L_Port_ID`, `Domain address`, `Area address`, `AL_PA`, `LIHA sequence`, `FC-AL-2`, `S_ID`, `ADISC Request frame`. Specifies `Hard Address of Originator` as a 24-bit `L_Port_ID`; if no hard address or no `FC-AL-2` capability, report zeroes. `N_Port_ID of Originator` is the 24-bit `S_ID` from the `ADISC Request frame` header.

nodes.title: 4.2.34 Third Party Process Logout (TPRLO)
4.2.34.1 Description
nodes.summary: Section `4.2.34.1 Description` defines `Third Party Process Logout (TPRLO)`: the `TPRLO ELS` invalidates operating environments at the recipient `Nx_Port` for the specified `TYPE`, removing image pairs and associated resources, and has the same effect as if all `Nx_Ports` that performed `Process Login` for that `TYPE` had performed `PRLO` with the recipient `Nx_Port`. Nearby protocol/context anchors included on this page: `4.2.34.2 Protocol` (`TPRLO Request Sequence`, `LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`) and `4.2.34.3 Request Sequence` with `S_ID` and `D_ID` field roles for initiating and destination `Nx_Port`. `Table 91 – Response summary to FDISC/PDISC`: response matrix for `FDISC`, `PDISC`, `ADISC` by `Logged in`/`Not Logged in`, `Responding Nx_Port`, `Responding F_Port Controller`, and `LS_ACC`/`LS_RJT`/`F_RJT`/`Discard` outcomes. Content types: response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 4.2.34.2 Protocol
nodes.summary: TPRLO protocol overview. Section `4.2.34.2 Protocol` defines the `Third Party Process Logout (TPRLO)` exchange at a high level: `a) TPRLO Request Sequence` and `b) LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`. Core topic is the request/reply protocol for `TPRLO`, with adjacent context that `S_ID` designates the initiating `Nx_Port` and `D_ID` designates the destination `Nx_Port`. Includes nearby response handling reference from `Table 91 – Response summary to FDISC/PDISC`, which shows outcomes for `FDISC`, `PDISC`, `ADISC` by `Responding Port Status` (`Logged in`, `Not Logged in`) and responder type/class (`Responding Nx_Port`, `Responding F_Port Controller`, `Class 1, 2, or 3`, `Class 1, or 2`, `Class 3`), including `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `F_RJT`, `LOGO`. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling.

nodes.title: 4.2.34.3 Request Sequence
nodes.summary: Section `4.2.34.3 Request Sequence` covers the `TPRLO` ELS request sequence: `S_ID` designates the Nx_Port initiating `TPRLO`, `D_ID` designates the destination Nx_Port. Includes [procedure/flow], [message/frame format], [response/branch handling], [completion/exception conditions]. `Table 91 – Response summary to FDISC/PDISC` shows responses for `FDISC`, `PDISC`, `ADISC` by responding port status (`Logged in`, `Not Logged in`) and responder type/class, including `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `F_RJT`, `LOGO`, `Discard`, reason codes `"Unable to perform command request"`, `"N_Port Login required"`, `"Command not supported"`, `"Request not supported"`, `"Login required"`. `Table 92 – TPRLO Payload` defines payload words/fields: `TPRLO (24h)`, `Obsolete (10h)`, `Payload Length` (shall be `20`), `Logout Parameter page`. `Table 93 – TPRLO logout parameter page` defines `TYPE Code or Common Service Parameters`, `TYPE Code Extension`, `Global Process Logout`, obsolete validity/associator/N_Port_ID fields, and reserved bits.

Core topic: `4.2.34 Third Party Process Logout (TPRLO)` defines the `TPRLO` ELS purpose and basic protocol for invalidating operating environments at a recipient `Nx_Port` for a specified `TYPE`, equivalent to prior `Process Login` participants issuing `PRLO`. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions. Protocol anchors: `TPRLO Request Sequence`; `LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`; Request Sequence addressing with `S_ID` = initiating `Nx_Port`, `D_ID` = destination `Nx_Port`. nodes.title: 4.2.35 Clock Synchronization Request (CSR)
4.2.35.1 Description
nodes.summary: Section `4.2.35.1 Description` defines the purpose of the `Clock Synchronization Request (CSR)` ELS. Core topic: `CSR` is used to request the `Clock Synchronization Server` to either send or quit sending periodic `Clock Synchronization Update (CSU)` ELS frames or `Clock Synchronization primitives`, depending on the method implemented (`FC-FS-2`). Contains: message/frame purpose, timer/negotiation context via periodic synchronization behavior. Nearby named protocol anchors introduced with this section family: `4.2.35.2 Protocol`, `Clock Synchronization Request Sequence`, `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`. Also present on the page is `Table 94 – TPRLO LS_ACC Payload`, which shows the `TPRLO LS_ACC Payload` format, including `Payload Length`, `Logout Parameter page`, `02h`, obsolete `10h`, and value `20` (contextual adjacent table).

nodes.title: 4.2.35.2 Protocol
nodes.summary: Section `4.2.35.2 Protocol` covers `Clock Synchronization Request (CSR)` protocol handling. Content is minimal and contains a `procedure/flow` plus `response/branch handling`: `a) Clock Synchronization Request Sequence`; `b) LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`. This section indicates the exchange pattern for the `CSR ELS`, specifically request sequencing and acceptance/reject reply sequencing, but provides no frame fields, timers, states, or exceptions beyond the named reply alternatives `LS_ACC` and `LS_RJT`. Nearby carried-over reference on the same page: `Table 94 – TPRLO LS_ACC Payload` — shows the `TPRLO LS_ACC Payload` format/definition, including `02h`, obsolete `10h`, `Payload Length`, and `Logout Parameter page`; not a CSR table. No figures.

nodes.title: 4.2.35.3 Request Sequence
nodes.summary: Section `4.2.35.3 Request Sequence` defines the `CSR` request for Clock Synchronization updates: addressing uses `S_ID` as requesting source `Nx_Port`, and `D_ID` as `Clock Synchronization Server (FFFFF6h)` or `Fabric Controller (FFFFFDh)`. Contains `procedure/flow`, `message/frame format`, and `response/branch handling`. Payload is `Table 95 – CSR Payload`, showing `Word 0` `CSR (68h)` and fields `Clock Sync Mode`, `CS_Accuracy`, `CS_Implemented_M`, `CS_Implemented_L`, `CS_Update_Period`. `Table 96 – CSR Clock Sync Mode Meaning` defines `Clock Sync Mode` values `00h`, `01h`, `02h–FEh`, `FFh`, with different meanings when sent to the `Clock Sync Server` versus the `Fabric Controller` (enable, return requested/unspecified Quality of Service parameters, disable, reserved). Also defines `CS_Accuracy_Mantissa` and `CS_Accuracy_Exponent` bit fields and the `T_reference ± ...` accuracy expression.

nodes.title: 4.2.35.4 Reply Sequence
nodes.summary: Core topic: `4.2.35.4 Reply Sequence` for Clock Synchronization Request/Reply handling of `CSR`. Response/branch handling: `LS_RJT` = rejection of the `CSR` command; `LS_ACC` = server agrees to perform the action requested in the `CSR Payload`. Message/frame format: `LS_ACC Payload` includes `Clock Sync Mode`, `CS_Accuracy`, `CS_Implemented_MSB`, `CS_Implemented_LSB`, `CS_Update_Period`; `Clock Sync Mode` in the `CSR LS_ACC Payload` shall contain the value received in the `CSR Payload`. `Table 97 – CSR LS_ACC Payload`: shows `LS_ACC` payload word/bit layout, including `Word 0 = 02h 00h 00h 00h`. `Table 98 – CSU Clock Sync Mode Meaning`: defines `00h` = enabled, `01h - FEh` = reserved, `FFh` = disabled. Also defines `CS_Accuracy_Mantissa`, `CS_Accuracy_Exponent`, `T_reference`, and semantic meanings of implemented MSB/LSB and update period.

Section `4.2.35 Clock Synchronization Request (CSR)` covers the `CSR ELS` used to request the `Clock Synchronization Server` to either send or quit sending periodic `Clock Synchronization Update (CSU) ELS frames` or `Clock Synchronization primitives`, depending on the implemented method (`see FC-FS-2`). nodes.title: 4.2.36 Clock Synchronization Update (CSU)
4.2.36.1 Description
nodes.summary: Clock Synchronization Update (CSU) ELS description: used by the Clock Synchronization Server to send its current clock value to clients (see FC-FS-2). Contains message/frame format and response/branch handling. Protocol notes: Clock Synchronization Update Request Sequence; No Reply Sequence; Request Sequence. Header/identifier details called out here: Information Category (Header word 0, bits 24-27) = Solicited Data (0001b); S_ID = Clock Synchronization Server well-known address (FFFFF6h); D_ID = Nx_Port(s). Payload includes `CSU (69h)`, `Reserved`, and `Clock Count` (8 bytes). Table 99 – CSU Payload: shows payload word/bit layout. Table 100 – Clock Count Field Meaning: defines byte 0 (MSB) through byte 7 (LSB) of `Clock Count`. Also notes Bit 7 in Word 2 = 1/106,25MHz.

Also notes `Bit 7 in Word 2` equals `1/106,25MHz`, roughly `9,4 98`.

nodes.title: 4.2.37 Report Port Buffer Conditions (RPBC)
4.2.37.1 Description
nodes.summary: Describes `Report Port Buffer Conditions (RPBC)` ELS purpose: a Port reports its buffer conditions. Normal response/branch handling: `LS_ACC` ELS Sequence; if unsupported, `LS_RJT` ELS Sequence with reason code `Command not supported`. Protocol anchors present: `RPBC` Request Sequence, `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `S_ID`, `D_ID`, `RPBC Payload`, `ELS Buffer Parameters`, `Originator S_ID - obsolete`, `RPBC (58h)`. Also notes addressing roles for `S_ID` and `D_ID`. Contains: message/frame format, response/branch handling.

nodes.title: 4.2.37.2 Protocol
nodes.summary: Section 4.2.37.2 Protocol defines the RPBC ELS exchange structure for `Report Port Buffer Conditions (RPBC)`: `a) Report Port Buffer Conditions (RPBC) Request Sequence` and `b) LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`. Core topic is the protocol-level request/reply pattern for `RPBC`, including normal and reject responses. Related payload context immediately tied to this protocol names `Table 101 – RPBC Payload`, which shows the `RPBC Payload` message/frame format: `Word 0` contains `RPBC (58h)` and `Reserved`; `Word 1` contains `ELS Buffer Parameters`; `Word 2` contains `Reserved` and `Originator S_ID - obsolete`. nodes.title: 4.2.37.3 Request Sequence
nodes.summary: Section 4.2.37.3 Request Sequence defines the `Report Port Buffer Conditions (RPBC)` request message/frame format and addressing for `RPBC`. Core topic: how `S_ID` and `D_ID` are used in the `RPBC` Request Sequence, and how the `RPBC Payload` is structured. Payload format: `word 0` contains `RPBC (58h)` and `Reserved`; `word 1` contains `ELS Buffer Parameters`; `word 2` contains `Reserved` and `Originator S_ID - obsolete`. It states that the `ELS Buffer Parameter` field in `word 1` relates to the sender’s ELS buffer conditions, with contents referenced in `table 102`. Table present: `Table 101 – RPBC Payload` — defines the RPBC payload word/bit layout. Content types: message/frame format, procedure/flow.

nodes.title: 4.2.37.4 Reply Sequence
nodes.summary:
Core topic: `4.2.37.4 Reply Sequence` for `RPBC`, defining reply outcomes `LS_RJT` and `LS_ACC` and the `RPBC LS_ACC Payload`. Content types: message/frame format; response/branch handling; completion/exception conditions.

`LS_RJT` signifies rejection of the `RPBC` command. `LS_ACC` signifies acceptance of the `RPBC Request` and its `Accept Payload`; payload format is in `Table 103 – RPBC LS_ACC Payload` (shows `Word 0 = 02h Reserved`, `Word 1 = ELS Buffer Parameters`). `ELS Buffer Parameters` in `RPBC LA_ACC Payload` word 1 relates to sender ELS buffer conditions and points to `Table 102 – ELS Buffer Parameters Field` (defines `Multi-frame ELS sequence supported` and `ELS Receive Data Field Size`). Includes identifier/code `02h`.

RPBC section defining how `Report Port Buffer Conditions (RPBC)` reports a Port’s buffer conditions. Covers `4.2.37.1 Description`, `4.2.37.2 Protocol`, and `4.2.37.3 Request Sequence`. Normal response is `LS_ACC`; unsupported recipient returns `LS_RJT` with reason code `Command not supported`. nodes.title: 4.2.38 Report node FC-4 Types (RNFT)
4.2.38.1 Description
nodes.summary: Core topic: `4.2.38.1 Description` defines `Report node FC-4 Types (RNFT)` use and semantics: the `RNFT ELS` exchanges supported `FC-4` protocol lists after `N_Port Login`, letting an `Nx_Port` verify whether a remote `Nx_Port` supports a given `FC-4 protocol`. `RNFT` requests the responder’s supported `FC-4` list; `RNFT LS_ACC` returns that list to the source of the `RNFT`. Response/branch handling: a remote `Nx_Port` returning `LS_RJT` with reason code `Command not supported` may be treated by a specific `FC-4` as not supporting that `FC-4`. Related message/frame format tables present on this page: `Table 102 – ELS Buffer Parameters Field` defines `Multi-frame ELS sequence supported` and `ELS Receive Data Field Size`; `Table 103 – RPBC LS_ACC Payload` shows the `RPBC LS_ACC Payload`, including `02h` and `ELS Buffer Parameters`.

nodes.title: 4.2.38.2 Protocol
nodes.summary: Section `4.2.38.2 Protocol` covers the RNFT protocol: `Report node FC-4 Types (RNFT) Request sequence` followed by `LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`. Contains `procedure/flow`, `message/frame format`, `response/branch handling`, and `completion/exception conditions`. Request-side details reference `4.2.38.3 Request Sequence`: addressing via `S_ID` and `D_ID`; request payload in `Table 104 – RNFT Payload`, with `RNFT (63h)`, `Maximum Size`, and `Index`. `Maximum Size` is a 16-bit value in `Bytes 2-3 of Word 0`; zero means the `RNFT LS_ACC` may be any size. `Index` in `Byte 3 of Word 1` selects the first `FC-4 Entry`. Reply-side details reference `4.2.38.4 Reply Sequence`: `LS_RJT` rejects `RNFT`; `LS_ACC` returns data per `Table 105 – RNFT LS_ACC Payload`, showing `02h`, `Payload Length (M)`, `List Length`, `Index`, and `FC-4 Entry` list structure.

nodes.title: 4.2.38.3 Request Sequence
nodes.summary: RNFT Request Sequence for Report node FC-4 Types (RNFT): defines addressing and request Payload for an RNFT request, plus the immediate reply paths `LS_ACC` or `LS_RJT`. `S_ID` identifies the source `Nx_Port`; `D_ID` identifies the destination `Nx_Port`. `Table 104 – RNFT Payload` shows the request message/frame format: `Word 0` = `RNFT (63h)`, `Reserved`, `Maximum Size`; `Word 1` = `Reserved`, `Index`. `Maximum Size` is the 16-bit value in Bytes 2-3 of `Word 0`; `Index` is Byte 3 of `Word 1`, selecting the first `FC-4 Entry` to return. `Table 105 – RNFT LS_ACC Payload` shows the `LS_ACC` reply format with `02h`, `Payload Length (M)`, `List Length`, `Index`, and `FC-4 Entry N`. Content types: message/frame format, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 4.2.38.4 Reply Sequence
nodes.summary: Section `4.2.38.4 Reply Sequence` covers `RNFT` reply handling for the Report node FC-4 Types request: `LS_RJT` rejects the `RNFT` command, and `LS_ACC` signifies the destination `Nx_Port` has transmitted the requested data. Content types: `response/branch handling`, `message/frame format`, `completion/exception conditions`. Exact anchors: `LS_RJT`, `LS_ACC`, `RNFT`, `Nx_Port`, `table 105`, `RNFT LS_ACC Payload`, `02h`, `Payload Length (M)`, `List Length`, `Index`, `FC-4 Entry 1`, `FC-4 Entry N`, `Word 0`, `Word 1`, `N+1`. `Table 105 – RNFT LS_ACC Payload` defines the reply payload layout, including header fields and the indexed `FC-4 Entry` list returned in the `LS_ACC`.

Section `4.2.38 Report node FC-4 Types (RNFT)` covers the `RNFT` ELS purpose and use: exchange of supported `FC-4` protocol lists after `N_Port Login`, request/response to verify whether a remote `Nx_Port` supports a given `FC-4` protocol. `RNFT LS_ACC` returns the requested supported protocol list; `LS_RJT` with reason code `Command not supported` may be interpreted by an FC-4 as indicating that FC-4 is not supported. Anchors present: `RNFT`, `RNFT LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `N_Port Login`, `Nx_Port`, `FC-4`, `Command not supported`.
