structure.summary: Covers Extended Link Services (ELS) basics and request rules: an ELS request solicits a destination `Nx_Port` function; an ELS reply is required unless otherwise specified. Defines single-`Sequence` request/reply structure, `ELS_Command` code in the first word/first byte of `Payload`, one-`Exchange` request/reply pairing, `Last Sequence bit` termination on `LS_ACC`, and normal `Exchange`/`Sequence` management per `FC-FS-2`. Specifies frame fields: `TYPE` = `01h`, `R_CTL` values, `Payload`, `D_ID`, `S_ID`, `LFA`, valid addresses, well known addresses, and `FLOGI` before all ELSs in a Fabric. `Table 2 – Extended Link Services Routing Bits and Information Categories` defines `R_CTL` routing/info categories: `0001b` Solicited Data, `0010b` Request, `0011b` Reply, Others Reserved; note `CSU`. Content types: message/frame format, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions, procedure/flow.

structure.title: 5 FC-4 Link Service
structure.summary: FC-4 Link Service rules for FC-4-specific request/reply exchanges supporting an individual FC-4 Device_Data protocol. Request uses Information Category `Unsolicited Control`; reply uses `Solicited Control`. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions, message/frame format. Protocol runs in a single Exchange as a two Sequence Exchange: request Sequence by Originator (`Nx_Port`), Sequence Initiative transfer, reply Sequence from Responder (`Nx_Port` or `Fx_Port`); each request/reply is a single Sequence, each Sequence one or more frames. Applies after `N_Port Login`; uses Discard multiple Sequences Exchange Error Policy; timeout is `twice the value of R_A_TOV`; on error abort with `ABTS` and retry with a different Exchange; if `ACK` has Abort Sequence bits `(5-4)` = `01b`, retry once after `Basic Accept`. `R_CTL bits 31-28 (Word 0)` = `0011b`; `TYPE` matches FC-4 Device_Data `TYPE`. No tables. No figures.

structure.title: 6 Login and Service Parameters

nodes.title: 6.1 Introduction
nodes.summary: Core topic: overview of the Login procedure for establishing an `Nx_Port` operating environment with a `Fabric`, if present, and other destination `Nx_Ports`; distinguishes `Fabric Login` and `N_Port Login`, long-lived login scope, concurrency limits, and lack of one-to-one relationship with `Class 1 dedicated connections`. Covers `explicit` versus `implicit` Login and states both provide the same functionality. Exact anchors: `FLOGI`, `PLOGI`, `ELS`, `4.2.7`, `Exchange`, `Service Parameters`, `Payload`, `LS_ACC`, `Responder`, `FLOGI Link Service request and reply`, `N_Port_ID`, `PLOGI Sequence`, `Address Identifiers`, `name server function`, `D_IDs`, `S_IDs`. Also lists how an `Nx_Port` determines its native `N_Port_ID` and how `Nx_Ports` collect `Address Identifiers`. Content types: procedure/flow; response/branch handling; timer/negotiation. Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 6.2 Fabric Login
6.2.1 Introduction
nodes.summary: Fabric Login (`FLOGI`) introduction for all `Nx_Ports`: Fabric communication shall not begin until Fabric Login completes. Core functions listed: detect Fabric presence/absence; exchange operating characteristics plus `F_Port_Name`, `Fabric_Name`, `N_Port_Name`, `Node_Name`; optionally assign/confirm `N_Port_ID`; initialize buffer-to-buffer Credit; enable Authentication (`FC-SP`) and Virtual Fabrics negotiation (`8.2`). Contains procedure/flow and timer/negotiation context. Leads into explicit Fabric Login: transmit `FLOGI` ELS (`4.2.7`) in a new Exchange, carrying Service Parameters, 64-bit `N_Port_Name`, 64-bit `Node_Name`; Service Parameters reference `6.6`, `table 150`, `table 155`. Required identifiers/fields/values: `OX_ID`, `D_ID = FFFFFEh`, `S_ID = 000000h` or `0000h || YY`, `N_Port_ID`, bits `23 to 8`, bits `7 to 0`, `AL_PA`, `FC-AL-2`. No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 6.2.2 Explicit Fabric Login
6.2.2.1 Introduction
nodes.summary: Core topic: `6.2.2.1 Introduction` to `Explicit Fabric Login` in `6.2 Fabric Login`: an `Nx_Port` must perform Fabric Login before communicating, and explicit login requires transmitting a `Fabric Login (FLOGI)` Link Service `ELS` (`see 4.2.7`). Captures what Fabric Login establishes: Fabric presence/absence, operating characteristics, `F_Port_Name`, `Fabric_Name`, `N_Port_Name`, `Node_Name`, optional assign/confirm of `N_Port_ID`, buffer-to-buffer Credit initialization, enablement of Authentication (`see FC-SP`), and Virtual Fabrics negotiation (`see 8.2`). States that explicit Fabric Login replaces previous `Service Parameters` and must follow `Exchange and Sequence management rules`, `buffer-to-buffer flow control rules`, and `end-to-end flow control rules` in `FC-FS-2`. Content types: procedure/flow, timer/negotiation. Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 6.2.2.2 Explicit Fabric Login Request
nodes.summary: Explicit Fabric Login Request for `FLOGI` ELS: procedure/flow and message/frame format for how an `Nx_Port` issues Fabric Login in a new Exchange. Payload contents: `Service Parameters`, 64-bit `N_Port_Name`, 64-bit `Node_Name`. `Service Parameters` are as specified for `F_Port Login` in `6.6`; applicability references `tables 150 and 155`. Required identifiers/fields: assign `OX_ID`; set `D_ID` to well-known `F_Port` address `FFFFFEh`; set `S_ID` according to identification state. If unidentified, `S_ID` shall be `000000h` or `0000h || YY`: `000000h` requests Fabric assignment of all 24 bits of `N_Port_ID`; `0000h || YY` requests assignment of bits 23 to 8 and validation of bits 7 to 0. `AL_PA` is the lower 8 bits. No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 6.2.2.3 Responses to Explicit Fabric Login
nodes.summary: Covers response/branch handling and completion/exception conditions for an Nx_Port transmitting `FLOGI` in explicit Fabric Login. Enumerates possible replies: `LS_ACC`, `F_BSY`, `P_BSY`, `F_RJT`, `P_RJT`, with `OX_ID` matching the `FLOGI`; distinguishes `N_Port/F_Port bit` set to one (`Fx_Port`) versus `Nx_Port/F_Port bit` set to zero (`Nx_Port`). Captures required `D_ID`/`S_ID` relationships and identifier cases `000000h`, `0000h || YY`, `XXXXXX`, `XXXXYY`, assigned `N_Port_ID`, `F_Port_Name`, `Fabric_Name`, `N_Port_Name`, `Node_Name`. Includes branch actions: continue operation, proceed to `N_Port Login`/`PLOGI`, wait, retry later, or choose different `S_ID`/class based on reason codes `Class not supported` and `Invalid S_ID`. References `6.6`, tables `150` and `155` for Service Parameters applicability. No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 6.2.2.4 Nx_Port response to FLOGI
nodes.summary: Covers Nx_Port behavior on receiving `FLOGI` in `6.2.2.4 Nx_Port response to FLOGI`: mandatory `LS_ACC` reply Sequence with `OX_ID` equal to received `FLOGI`, Common Service Parameter `Nx_Port/F_Port bit` set to zero, `D_ID` equal to received `S_ID`, Payload containing received Service Parameters with all classes marked invalid plus `64-bit N_Port_Name` and `64-bit Node_Name` of the connected `Nx_Port`. Branch handling by `N_Port_Name` comparison: if received `N_Port_Name` is less than its `N_Port_Name`, proceed to `N_Port Login`; if greater, wait for `PLOGI` from attached `N_Port`. Content types: procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions. Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 6.2.2.5 Relogin with the Fabric
nodes.summary: Core topic: `6.2.2.5 Relogin with the Fabric` rules for whether an `Nx_Port` may keep existing communications after a new Fabric Login, based on whether `N_Port_ID`, `F_Port_Name`, and `Fabric_Name` match the previous login. If all three are unchanged, the `Nx_Port` may continue communications with other `Nx_Ports` with established logins; if `N_Port_ID`, `F_Port_Name`, and/or `Fabric_Name` changed, it shall implicitly logout with all `Nx_Ports`, wait an `R_A_TOV` timeout period starting when the change is detected, then require new `N_Port Logins` before initiating or accepting communication. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions.

Core topic: explicit Fabric Login request requirements for an `Nx_Port` initiating `FLOGI` to a Fabric. Contains `procedure/flow` and `message/frame format`. `6.2.2.1 Introduction`: explicit Fabric Login requires transmission of a `Fabric Login (FLOGI)` Link Service `ELS` (see `4.2.7`); explicit Fabric Login replaces previous Service Parameters and follows Exchange/Sequence management, buffer-to-buffer flow control, and end-to-end flow control rules in `FC-FS-2`. `6.2.2.2 Explicit Fabric Login Request`: send `FLOGI` in a new Exchange; payload contains Service Parameters, `64-bit N_Port_Name`, and `64-bit Node_Name`; Service Parameters as for `F_Port Login` in `6.6`, with applicability in `tables 150 and 155`. Set `OX_ID`, `D_ID = FFFFFEh`, and `S_ID = 000000h` or `0000h || YY`; `000000h` requests all 24 bits of `N_Port_ID`, `0000h || YY` requests upper 16 bits and validates lower 8 bits (`AL_PA`).

nodes.title: 6.2.3 SOFs
nodes.summary: Section `6.2.3 SOFs` covers SOF/class selection rules for `Fabric Login`/`Fabric Relogin` using `FLOGI`, including retry behavior when the Fabric does not support the initial class. `Fabric Login` shall only use `Class 1, 2, or 3`; `SOFc1` may be used to attempt `Fabric Login` or `Fabric Relogin`; `SOFi1` is not allowed; `Class 6` shall not be used because the `D_ID` field requires an `Alias_ID` multicast address. SOF selection is based on classes supported by the originating `Nx_Port`; the `FLOGI Sequence` follows `6.2.2.3`. If an `F_RJT` with reason code `"Class of service not supported by entity at FFFFFEh"` is received, another supported SOF shall be attempted until login completes or all supported SOF choices are exhausted. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 6.2.4 Frequency
nodes.summary: Section `6.2.4 Frequency` covers Fabric Login frequency/lifetime guidance: Login between an `Nx_Port` and the `Fabric` should be long-lived; after `Implicit Logout`, a new Login with the `Fabric` is required (see `6.4.4`). Named anchors present: `6.2.4 Frequency`, `Nx_Port`, `Fabric`, `Implicit Logout`, `Login`, `6.4.4`. Content types: `procedure/flow` (re-Login requirement after logout), `completion/exception conditions` (logout triggers mandatory new Login). Nearby continuation on same page also names `FLOGI`, `LS_ACC`, `ACK`, `EOFt`, `EOFdt`, `Class 1`, `Class 2`, `Class 3`, `buffer-to-buffer Credit`, `Fabric Login completion - Originator`, `Fabric Login completion - Responder`, but these belong to adjacent completion sections, not the frequency rule itself. Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 6.2.5 Fabric Login completion - Originator
nodes.summary: Core topic: `6.2.5 Fabric Login completion - Originator` defines when the Originator considers `Fabric Login` complete for a `FLOGI` request and what is initialized at completion. Content types: `procedure/flow`, `response/branch handling`, `completion/exception conditions`. Completion conditions branch by service class: in `Class 1`, completion occurs when the Originator has transmitted the `ACK (EOFt or EOFdt)` to the `LS_ACC`; in `Class 2`, when it has transmitted the `ACK (EOFt)` to the `LS_ACC`; in `Class 3`, when it has received the `LS_ACC`. After Login is ended, `buffer-to-buffer Credit` values are initialized. Nearby context on the same page also names `6.2.6 Fabric Login completion - Responder` and transition into `6.3 N_Port Login`. No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 6.2.6 Fabric Login completion - Responder
nodes.summary: Section 6.2.6 defines Fabric Login completion for the Responder in the FLOGI request: completion/exception conditions and response/branch handling by class. Fabric Login is considered ended when, in Class 1, the Responder has received the ACK (`EOFt` or `EOFdt`) to the `LS_ACC`; in Class 2, the Responder has received the ACK (`EOFt`) to the `LS_ACC`; in Class 3, the Responder has transmitted the `LS_ACC`. On successful completion, buffer-to-buffer Credit values are initialized. Context anchors immediately tied to this completion rule: `Fabric Login`, `Responder`, `Originator`, `FLOGI request`, `LS_ACC`, `ACK`, `EOFt`, `EOFdt`, `Class 1`, `Class 2`, `Class 3`, `buffer-to-buffer Credit`. No tables. No figures.

Fabric Login (`FLOGI`) requirements for all `Nx_Ports` before any communication with other `Nx_Ports`; covers Fabric presence detection, exchange of operating characteristics (`F_Port_Name`, `Fabric_Name`, `N_Port_Name`, `Node_Name`), optional/confirmed `N_Port_ID` assignment, buffer-to-buffer Credit initialization, and enabling Authentication (`FC-SP`) and Virtual Fabrics negotiation (`8.2`). Explicit procedure/flow: `Nx_Port` sends `Fabric Login (FLOGI) Link Service ELS` in a new Exchange per `4.2.7`, replacing previous Service Parameters and following Exchange/Sequence and flow-control rules in `FC-FS-2`. Message/frame format anchors: Payload contains Service Parameters, 64-bit `N_Port_Name`, 64-bit `Node_Name`; `OX_ID`; `D_ID`=`FFFFFEh`; `S_ID`=`000000h` or `0000h || YY`; bits `23 to 8`, bits `7 to 0`, `AL_PA`; references `6.6`, tables `150` and `155`. Content types: procedure/flow, message/frame format, timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 6.3 N_Port Login
6.3.1 Introduction
nodes.summary: Core topic: `6.3.1 Introduction` to `N_Port Login` after `Fabric Login`; selects behavior per `6.3.2.2` when a `Fabric` is present and `6.3.2.4` when not present. Functions: exchange operating characteristics, `N_Port_Name`, `Node_Name`; assign native `N_Port_ID` if no Fabric; initialize end-to-end Credit and, in point-to-point or same-loop `NL_Ports`, buffer-to-buffer Credit. Completion when each `Nx_Port` has received the other’s `Service Parameters`, via implicit or explicit login. Requires login to each destination, including reserved and well-known address identifiers. Anchors: `PLOGI`, `ADISC`, `PDISC`, `ACC`, `LOGO`, `P_RJT`, `R_A_TOV`, `Address_Identifier`, `Port_Name`, `N_Port Common Service Parameters`, `6.6.2`, `table 150`, `N_Port Class Service Parameters`, `6.6.5`, `table 155`, `6.4`. [procedure/flow] [response/branch handling] [timer/negotiation] [completion/exception conditions]

nodes.title: 6.3.2 Explicit N_Port Login
6.3.2.1 Introduction
nodes.summary: Explicit N_Port Login introduction: explicit N_Port Login requires an Nx_Port to transmit a `PLOGI` Link request `Sequence`; this replaces previous Service Parameters and follows FC-FS-2 Exchange and Sequence management, buffer-to-buffer flow control, and end-to-end flow control rules. A well-behaved `Nx_Port` should `Logout` before new login; if a `PLOGI` is sent or received anyway, it shall abnormally terminate open `Sequences` and treat new `Sequences` as if `Logout` occurred. During login, other communication with the destination `Nx_Port` shall not be initiated or accepted. Successful completion occurs when `LS_ACC` reply is received; then either `Nx_Port(A)` or `Nx_Port(B)` may initiate communication. Names/fields mentioned: `PLOGI`, `LS_ACC`, `Nx_Port(A)`, `Nx_Port(B)`. Content types: procedure/flow, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 6.3.2.2 N_Port Login - Fabric present
nodes.summary: Covers `6.3.2.2 N_Port Login - Fabric present`: explicit destination `Nx_Port` login when a Fabric is present. [procedure/flow] `PLOGI` Link Service Sequence is sent in an Exchange with assigned `OX_ID`, `D_ID` of destination `Nx_Port`, and `S_ID` of originating `Nx_Port`. Payload contains `Service Parameters`, `N_Port_Name`, and `Node_Name`; `N_Port Service Parameters` are as specified in `6.6`, with applicability given in `tables 150 and 155`. [message/frame format] Normal reply is `LS_ACC` Link Service Reply Sequence in the Exchange identified by the `OX_ID` of the Login Sequence and `RX_ID` assigned by the Responder, with `D_ID` = originating `Nx_Port` and `S_ID` = responding `Nx_Port`; `LS_ACC` payload contains the responding `Nx_Port` `Service Parameters`. No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 6.3.2.3 Responses to N_Port Login - Fabric present
nodes.display_title: 6.3.2.3 Responses to N_Port Login - Fabric present
nodes.raw_title: Responses to N_Port Login - Fabric present
nodes.full_title: 6.3.2.3 Responses to N_Port Login - Fabric present
nodes.summary: Response/branch handling for explicit N_Port Login (`PLOGI`) when a Fabric is present: enumerates what an `Nx_Port` may receive after transmitting `PLOGI`. Covers normal success path `LS_ACC` reply Sequence with `OX_ID` equal to the `OX_ID` of the `PLOGI` and `Common Service N_Port/F_Port bit = 0 (Nx_Port)`: `D_ID` of `LS_ACC` = `S_ID` from `PLOGI`, `S_ID` of `LS_ACC` = `D_ID` from `PLOGI`, Payload includes destination `Service Parameters`, `64-bit N_Port_Name`, and `64-bit Node_Name`; `N_Port Service Parameters` as specified in `6.6`, applicability in `tables 150 and 155`; completion condition: `Nx_Port` may begin normal communication with the remote `N_Port`. Exception/branch: `F_BSY` with matching `OX_ID`; `D_ID` = `S_ID` of `PLOGI`; Fabric busy, retry later. No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 6.3.2.4 N_Port Login - No Fabric present
nodes.summary: Core topic: `6.3.2.4 N_Port Login - No Fabric present` — point-to-point explicit Login after discovering Fabric is not present during attempted `Fabric Login` (see `6.2.2.3`). Content types: procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions. Procedure: destination `N_Port` explicit Login requires transmitting a `PLOGI` `Link Service Sequence` in a new `Exchange`; only one `Nx_Port` needs to send `PLOGI`; if `N_Port_Names` were exchanged during `Fabric Login`, the `Nx_Port` with the highest `N_Port_Name` shall transmit `PLOGI`; if either side lacks the connected `N_Port_Name`, it may send `PLOGI`, and response processing resolves simultaneous `PLOGI`. Format/fields: `S_ID` shall be different than `D_ID`; Payload contains `Service Parameters`, `N_Port_Name`, `Node_Name`; `N_Port Service Parameters` as specified in `6.6`; applicability given in tables `150` and `155`. Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 6.3.2.5 Responses to N_Port Login - No Fabric present
nodes.display_title: 6.3.2.5 Responses to N_Port Login - No Fabric present
nodes.raw_title: Responses to N_Port Login - No Fabric present
nodes.full_title: 6.3.2.5 Responses to N_Port Login - No Fabric present
nodes.summary: Core topic: responses an `Nx_Port` may receive after transmitting `PLOGI` in point-to-point `N_Port Login` when no fabric is present. Covers response/branch handling, procedure/flow, timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions. Named anchors: `LS_ACC`, `P_BSY`, `P_RJT`, `LS_RJT`, `PLOGI`, `OX_ID`, `D_ID`, `S_ID`, `Common Service N_Port/F_Port bit = 0 (Nx_Port)`, `64-bit N_Port_Name`, `64-bit Node_Name`, `N_Port_ID`, `Service Parameters`, `N_Port Service Parameters`, `6.6`, `tables 150 and 155`, `Class not supported`, `Command already in progress`, `FC-FS-2`, `SOFs`, `Fabric Login`, `6.2.3`, `Class 1`, `Class 2`, `Class 3`. Branches: normal `LS_ACC` allows communication; busy/reject handling via `P_BSY`/`P_RJT`/`LS_RJT`; collision resolution by comparing `N_Port_Name`; no-response recovery and self-connect (`received N_Port_Name` equal) exception.

Core topic: explicit N_Port Login for Nx_Port-to-Nx_Port login with Fabric present, using a `PLOGI` Link request Sequence and normal `LS_ACC` reply Sequence. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions. Procedure: explicit login replaces previous Service Parameters; follows FC-FS-2 Exchange/Sequence management, buffer-to-buffer flow control, and end-to-end flow control. A well-behaved `Nx_Port` should `Logout` before new login; if `PLOGI` is sent/received anyway, open Sequences are abnormally terminated and new Sequences are treated as though Logout occurred. Message/frame details: `PLOGI` sent with `OX_ID`, `D_ID`, `S_ID`; payload includes Service Parameters, `N_Port_Name`, `Node_Name`; `LS_ACC` uses same `OX_ID` plus responder `RX_ID`. Responses: `LS_ACC` with Common Service `N_Port/F_Port bit = 0`; `F_BSY` means Fabric busy, retry later. References: `6.6`, tables `150` and `155` for applicable Service Parameters.

nodes.title: 6.3.3 SOFs
nodes.summary: Section `6.3.3 SOFs` covers `N_Port Login` class/SOF selection and retransmission rules for `PLOGI` when the destination `Nx_Port` does not support all login classes. `N_Port Login` is only supported in `Class 1`, `2`, and `3`; the `PLOGI Sequence` may require retransmission in a different `Class` with the appropriate `SOF`, in the same manner as `Fabric Login` (`6.2.3`). Login validity for all supported classes is determined by the validity bits in the `PLOGI LS_ACC Reply Sequence`. Named anchors: `SOFs`, `N_Port Login`, `PLOGI`, `PLOGI Sequence`, `Fabric Login`, `LS_ACC Reply Sequence`, `Nx_Port`, `Class 1`, `Class 2`, `Class 3`, `validity bits`, `6.2.3`. Content types: `procedure/flow`, `timer/negotiation`, `completion/exception conditions`. No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 6.3.4 Frequency
nodes.summary: Covers `6.3.4 Frequency` of `N_Port Login`: installation-dependent frequency based on configuration changes that may alter `N_Port_ID`; other `Nx_Ports` `Service Parameters` persist until next `N_Port Login` or `N_Port Logout` (implicit or explicit). Also includes adjacent completion rules for `PLOGI`/`LS_ACC` exchange: originator/responder login end conditions by `Class 1`, `Class 2`, `Class 3`, using `ACK`, `EOFt`, `EOFdt`, `LS_ACC`; credit initialization for `Fabric present` vs `point-to-point topology` (`end-to-end Credit`, `buffer-to-buffer Credit`). Mentions transition into `6.4 Logout` with `LOGO`, explicit/implicit logout, `Fabric Logout`, and sequence termination notes. Content types: procedure/flow; response/branch handling; completion/exception conditions. Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 6.3.5 N_Port Login completion - Originator
nodes.summary: Core topic: `6.3.5 N_Port Login completion - Originator` defines when the Originator considers `PLOGI` Login ended and what Credit initialization occurs at completion. Content types: procedure/flow; response/branch handling; completion/exception conditions; timer/negotiation. Completion conditions by class: Class 1 when Originator has transmitted `ACK (EOFt or EOFdt)` to `LS_ACC`; Class 2 when Originator has transmitted `ACK (EOFt)` to `LS_ACC`; Class 3 when Originator has received `LS_ACC`. Post-completion initialization: with a Fabric present, `end-to-end Credit` is initialized; in point-to-point topology, `buffer-to-buffer` and `end-to-end Credit` are initialized. Exact anchors: `PLOGI`, `ACK`, `EOFt`, `EOFdt`, `LS_ACC`, `Class 1`, `Class 2`, `Class 3`, `Fabric`, `point-to-point topology`, `end-to-end Credit`, `buffer-to-buffer`, `Originator`. Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 6.3.6 N_Port Login completion - Responder
nodes.summary: Core topic: `6.3.6 N_Port Login completion - Responder` defines when the Responder considers `N_Port Login` ended for a `PLOGI` request and what Credit values are initialized at completion. Completion/exception conditions, response/branch handling: Class 1 ends when the Responder has received the ACK (`EOFt` or `EOFdt`) to the `LS_ACC`; Class 2 ends when the Responder has received the ACK (`EOFt`) to the `LS_ACC`; Class 3 ends when the Responder has transmitted the `LS_ACC`. With a Fabric present, `end-to-end Credit` is initialized. In a point-to-point topology, `buffer-to-buffer` and `end-to-end Credit` are initialized. Contained content types: procedure/flow; response/branch handling; completion/exception conditions. Tables: none. Figures: none.

Section `6.3 N_Port Login` covers N_Port Login after `Fabric Login`, deciding behavior based on whether a `Fabric` is present and directing an `Nx_Port` to `6.3.2.2` or `6.3.2.4`. Core functions: exchange each `Nx_Port`’s operating characteristics, `N_Port_Name`, and `Node_Name`; if a Fabric is not present, assign the native `N_Port_ID` for both `Nx_Ports`; initialize `Nx_Port` end-to-end `Credit`. Exact anchors present in surrounding completion context: `FLOGI`, `LS_ACC`, `ACK`, `EOFt`, `EOFdt`, `Class 1`, `Class 2`, `Class 3`, buffer-to-buffer `Credit`. Content types: procedure/flow (sequence after `Fabric Login`), response/branch handling (Fabric present vs not present), completion/exception conditions (depends on Fabric determination). Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 6.4 Logout
6.4.1 Introduction
nodes.summary: Core topic: `6.4.1 Introduction` to `6.4 Logout`—destination Logout procedure for removing service between two `N_Port_IDs` or between an `N_Port_ID` and a `Fabric`; Logout releases resources, identifiers, and relationships associated with maintaining service. Named anchors: `Explicit Nx_Port Logout`, `Explicit Fabric Logout`, `Implicit Logout`, `Nx_Port`, `N_Port_ID`, `Fabric`, `6.4.2`, `6.4.3`, `6.4.4`, `Logout (LOGO)`, `LS_ACC`, `FC-FS-2`, `destination Nx_Port`, `virtual N_Port_ID`. States that explicit Nx_Port Logout may be requested Nx_Port-to-Nx_Port; explicit Fabric Logout may be requested Nx_Port-to-Fabric or Fabric-to-Nx_Port; implicit Logout may occur between an Nx_Port and the Fabric. Content types: procedure/flow, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 6.4.2 Explicit N_Port Logout
nodes.summary: Core topic: `6.4.2 Explicit N_Port Logout` defines explicit Logout between Nx_Ports. `Logout (LOGO) Link Service request Sequence` is transmitted to a destination `Nx_Port`; the Logout procedure is complete when the responding `Nx_Port` transmits a `LS_ACC Link Service reply Sequence`. Contains `procedure/flow` and `completion/exception conditions`. Key anchors: `LOGO`, `LS_ACC`, `Nx_Port`, `N_Port_ID`, `Sequence`, `FC-FS-2`. Preconditions/exception note: the initiating `Nx_Port` shall terminate other open `Sequences` that it initiated with the destination `Nx_Port` before performing Logout; otherwise, the state of other open `Sequences` is unspecified in the provided text. Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 6.4.3 Explicit Fabric Logout
nodes.summary: Covers **6.4.3 Explicit Fabric Logout**: procedure/flow and response/branch handling for explicit Fabric Logout of an `N_Port_ID` using `Logout (LOGO)` Link Service request Sequence (`4.2.8`) between `Nx_Port` and `Fx_Port`, including `Well-known address FFFFFEh`. Completion/exception conditions: complete for responding `FC_Port` on transmit of `LS_ACC` Link Service reply Sequence; complete for requesting `FC_Port` on receipt of `LS_ACC`. Unsupported handling: reject with `LS_RJT` using reason code `"Command not supported"` and reason code explanation `"No additional explanation"`; sender receiving that `LS_RJT` shall implicitly log out the other `FC_Port`. Includes virtualization requirement for `Fx_Port` supporting `N_Port_ID virtualization`, and post-logout behavior: implicit logout with all other `N_Port_IDs`, no originating/routing frames until subsequent reassignment of the `N_Port_ID` by the Fabric.

nodes.title: 6.4.4 Implicit Logout
nodes.summary: Core topic: `6.4.4 Implicit Logout` rules for when an `Nx_Port` is implicitly logged out from the `Fabric`, if present, or attached `Nx_Port` in a point-to-point topology, and when communication may resume. Contains: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions. Triggers include receiving or transmitting `NOS` or `OLS`. Re-entry condition: communication with other `Nx_Ports` shall not be accepted until the `Fabric Login` procedure is complete (`implicit` or `explicit`). During Login, if `N_Port_ID`, `F_Port_Name` and/or `Fabric_Name` changed since last Fabric Login and `Clean Address bit` is zero, the `Nx_Port` shall implicitly logout with all `Nx_Ports`, wait an `R_A_TOV` timeout period starting when the change is detected, then require new `N_Port Logins` before communication. Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 6.4.5 The effects of FLOGI, FDISC, and LOGO
nodes.display_title: 6.4.5 The effects of FLOGI, FDISC, and LOGO
nodes.raw_title: The effects of FLOGI, FDISC, and LOGO
nodes.full_title: 6.4.5 The effects of FLOGI, FDISC, and LOGO
nodes.summary: Core topic: effects of `FLOGI`, `FDISC`, and `LOGO` on pre-existing Fabric Logins and `Permanent Port Name (PPN)` associations at an `F_Port`/`Nx_Port`. `Table 148 – Effects of FLOGI, FDISC, & LOGO on Permanent Port Name (PPN)`: defines per-ELS outcomes by `Condition 1: FLOGI not completed, or all IDs logged out`, `Condition 2: FLOGI Completed, & at least one ID logged in`, `Condition 3: FDISC(s) completed, & at least one ID logged in`. Anchors: `D_ID FFFFFEh`, `S_ID=0d`, `S_ID not = 0d`, `BB_Credit`, `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `Port_Name`, `N_Port ID`, `PPN`, `Name Server`, `RSCNs`, `OLS`, `R_A_TOV`, `Fx_Port`, `Unable to perform command request`, `insufficient resources`, `Logical error, invalid port name`, `6.2.2.3 Response to Explicit Fabric Login`. Content types: response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions, timer/negotiation.

Core topic: `6.4 Logout` defines destination Logout for removing service between two `N_Port_ID`s or between an `N_Port_ID` and a `Fabric`, releasing resources, identifiers, and relationships. Anchors: `Logout`, `LOGO`, `LS_ACC`, `Nx_Port`, `N_Port_ID`, `Fabric`, `Explicit N_Port Logout`, `Explicit Fabric Logout`, `Implicit Logout`, `FC-FS-2`, `Sequence`. `6.4.1 Introduction` scopes explicit Nx_Port-to-Nx_Port, Nx_Port-to-Fabric, Fabric-to-Nx_Port, and implicit Nx_Port/Fabric logout. `6.4.2 Explicit N_Port Logout` states Logout is done by transmitting a `Logout (LOGO) Link Service request Sequence` to a destination `Nx_Port`; procedure completes when the responder transmits an `LS_ACC Link Service reply Sequence`. Before explicit Logout, the initiator shall terminate other open `Sequences` it initiated with the destination `Nx_Port`. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 6.5 Extended Login Processing
nodes.summary: Section `6.5 Extended Login Processing` covers support signaling and scope of an Extended Login request in `PLOGI` or `FLOGI`, indicated by the `Payload Bit` (see `6.6.2.4.19`) set to one in the request and associated `LS_ACC`; states that Extended Login provides additional information. Also includes `Table 148 – Effects of FLOGI, FDISC, & LOGO on Permanent Port Name (PPN)`, which defines response/branch handling and completion/exception conditions for `FLOGI`, `FDISC`, `LOGO` against F_Port state conditions `Condition 1`, `Condition 2`, `Condition 3`, including `D_ID FFFFFEh`, `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `F_RJT`, `RC=login required`, `RC=command not supported`, `RCE=Request not supported`, `S_ID=0`, `OLS/NOS`, `Primitive Sequence Protocols`, `PPN`, `Port_Name`, `N_Port ID`, `Unable to perform command request`, and `insufficient resources`. Content types: response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 6.6 Service Parameters
6.6.1 ELS and LS_ACC Payload
nodes.summary: Defines the `ELS and LS_ACC Payload` for `FLOGI`, `PLOGI`, `PLOGI LS_ACC`, and `FLOGI LS_ACC`, via `Table 149 – FLOGI, PLOGI or LS_ACC Payload`, which defines the payload/message format and field layout. Covers payload composition for `ELS_Command code`, `Common Service Parameters`, `Port_Name`, `Node_ or Fabric_Name`, `Class 1 and Class 6 Service Parameters`, `Class 2 Service Parameters`, `Class 3 Service Parameters`, `Vendor Version Level`, `Services Availability`, `Login Extension Data Length`, `Clock Synchronization QoS`, and `Login Extension Data`. Notes `Payload Bit` gating: when set to one, extended fields are present; when set to zero, payload is `116 bytes long`. Includes procedure/flow and response/branch handling for large Login: send Login with `Payload Bit` zero, inspect `Query Buffer Conditions bit` in `LS_ACC`, optionally issue `RPBC ELS`, then compare `ELS Receive Data Field Size` and `Buffer-to-buffer Receive Data_Field Size` against `256 bytes`.

nodes.title: 6.6.2 Common Service Parameters
6.6.2.1 Applicability
nodes.summary: Defines applicability of Common Service Parameters for Login exchanges, keyed by class and message context: `PLOGI`, `FLOGI`, `PLOGI LS_ACC`, `FLOGI LS_ACC`, plus `Default Login Value` for payload `words 1-4`. `Table 150 – Common Service Parameter applicability` defines per-class (`Class 1a`, `2`, `3`) yes/no meaning and defaults for fields including `FC-PH Version - obsolete` (`2020h`), `Buffer-to-buffer Credit`, `Common Features`, `Continuously increasing relative offset`, `Clean Address`, `Multiple N_Port_ID Support`, `Random relative offset`, `Virtual Fabrics bit`, `Valid Vendor Version Level`, `Multiple N_Port_ID Assignment`, `N_Port/F_Port`, `BB_Credit Management`, `E_D_TOV Resolution`, `Query Data Buffer conditions`, `R_T_TOV Value`, `SEQ_CNT`, `Payload Bit`. Content types: `timer/negotiation`, `message/frame format`, `completion/exception conditions` (applicable/not applicable).

nodes.title: 6.6.2.2 Payload
nodes.summary: Section `6.6.2.2 Payload` covers the `Common Service Parameters Payload for FLOGI`, giving the `FLOGI` message/frame format and applicability context from continued `PLOGI`, `FLOGI`, and `LS_ACC` Common Service Parameters. `Table 150 – Common Service Parameter applicability(Continued)` shows applicability/defaults for `BB_SC_N`, `Buffer-to-Buffer Receive Data Field Size`, `Nx_Port Total Concurrent Sequences`, `Relative offset by Info Category`, `R_A_TOV`, and `E_D_TOV Value` across `PLOGI`, `FLOGI`, and `LS_ACC`, including notes on `Class 1`, `Class 2`, `Class 3`, `Class 6`, `N_Port`, `F_Port`, and `L_Port` (timer/negotiation). `Table 151 – Common Service Parameters - FLOGI` defines the payload word/bit layout: `Word 0` `FC-PH Version - obsolete` and `Buffer-to-buffer Credit`; `Word 1` `Common Features (see table 150)`, `BB_SC_N`, `Receive Data Field size`; `Word 2` and `Word 3` reserved (message/frame format).

nodes.title: 6.6.2.3 Buffer-to-buffer Credit
nodes.summary: Section `6.6.2.3 Buffer-to-buffer Credit` defines the `Buffer-to-buffer Credit` field in Common Service Parameters payloads: `word 0, bits 15-0`, number of buffers available for `Class 1` and `Class 6 connect-request`, `Class 2`, or `Class 3` frames subject to buffer-to-buffer flow control; valid values `1 to 32767`, `0` reserved. Scope note: for `N_Port Login`, meaningful only for an `Nx_Port` in point-to-point topology and between two `NL_Ports` on the same loop. References surrounding message/frame formats for `PLOGI`, `PLOGI LS_ACC`, and `FLOGI LS_ACC`. Table `151 – Common Service Parameters - FLOGI`: shows Common Service Parameters layout including `Buffer-to-buffer Credit`. Table `153 – Common Service Parameters - FLOGI LS_ACC`: shows Common Service Parameters payload for `FLOGI LS_ACC`, including `Buffer-to-buffer Credit (Fx_Port)`. Content types: `message/frame format`, `timer/negotiation`.

nodes.title: 6.6.2.4 Common Features
6.6.2.4.1 Continuously increasing relative offset
nodes.summary: Defines `6.6.2.4.1 Continuously increasing relative offset`: semantics of the Common Features bit `word 1, bit 31` for support of continuously increasing relative offset in Common Service Parameters. Values: `0 = not supported`, `1 = supported`. Applies when `F_CTL bit 3` is present, on a frame-by-frame `SEQ_CNT` basis within a `Sequence`, and only for Information Categories supporting relative offset (`word 2, bits 15-0`). States capability requirements for the `Nx_Port` supplying the parameter and applicability to both `Sequence Initiator` and `Sequence Recipient` for all supported Classes of Service. References surrounding message/frame format context for `PLOGI`, `PLOGI LS_ACC`, and `FLOGI LS_ACC`. Table `153 – Common Service Parameters - FLOGI LS_ACC`: shows Common Service Parameters Payload field layout. Content types: message/frame format, timer/negotiation.

nodes.title: 6.6.2.4.2 Clean Address
nodes.summary: Defines `Clean Address` semantics for `FLOGI LS_ACC`: `word 1, bit 31`; `0 = No information`, `1 = Clean Address`. Core rule: tells an `Nx_Port` whether the Fabric-assigned address was previously used by another device within `R_A_TOV`. `0` means the address may or may not have been used within `R_A_TOV`; `1` means it has not been used by another device within `R_A_TOV`, or was assigned to the current device for a previous `FLOGI` and not changed within `R_A_TOV`. Scope/meaning constraint: only meaningful in the `FLOGI LS_ACC`; not meaningful in the `FLOGI request`. Content types: `message/frame format`, `timer/negotiation`, `completion/exception conditions`, `response/branch handling`. No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 6.6.2.4.3 Multiple N_Port_ID Support
nodes.summary: Core topic: `6.6.2.4.3 Multiple N_Port_ID Support` defines the `Multiple N_Port_ID Support bit` (`word 1, bit 31`) values `0 = not supported`, `1 = supported`, and when an `N_Port` sets it to indicate capability to request multiple `N_Port_ID`s using the `FDISC ELS`. It states the bit in a `FLOGI request` means the supplying `N_Port` is or is not capable of requesting additional `N_Port_ID`s, and that this bit is only meaningful in the `FLOGI request`, not meaningful in the `FLOGI LS_ACC`. Exact anchors: `N_Port`, `N_Port_ID`, `Multiple N_Port_ID Support bit`, `word 1, bit 31`, `FDISC ELS`, `FLOGI request`, `FLOGI LS_ACC`. Content types: message/frame format; response/branch handling.

nodes.title: 6.6.2.4.4 Random relative offset
nodes.summary: Covers `6.6.2.4.4 Random relative offset`: semantics of the `random relative offset bit` (`word 1, bit 30`) and when an `Nx_Port` must support random relative offset values if present via `F_CTL bit 3`. States `0 = not supported`, `1 = supported`; random values may increase, decrease, or fluctuate within a `Sequence`. Applicability is limited to Information Categories where relative offset is supported (`word 3, bits 15-0`), with reference to `FC-FS-2` for use. Also states the bit applies to both `Sequence Initiator` and `Sequence Recipient` for all supported `Classes of Service`. Content types: `message/frame format`, `timer/negotiation` (capability indication). Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 6.6.2.4.5 Virtual Fabrics bit
nodes.summary: Defines the `Virtual Fabrics bit` in `word 1, bit 30`: `0 = not supported`, `1 = supported`; this bit indicates support for `Virtual fabrics` and references `clause 8`. Core topic is the semantics of the `Virtual Fabrics bit` parameter value in the login/service parameter context. Nearby exact anchors named in this section text include `Random relative offset`, `F_CTL bit 3`, `word 3, bits 15-0`, `FC-FS-2`, `Valid Vendor Version Level`, `PLOGI`, `PLOGI LS_ACC`, `FLOGI`, `Vendor Version Level`, `words 25 through 28`, and `table 149`, but this subsection itself only states the `Virtual Fabrics bit` meaning. Content types: message/frame format (bit/field definition), timer/negotiation (capability support indication). No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 6.6.2.4.6 Valid Vendor Version Level
nodes.summary: Defines the `Valid Vendor Version Level` indicator in `6.6.2.4.6`: bit semantics for `word 1, bit 29` and when the `Vendor Version Level` field is meaningful. Applies in `PLOGI`, `PLOGI LS_ACC`, and `FLOGI`; `0 = not valid`, `1 = Valid`. If set to one, `Vendor Version Level` (`words 25 through 28 in table 149`) contains valid information; if zero, the field is not meaningful. Core identifiers/anchors: `Valid Vendor Version Level bit`, `Vendor Version Level`, `word 1, bit 29`, `words 25 through 28`, `table 149`, `PLOGI`, `PLOGI LS_ACC`, `FLOGI`. nodes.title: 6.6.2.4.10 E_D_TOV Resolution

Content types: `timer/negotiation`.

nodes.title: 6.6.2.4.11 Multicast
nodes.summary: Defines `Multicast` capability signaling in login parameters: the `Multicast bit` (`word 1, bit 25`) indicates whether multicast is supported by the Fabric. Values are `0 = Multicast not supported by the Fabric` and `1 = Multicast supported by the Fabric`. Scope/usage constraint: only meaningful in the `FLOGI LS_ACC`; multicast requirements are referenced to `FC-FS-2`. Nearby identifiers named in this page context include `FLOGI request`, `FLOGI LS_ACC`, `Multiple N_Port_ID Assignment bit`, `N_Port/F_Port bit`, `BB_Credit Management bit`, and `E_D_TOV resolution bit`, but this section itself specifically defines `Multicast bit (word 1, bit 25)`. Content types: `message/frame format`, `response/branch handling`. Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 6.6.2.4.12 Broadcast
nodes.summary: Defines `Broadcast` capability signaling in FLOGI service-parameter word 1, bit 24 context: `6.6.2.4.12 Broadcast` states `0 = Broadcast not supported by the Fabric`, `1 = Broadcast supported by the Fabric`. Core topic is the `Broadcast` bit as a Fabric support indicator, adjacent to related FLOGI/FLOGI `LS_ACC` capability bits in the same service-parameter block. nodes.title: 6.6.2.4.7 Multiple N_Port_ID Assignment
nodes.summary: Defines `Multiple N_Port_ID Assignment` capability signaling for `FLOGI LS_ACC`: `0 = not supported`, `1 = supported`. Core rule: when `Multiple N_Port_ID Support bit` (`word 1, bit 31`) in the `FLOGI request` is one, `Multiple N_Port_ID Assignment bit` (`word 1, bit 29`) shall be one if the `F_Port` can assign multiple `N_Port IDs` to the attached `N_Port` using the `FDISC ELS`; otherwise zero. Also zero when `Multiple N_Port_ID Support bit` in the `FLOGI request` is zero. Bit is only meaningful in `FLOGI LS_ACC`, not meaningful in the `FLOGI request`. Includes `NOTE 14` noting modification from previous revisions. Content types: `response/branch handling`, `message/frame format`, `timer/negotiation` (via surrounding anchors `E_D_TOV`). Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 6.6.2.4.8 N_Port/F_Port
nodes.summary: Core topic: `N_Port/F_Port` bit semantics in login exchanges, specifically `word 1, bit 28` values and who sets them for `PLOGI`, `PLOGI LS_ACC`, `FLOGI`, and `FLOGI LS_ACC`. `0 = Nx_Port`, `1 = Fx_Port`. An `Nx_Port` shall set the `N_Port/F_Port` bit to zero for `PLOGI`, `PLOGI LS_ACC`, and `FLOGI`. In Fabric topology, the `Fx_Port` sets the bit to one in `FLOGI LS_ACC`; in point-to-point topology, the `N_Port` sets it to zero in `FLOGI LS_ACC`. Nearby anchors referenced in surrounding text: `Multiple N_Port_ID Assignment`, `Multiple N_Port_ID Support bit`, `FDISC ELS`. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, message/frame format. No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 6.6.2.4.9 BB_Credit Management
nodes.summary: Covers `BB_Credit Management` semantics for `word 1, bit 27` in login service parameters: `0 = BB_Credit management specified in FC-FS-2 shall be used`, `1 = Alternate BB_Credit management specified in FC-AL-2 shall be used`. Core rule: the `BB_Credit Management bit` specifies the type of BB_Credit Management to be used. Exact anchors present in surrounding normative context on this page: `FLOGI`, `FLOGI LS_ACC`, `PLOGI`, `PLOGI LS_ACC`, `Multiple N_Port_ID Assignment`, `N_Port/F_Port`, `BB_Credit Management`, `E_D_TOV Resolution`, `E_D_TOV`, `Multicast`, `Broadcast`, `Nx_Port`, `Fx_Port`, `N_Port_ID`, `FDISC`. No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 6.6.2.4.13 Hunt Group
nodes.summary: Defines the `Hunt Group` capability bit in common service parameters: `Hunt Group bit` (`word 1, bit 23`) indicates whether the Fabric supports Hunt Group routing in `FLOGI LS_ACC`; `0 = Hunt Groups not supported`, `1 = Hunt Groups supported`; if `0`, the Fabric shall not support Hunt Group routing, if `1`, it shall support it (see `FC-FS-2`). Context on the same page also names adjacent fields `Broadcast bit` (`word 1, bit 24`), `Query Buffer Conditions bit` (`word 1, bit 22`), `Report Port Buffer Conditions (RPBC) ELS`, `Clock Synchronization Primitive Capable bit` (`word 1, bit 20`), and `R_T_TOV value`. `Table 154 – Clock Synchronization Applicability`: defines meanings when the clock sync bit is set to one for server, other `FC_Ports`, and Fabric. Content types: `response/branch handling`, `completion/exception conditions`.

nodes.title: 6.6.2.4.14 Query Buffer Conditions
nodes.summary: Defines `Query Buffer Conditions` bit semantics in `word 1, bit 22`: `0 = No buffer conditions to report`, `1 = Buffer conditions to report`. Covers when an `FC_Port` may assert the bit and ties it to `Report Port Buffer Conditions (RPBC) ELS` for gathering reported conditions. Assertion is allowed only if the `FC_Port` supports `RPBC ELS` and either `ELS Receive Data Field Size` differs from `Buffer-to-buffer Receive Data_Field Size` in the common service parameters, or `multi-frame ELSs` are not supported. Nearby named context on the same page includes `Broadcast` (`word 1, bit 24`), `Hunt Group` (`word 1, bit 23`), `Clock Synchronization Primitive Capable` (`word 1, bit 20`), and `R_T_TOV`. Table `154 – Clock Synchronization Applicability`: defines meaning of clock synchronization capability by port/fabric type. Content types: completion/exception conditions, response/branch handling.

nodes.title: 6.6.2.4.15 Clock Synchronization Primitive Capable
nodes.summary: Core topic: `6.6.2.4.15 Clock Synchronization Primitive Capable` defines the `Clock Synchronization Primitive Capable` bit at `word 1, bit 20`, with values `0 = not Clock Synchronization Primitive Capable` and `1 = Clock Synchronization Primitive Capable`, and ties support to the Primitive method of Clock Synchronization (`FC-FS-2`). Applicability differs by login/context: `Clock Synchronization Server (N_Port Login only)` can generate clock synchronization Primitive Signals; `Other FC_Ports (N_Port Login only)` can receive and act on them; `Fabric (FLOGI LS_ACC only)` can receive and act on them. Includes `Table 154 – Clock Synchronization Applicability`, which defines meaning when the bit is set to one by port/fabric type. Content types: timer/negotiation, response/branch handling.

nodes.title: 6.6.2.4.16 R_T_TOV value
nodes.summary: Core topic: `6.6.2.4.16 R_T_TOV value` enumerates the `R_T_TOV` setting values: `0 = Default value of 100 milliseconds`, `1 = Short value of 100 microseconds` [timer/negotiation]. Nearby anchors on the same page include `Broadcast bit` (`word 1, bit 24`, meaningful only in `FLOGI LS_ACC`), `Hunt Group` (`word 1, bit 23`), `Query Buffer Conditions` (`word 1, bit 22`, may trigger `Report Port Buffer Conditions (RPBC) ELS`), and `Clock Synchronization Primitive Capable` (`word 1, bit 20`). `Table 154 – Clock Synchronization Applicability` defines meaning of the bit being set to one for `Clock Synchronization Server (N_Port Login only)`, `Other FC_Ports (N_Port Login only)`, and `Fabric (FLOGI LS_ACC only)` [response/branch handling, timer/negotiation].

nodes.title: 6.6.2.4.17 Dynamic Half Duplex Supported
nodes.summary:
Core topic: `6.6.2.4.17 Dynamic Half Duplex Supported` defines `Dynamic Half Duplex Supported` (`word 1, bit 18`) in Service Parameters: `0 = DHD not supported`, `1 = DHD supported`; if set, the `FC_Port` supports reception of the `DHD primitive`. Note states `DHD` applies to `FC-AL` topologies; see `FC-AL-2`.

Also present on this page: `R_T_TOV` (`word 1, bit 19`) short-value support (`100 milliseconds` vs `100 microseconds`); `6.6.2.4.18 SEQ_CNT` (`word 1, bit 17`) with `Continuously Increasing SEQ_CNT`; `6.6.2.4.19 Payload Bit` (`word 1 bit 16`) for `FLOGI`, `PLOGI`, `LS_ACC`, `Login Extension Length field`; `6.6.2.5 BB_SC_N`; `6.6.2.6 Buffer-to-buffer Receive Data_Field Size`. Content types: `message/frame format`, `response/branch handling`, `timer/negotiation`.

nodes.title: 6.6.2.4.18 SEQ_CNT
nodes.summary: Core topic: `6.6.2.4.18 SEQ_CNT` defines `SEQ_CNT` (`word 1, bit 17`) behavior in `PLOGI`/`FLOGI` Service Parameters: `0 = Normal rules apply to SEQ_CNT`, `1 = Continuously Increasing SEQ_CNT shall be used.` If set, the `Nx_Port` guarantees all frames in an `Exchange` use monotonically increasing `SEQ_CNT`; each `Exchange` starts at `SEQ_CNT` = `0` in the first frame, and every transmitted frame increments by one, including across transfers of `Sequence Initiative`; frames received from the other `Nx_Port` do not affect transmitted `SEQ_CNT`. References `FC-FS-2`. Nearby anchors on the same page: `R_T_TOV`, `Dynamic Half Duplex Supported`, `Payload Bit`, `BB_SC_N`, `LS_ACC`. Content types: timer/negotiation, message/frame format, response/branch handling.

nodes.title: 6.6.2.4.19 Payload Bit
nodes.summary: Section `6.6.2.4.19 Payload Bit` defines the `Payload Bit` (`word 1 bit 16`) in `FLOGI`/`PLOGI` Service Parameters and how it determines login payload length. Values: `0 = Payload length is 116 bytes`; `1 = Payload length in bytes is 256 plus four times the value of the Login Extension Length field`. It also specifies response handling: if `Payload Bit` is `0` in `PLOGI` or `FLOGI`, then `LS_ACC` sent in response shall have `Payload Bit` set to `0`. Nearby anchors appearing in the excerpt: `Login Extension Length field`, `LS_ACC`, `BB_SC_N`, `word 1, bits 15-12`. Content types: message/frame format, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions. Tables: none. Figures: none.

Section `6.6.2.4 Common Features` defines the `Common Features` field in Common Service Parameters, specifically `6.6.2.4.1 Continuously increasing relative offset`. Core rule: `word 1, bit 31` indicates `0 = not supported`, `1 = supported` for continuously increasing relative offset; if set, the `Nx_Port` shall support relative offset when `F_CTL bit 3` is present, on a frame-by-frame `SEQ_CNT` basis, for applicable Information Categories (`word 2, bits 15-0`). Applies to both `Sequence Initiator` and `Sequence Recipient` for all supported Classes of Service. References surrounding payload layouts for `PLOGI`, `PLOGI LS_ACC`, and `FLOGI LS_ACC`. Tables: `Table 153 – Common Service Parameters - FLOGI LS_ACC` shows payload field layout for `FLOGI LS_ACC`; `Table 151 – Common Service Parameters - FLOGI` shows Common Service Parameters field layout for `FLOGI`. Content types: message/frame format, timer/negotiation.

nodes.title: 6.6.2.5 BB_SC_N
nodes.summary: Core topic: `BB_SC_N` field semantics in Login Service Parameters. `BB_SC_N` (word 1, bits 15-12) is the Buffer-to-buffer State Change Number in a `PLOGI` or `FLOGI` frame; it specifies that the sender is requesting `2BB_SC_N` frames between consecutive `BB_SCs` primitives, and `2BB_SC_N` `R_RDY` primitives between consecutive `BB_SCr` primitives. Names/anchors: `BB_SC_N`, `Buffer-to-buffer State Change Number`, `PLOGI`, `FLOGI`, `BB_SCs`, `BB_SCr`, `R_RDY`, `BB_Credit recovery process`, `FC-FS-2`, `word 1`, `bits 15-12`. Content types: message/frame format; timer/negotiation. Tables/figures: none.

nodes.title: 6.6.2.6 Buffer-to-buffer Receive Data_Field size
nodes.summary: Section `6.6.2.6 Buffer-to-buffer Receive Data_Field size` defines the `buffer-to-buffer Receive Data_Field Size field` (`word 1, bits 11-0`): it specifies the largest frame `Data_Field Size` that may be received by the `Nx_Port` supplying the `Service Parameters` as a `Sequence Recipient`. Use this section for questions about receive-side frame payload size limits advertised in login/service parameters, especially interpretation of the `Buffer-to-buffer Receive Data_Field Size` value and recipient capability. Exact anchors present: `Buffer-to-buffer Receive Data_Field Size field`, `Data_Field Size`, `Nx_Port`, `Service Parameters`, `Sequence Recipient`, `word 1, bits 11-0`. Content types: `message/frame format`, `timer/negotiation`. Tables/figures: none.

nodes.title: 6.6.2.10 E_D_TOV
nodes.summary: Defines `E_D_TOV` encoding and negotiation semantics in `6.6.2.10 E_D_TOV`. Core rule: `E_D_TOV Resolution bit` (`word 1, bit 26`) selects units for the `E_D_TOV value`: `0` = `1 millisecond` increments, `1` = `1 nanosecond` increments; example `0000000Ah` means either `10 milliseconds` or `10 nanoseconds` depending on the bit. For `PLOGI`, the `E_D_TOV value` in `LS_ACC` to the `PLOGI` shall be greater than or equal to the value in the `PLOGI`; the `E_D_TOV value` in the `LS_ACC` is then used by each `Nx_Port`. Includes pointer to `FC-FS-2` for `E_D_TOV` definition. Content types: timer/negotiation, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions. No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 6.6.2.7 Total Concurrent Sequences
nodes.summary: Defines `Total Concurrent Sequences` (word 2, bits 23 - 16): total Concurrent Sequences across all classes an `Nx_Port` can support as a Recipient, constrained to be less than or equal to the sum of class-by-class Concurrent Sequences; includes example `Class 2` + `Class 3` capacities (e.g., 10 + 10 but total 15). Nearby anchors appearing in this section block: `Relative offset by category` (word 2, bits 15 - 0), `Information Category 1110b`, `0010b`, `R_A_TOV`, `0000000Ah`, `E_D_TOV`, `E_D_TOV Resolution bit` (word 1, bit 26), `PLOGI`, `LS_ACC`, `Port_Name`, `Name_Identifier`, bits `63-60`, `FC-FS-2`. nodes.title: 6.6.5.3 Class validity
nodes.summary: Section 6.6.5.3 defines Class validity for Class Service Parameters: `Class validity bit` at `word 0, bit 31`; `0 = Invalid - Class not supported`, `1 = Valid - Class supported`. nodes.title: 6.6.5.4 Service options
6.6.5.4.1 Introduction
nodes.summary: Defines service options introduction for class-of-service parameters, centered on `Intermix Mode` semantics and support negotiation for Class 1/6, Class 2, and Class 3. States that service options specify optional features supported by the port supplying the service parameters; `Intermix Mode bit (word 0, bit 30)` is meaningful only for Class 1 and 6. Covers exclusive connections, `F_BSY` response behavior to Class 2 frames and connect-request Data frames (`SOFc1`), and Fabric/N_Port Login outcomes using `LS_ACC`. `Table 159 – Intermix Mode Support` shows `Nx_Port`/`Fx_Port` bit combinations and whether Intermix is functional. Includes response/branch handling, timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions: if both N_Ports indicate support, Intermix is functional; otherwise Class 1 dedicated connections shall be removed before Class 2 or Class 3 transmission.

nodes.title: 6.6.5.4.2 Intermix Mode
nodes.summary: Intermix Mode service option for Class 1/Class 6 login negotiation and when intermixed traffic is functional. Field/bit: `Intermix Mode bit` (`word 0, bit 30`), values `0 = Intermix not requested`, `1 = Intermix requested`; meaningful only for `Class 1` and `Class 6`, not `Class 2`/`Class 3`. Covers exclusive connections, `F_BSY` response to `Class 2` frames and `SOFc1` connect-request Data frames from third `N_Port`s, and behavior for Fabric Login / N_Port Login. Named messages/identifiers: `LS_ACC`, `F_BSY`, `SOFc1`, `N_Port`, `Nx_Port`, `F_Port`, `Fx_Port`. Table `Table 159 – Intermix Mode Support`: defines `Nx_Port`/`Fx_Port` bit combinations and whether “Intermix is functional.” Content types: response/branch handling, timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 6.6.5.4.3 Stacked Connect-requests
nodes.summary: Core topic: `6.6.5.4.3 Stacked Connect-requests` in `Fabric Login` and adjacent `Sequential delivery` login parameter semantics for `FLOGI`/`LS_ACC`. Defines `Stack Connect-request bits` (`word 0, bits 29 - 28`) as only meaningful for `Class 1` in `FLOGI LS_ACC`; values `00b` = not supported, `01b` = `Lock-Down Mode`, `10b` = `Transparent Mode`, `11b` = `Reserved`. Notes optional support in `Fabric`, `Nx_Port`, and changed `Nx_Port`/`Fx_Port` behavior when functional. Also covers `Sequential delivery bit` (`word 0, bit 27`) for `class 2 and 3 N_Port Class Service Parameters`, including `Nx_Port` request / `Fx_Port` capability combinations and in-order delivery rules absent `Fabric events`. `Table 160 – Stacked Connect-request support Login Bits`: defines bit-combination meanings. `Table 161 – Sequential delivery support`: defines `Nx_Port`/`Fx_Port` support outcomes. Content types: timer/negotiation, response/branch handling.

nodes.title: 6.6.5.4.4 Sequential delivery
