It states applicability/not meaningful cases (`Class 1`, `Class 6`, `N_Port Login`), `Nx_Port` request semantics, `Fx_Port` behavior in `LS_ACC`, and Fabric behavior/limits under `Fabric events` such as `Fabric reconfiguration` or `zoning changes`; includes `Data`, `Link_Control`, `F_BSY`, `Class 2`, `Class 3`. `Table 160 – Stacked Connect-request support Login Bits` defines `word 0, bits 29 - 28` meanings (`00b`, `01b`, `10b`, `11b`). `Table 161 – Sequential delivery support` defines `Nx_Port`/`Fx_Port` combinations and whether sequential delivery is functional. Content types: timer/negotiation, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 6.6.5.4.5 Priority/Preemption
nodes.summary: Defines `Priority/Preemption` negotiation semantics for Fabric Login support, centered on the `Priority/Preemption bit` (`word 0, bit 23`) in `Class 1, 2, 3, and 6`, with `LS_ACC` outcome meanings for `Nx_Port`/`Fabric` agreement. Includes request/response behavior: an `Nx_Port` performing Login with a Fabric requests support by setting the bit to `1`; if `LS_ACC` also sets it to `1`, Priority and Preemption are available for use. Enumerates support codes `0` and `1` and the four `Nx_Port`/`Fx_Port` combinations. `Table 162 – Fabric Login Priority and Preemption Support`: defines the meaning of `Nx_Port` and `Fx_Port` bit combinations (`0/0`, `0/1`, `1/0`, `1/1`). Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, timer/negotiation.

nodes.title: 6.6.5.4.6 Preference
6.6.5.4.6.1 Nx_Port
nodes.summary: Covers `6.6.5.4.6.1 Nx_Port` Preference-bit semantics for Nx_Port-to-Nx_Port Login: `Preference bit` (`word 0, bit 22`) values `0 = non-zero CS_CTL may be tolerated`, `1 = non-zero CS_CTL shall be tolerated`. In Login, an `Nx_Port` sets the bit to one to indicate tolerance for non-zero `CS_CTL`; the peer returns tolerance by setting it in `LS_ACC`. An `Nx_Port` tolerating non-zero `CS_CTL` shall not reject/deprecate a frame solely because `CS_CTL` is non-zero. For `Class 2` and `Class 3`, if set to one, the Nx_Port shall tolerate the `PREF field` in the `CS_CTL` field of the `Frame_Header`; as Sequence Recipient it shall ignore `PREF field` (see `FC-FS-2`). No meaning in `Class 1` and `Class 6`. Content types: negotiation, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions. `Table 162 – Fabric Login Priority and Preemption Support`: defines `Nx_Port`/`Fx_Port` Priority/Preemption combinations.

nodes.title: 6.6.5.4.6.2 Fx_Port
nodes.summary: Section `6.6.5.4.6.2 Fx_Port` covers Fabric Login `Preference bit` (`word 0, bit 22`) behavior for `Fx_Port`: `0 = Normal delivery`, `1 = Preferred delivery functional`. It states how an `Nx_Port` setting bit 22 requests delivery per the `PREF field` in the `CS_CTL field` of the `Frame_Header`, and how an `Fx_Port` reply indicates Fabric handling of `Class 2` and `Class 3` frames; `Class 1` and `Class 6` are not meaningful. Includes `NOTE 17` that preferred delivery may still work even if `Fx_Port` returns zero. `Table 163 – Class 2 and 3 Preference Bit Function`: defines `Nx_Port`/`Fx_Port` bit-22 combinations and meaning. `Table 164 – Relationship between Preferred delivery and sequential delivery`: defines ordering behavior for preferred vs sequential delivery. Content types: `timer/negotiation`, `response/branch handling`, `completion/exception conditions`.

Covers `6.6.5.4.6 Preference`: login negotiation of the `Preference bit` (`word 0, bit 22`) for tolerance of non-zero `CS_CTL` during Nx_Port-to-Nx_Port Login, plus `6.6.5.4.6.2 Fx_Port` values for preferred delivery. `6.6.5.4.6.1 Nx_Port`: `0 = non-zero CS_CTL may be tolerated`, `1 = non-zero CS_CTL shall be tolerated`; requester sets bit to one, peer indicates tolerance by setting it to one in the `LS_ACC`; tolerant `Nx_Port` shall not reject/deprecate a frame solely because `CS_CTL` is non-zero. In `Class 2 and 3`, tolerate `PREF field` in `CS_CTL` of `Frame_Header`; as Sequence Initiator it may specify Preference to the Fabric, as Sequence Recipient the `PREF field` shall be ignored (`FC-FS-2`). No meaning in `Class 1` and `Class 6`. `Fx_Port`: `0 = Normal delivery`, `1 = Preferred delivery functional`. Table `Table 162 – Fabric Login Priority and Preemption Support` defines `Priority/Preemption` support combinations. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, timer/negotiation.

nodes.title: 6.6.5.4.7 DiffServ QoS
6.6.5.4.7.1 N_Port Login
nodes.summary: Covers `6.6.5.4.7.1 N_Port Login` DiffServ QoS negotiation during Login between `Nx_Port` peers. `procedure/flow`, `response/branch handling`, `timer/negotiation`. An `Nx_Port` indicates support by setting the `DiffServ QoS bit` (`word 0, bit 21`) to `1`; the other `Nx_Port` confirms support by setting the same bit to `1` in `LS_ACC`. As `Exchange Originator` and `Exchange Responder`, support means `CS_CTL/Priority Enable bit` (`F_CTL bit 17`) is `0`, so the `DSCP field` of `CS_CTL` (`word 1, bits 29-24`) specifies Differentiated Services QoS policy. `0 = DiffServ QoS not supported`, `1 = DiffServ QoS supported`. Applies only to `Classes 2 and 3`. `Table 165 – DiffServ QoS bit definition`: defines `Nx_Port`/`Fx_Port` bit combinations and availability outcomes.

Covers DiffServ QoS capability signaling and negotiation in `6.6.5.4.7 DiffServ QoS`, specifically `6.6.5.4.7.1 N_Port Login` and `6.6.5.4.8 F_Port Login`. Key anchors: `N_Port Login`, `F_Port Login`, `LS_ACC`, `DiffServ QoS bit` (`word 0, bit 21`), `CS_CTL/Priority Enable bit` (`F_CTL bit 17`), `DSCP field of the CS_CTL field` (`word 1, bits 29-24`), `Classes 2 and 3`, `Nx_Port`, `Fx_Port`, `Exchange Originator`, `Exchange Responder`, `FC-FS-2`. Defines `0 = DiffServ QoS not supported` / `1 = DiffServ QoS supported` and login reply meaning, including when DiffServ QoS is available for use. `Table 165 – DiffServ QoS bit definition`: defines Nx_Port/Fx_Port bit combinations and resulting support/availability outcomes. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, timer/negotiation.

nodes.title: 6.6.5.4.8 F_Port Login
nodes.summary: Section `6.6.5.4.8 F_Port Login` covers `Fabric Login` negotiation of `DiffServ QoS` support between `Nx_Port` and `Fx_Port` using the `DiffServ QoS bit` (`word 0, bit 21`) and `LS_ACC` reply semantics. Core rule: an `Nx_Port` requests support by setting `word 0, bit 21` to `1`; if the responding `Fx_Port` also sets the bit to `1` in `LS_ACC`, `Differentiated Services QoS` is available for use. Includes value meanings `0 = DiffServ QoS not Supported`, `1 = DiffServ QoS Supported`; applies only to `Classes 2 and 3`; references `FC-FS-2`. `Table 165 – DiffServ QoS bit definition`: defines all `Nx_Port`/`Fx_Port` bit combinations (`0/0`, `0/1`, `1/0`, `1/1`) and resulting support/availability outcomes. Content types: timer/negotiation, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

Section 6.6.5.4 Service options covers optional class-of-service features in service parameters, specifically `Intermix Mode` in `word 0, bit 30`, with Class 1/6 applicability and no meaning for Class 2 or Class 3. Anchors: `Intermix Mode`, `Class 1`, `Class 6`, `Class 2`, `Class 3`, `N_Port`, `Nx_Port`, `F_Port`, `Fx_Port`, `Fabric`, `Login`, `LS_ACC`, `F_BSY`, `SOFc1`, `FC-FS-2`. Defines values `0 = Intermix not requested`, `1 = Intermix requested`; describes exclusive connections vs intermixed dedicated connection behavior; includes Fabric Login and N_Port Login result handling, including when Intermix is functional and when Class 1 dedicated connections shall be removed before Class 2/Class 3 transmission. Table 159 – Intermix Mode Support: defines Nx_Port/Fx_Port bit combinations and resulting Intermix support meaning. Content types: response/branch handling, timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 6.6.5.5 Initiator control
6.6.5.5.1 Introduction
nodes.summary: Introduces `6.6.5.5 Initiator control`: Initiator Control Flags specify which protocols, policies, or functions the Sequence Initiator in an `Nx_Port` requests of the recipient or supports as a Sequence initiator. Covers `Initial Process_Associator` (`word 0, bits 13-12`) and `ACK_0 capability` (`word 0, bit 11`). `Table 166 – Initial Process_Associator Bits Definition` defines `00b` not supported, `01b` supported, `10b` reserved, `11b` required (and supported). Notes `Association_Header`, `Process_Associator`, `Responder Process_Associator`, `X_ID interlock`, `Class 1`, `Class 2`, `PLOGI`, `PLOGI LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, and conflict handling when `Responder`/`Originator` support or require `Initial Process_Associator`; in conflict cases `Nx_Ports` are unable to communicate. Also states `ACK_0 incapable`/`ACK_0 capable` for acknowledged class of service Sequences. Content types: response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 6.6.5.5.2 Initial Process_Associator
nodes.summary: Defines `Initial Process_Associator` within `6.6.5.5 Initiator control`: `word 0, bits 13-12` meanings for `PLOGI`/`PLOGI LS_ACC`, support vs required semantics, `Association_Header` use at certain Sequence boundaries, required initial `Process_Associator` value, and dependency that if `Initial Process_Associator` is required or supported then `X_ID interlock` is also required in `Class 1` and `2`. Covers conflict handling via `LS_RJT` when Originator/Responder `Nx_Port` capabilities mismatch, including “Initiator Control bits are in conflict” and inability to communicate. `Table 166 – Initial Process_Associator Bits Definition`: defines encodings `00b` not supported, `01b` supported, `10b` reserved, `11b` required (and supported). Content types: response/branch handling, timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 6.6.5.5.3 ACK_0 capability
nodes.summary: Section `6.6.5.5.3 ACK_0 capability` defines the `ACK_0 capability bit` (`word 0, bit 11`) in `Initiator control` Class Service Parameters: `0 = ACK_0 incapable`, `1 = ACK_0 capable`. Core topic: whether the `Nx_Port` supplying the parameters can support `ACK_0` as a `Sequence Initiator` for acknowledgement of an entire `Sequence` under `Discard` or `Process Exchange Error Policies`. It states that, as a `Sequence Initiator`, an `Nx_Port` receives and processes `ACK frames` in response to `Data frame` transmission, and that `ACK_0` applies to acknowledged class of service `Sequences` (see `FC-FC-2`). Table present: `Table 166 – Initial Process_Associator Bits Definition`, defining `Word 0, bits 13-12` meanings for `Initial Process_Associator`. Content types: timer/negotiation.

nodes.title: 6.6.5.5.4 ACK generation assistance
nodes.summary: Section `6.6.5.5.4 ACK generation assistance` defines the `ACK generation assistance bit (word 0, bit 9)` for Initiator Control: `0 = No ACK generation assistance is provided to Sequence Recipient`, `1 = ACK generation assistance is provided to Sequence Recipient`; usage is specified in `FC-FS-2`. Nearby context on the same page also includes `Table 167 – ACK_0 Support Conditions (Initiator Control)`, which defines when `ACK_0` is supported based on `Nx_Port A Word 0, Bit 11`, `Nx_Port B Word 1, Bit 31`, and `Nx_Port A as Sequence Initiator`; `ACK_0` takes precedence over `ACK_1`. References present: `6.6.5.5.5 Clock synchronization ELS capable`, `word 0, bit 4`, `Clock Synchronization Server` `FFFFF6h`, `CSU ELS`. Content types: negotiation/feature bit definitions, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 6.6.5.5.5 Clock synchronization ELS capable
nodes.summary: Section `6.6.5.5.5 Clock synchronization ELS capable` defines the `Clock synchronization ELS capable bit (word 0, bit 4)` in Initiator Control: `0 = Initiator does not have clock synchronization ELS capability`, `1 = Initiator has clock synchronization ELS capability`. It is only meaningful from the `Clock Synchronization Server` well-known address `FFFFF6h`; `0` means the `Nx_Port` does not support the ELS method of Clock Synchronization, `1` means the `Nx_Port` can generate `CSU ELS frames`; see `FC-FS-2`. Nearby carryover context includes `ACK_0`, `ACK_1`, `ACK generation assistance bit (word 0, bit 9)`, and `Table 167 – ACK_0 Support Conditions (Initiator Control)` showing when `ACK_0` is supported for `Nx_Port A`/`Nx_Port B`. Content types: response/branch handling.

Defines `Initiator Control Flags` for the Sequence Initiator in an `Nx_Port`, covering requested/capable protocols, policies, and functions in Service Parameters. `6.6.5.5.2 Initial Process_Associator` defines `word 0, bits 13-12`: `00b` not supported, `01b` supported, `10b` reserved, `11b` required (and supported); ties to `Association_Header`, `Process_Associator`, `Responder Process_Associator`, `X_ID interlock`, `Class 1`, `Class 2`, `PLOGI`, `PLOGI LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, and conflict handling when Initiator Control bits mismatch and Nx_Ports cannot communicate. `6.6.5.5.3 ACK_0 capability` defines `word 0, bit 11`: `0 = ACK_0 incapable`, `1 = ACK_0 capable`, for acknowledged class Sequences under `Discard` or `Process Exchange Error Policies`. Table `166 – Initial Process_Associator Bits Definition`: defines `word 0, bits 13-12` meanings. Content types: response/branch handling, timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 6.6.5.6 Recipient control
6.6.5.6.1 Introduction
nodes.summary: Section `6.6.5.6.1 Introduction` introduces `Recipient Control Flags`: they specify which protocols, policies, or functions are supported by the `Recipient Initiator function` in the `Nx_Port` supplying `Service Parameters` when acting as a recipient of `Data frames`. Context on `ACK_0` support is included: `Table 167 – ACK_0 Support Conditions (Initiator Control)` defines when `ACK_0` is supported for `Nx_Port A` (`Word 0, Bit 11`) and `Nx_Port B` (`Word 1, Bit 31`) with `Nx_Port A as Sequence Initiator`; only `1/1` yields `ACK_0 supported`, otherwise `ACK_0 not supported`, and `ACK_0` takes precedence over `ACK_1`. Also names `ACK generation assistance` (`word 0, bit 9`), `Clock synchronization ELS capable` (`word 0, bit 4`), `Clock Synchronization Server` `FFFFF6h`, and `CSU ELS frames`. Content types: timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 6.6.5.6.2 ACK_0 capability
nodes.summary: Defines `ACK_0 capability` in Class Service Parameters: `word 1, bit 31`, with values `0 = ACK_0 incapable`, `1 = ACK_0 capable`. Core rule: ACK_0 support for acknowledged class-of-service Sequences as Sequence Recipient requires infinite buffering and ability to transmit `ACK_0` frames in response to Data frames; support applies only when Sequence Initiator capability `Word 0, Bit 11` and Sequence Recipient capability `Word 1, Bit 31` are both `1`. Notes asymmetrical capability per `Nx_Port`; if both bits are `1`, port supports ACK_0 as either Sequence Initiator or Sequence Recipient. `Table 168 – ACK_0 Support Conditions (Recipient Control)` defines the 2-bit support matrix (`ACK_0 supported` / `ACK_0 not supported`). Content types: timer/negotiation, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 6.6.5.6.3 X_ID interlock
nodes.summary: Defines `X_ID interlock` (`word 1, bit 29`) for Class 1 and Class 2 Class Service Parameters: `0 = X_ID interlock not required`, `1 = X_ID interlock required`. Covers the X_ID assignment interlock procedure/flow: in the first Data frame of a Sequence, the Sequence Initiator sets `Recipient X_ID` to `FFFFh`; the Recipient returns its X_ID in the `ACK` frame for that first Data frame; the Sequence Initiator shall not transmit additional frames until the corresponding `ACK` is received; after `ACK` reception it continues using both assigned X_ID values. References `FC-FS-2`. Also includes `Table 168 – ACK_0 Support Conditions (Recipient Control)`, which defines when `ACK_0` is supported from `Word 0, Bit 11` and `Word 1, Bit 31`. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling.

nodes.title: 6.6.5.6.4 Error policy supported
nodes.summary: Defines `Error policy supported` capability bits and adjacent recipient capability fields in word 1: bits `28-27` for missing frame condition handling, bits `25-24` for `Categories per Sequence`, plus `Clock synchronization ELS capable`. `Table 169 – Error Policy Bits Definition` shows `00b` = `Only discard policy supported`, `10b` = `Both discard and process policies supported`, `01b`/`11b` reserved; policy for an Exchange is specified by the `Exchange Originator` per `FC-FS-2`. `Table 170 – Categories per Sequence Bits Definition` shows `00b` = `1 Category/Sequence`, `01b` = `2 Categories/Sequence`, `11b` = `More than 2 Categories/Sequence`, `10b` reserved. Covers `Nx_Port`, `Recipient`, `Sequence Initiator`, `Sequence Recipient`, `N_Port Login`, `Information Categories`, `R_CTL bits 27-24`, `Frame_Header`, `Class of Service`, `FC-4`. Content types: `message/frame format`, `timer/negotiation`, `completion/exception conditions`.

nodes.title: 6.6.5.6.5 Categories per Sequence
nodes.summary: Defines `Categories per Sequence` capability bits for N_Port Login: `word 1, bits 25-24`, indicating recipient support for `1 Category/Sequence`, `2 Categories/Sequence`, or `More than 2 Categories/Sequence` via `Information Categories` (`R_CTL bits 27-24 in the Frame_Header`) within a single `Sequence`. Applies per `Class of Service`; a `Sequence Initiator` must restrict categories based on the `Sequence Recipient's` capability learned during `N_Port Login`. Notes interoperability impact on `FC-4` protocols and preference for single-category compatibility with multi-category support where possible. `Table 170 – Categories per Sequence Bits Definition`: defines encodings `00b`, `01b`, `10b Reserved`, `11b`. Nearby context includes `Table 169 – Error Policy Bits Definition`: defines `word 1, bits 28-27` error-policy support. Content types: timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 6.6.5.6.6 Clock synchronization ELS capable
nodes.summary: Defines capability bits around `6.6.5.6.6 Clock synchronization ELS capable` and nearby word 1 capability fields used during N_Port Login. Covers `Error policy supported` (`word 1, bits 28-27`) with meanings `00b` Only discard policy supported, `10b` Both discard and process policies supported, and `01b`/`11b` Reserved; `Categories per Sequence` (`word 1, bits 25-24`) with `00b` 1 Category/Sequence, `01b` 2 Categories/Sequence, `10b` Reserved, `11b` More than 2 Categories/Sequence; and `Clock synchronization ELS capable` values `0` = no capability, `1` = capability. `Table 169 – Error Policy Bits Definition`: defines Error policy supported bit meanings. `Table 170 – Categories per Sequence Bits Definition`: defines Categories per Sequence bit meanings. Content types: message/frame format, timer/negotiation, response/branch handling.

Section `6.6.5.6 Recipient control` introduces `6.6.5.6.1 Introduction`: the `Recipient Control Flags` specify which protocols, policies, or functions are supported by the `Recipient Initiator` function in an `Nx_Port` supplying `Service Parameters` when acting as a recipient of `Data frames`. Context carried into this page includes `ACK_0` support rules and asymmetry between `Sequence Initiator` and `Sequence Recipient`, with `ACK_0` taking precedence over `ACK_1`; capability bits referenced are `word 0, bit 11`, `word 1, bit 31`, `word 0, bit 9`, and `word 0, bit 4`, plus `FFFFF6h` and `FC-FS-2`. `Table 167 – ACK_0 Support Conditions (Initiator Control)` defines when `ACK_0` is supported based on `Nx_Port A`/`Nx_Port B` bit settings. Content types: timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 6.6.5.7 Receive Data_Field Size
nodes.summary: 6.6.5.7 `Receive Data_Field Size`: defines `Receive Data_Field Size` (word 1, bits 11-0) in Service Parameters, specifying the largest `Data_Field Size` for a frame that may be received by the `Nx_Port` as a `Sequence Recipient` (see `FC-FS-2`). Valid values are 256 to 2 112, must be multiples of four bytes, and an `Nx_Port` shall support at least 256 bytes. For `Class 1` and `Class 6`, this is the post-connection receive limit; connect-request size is in `Buffer-to-Buffer Receive Data_Field size` in `Common Service Parameters` (see `6.6.2.6`). For `Class 2` and `Class 3`, it shall be equal to or less than that common-parameter value. Content types: timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions. Tables/Figures: none.

nodes.title: 6.6.5.8 Concurrent Sequences
nodes.summary: Defines `Concurrent Sequences` in Service Parameters: `word 2, bits 23-16` specify the number of `Sequence Status Blocks` available in the `Nx_Port` as `Sequence Recipient` for tracking a `Sequence`. Allowable values: `01h - FFh`; `00` reserved; maximum `255` per `Nx_Port` as Recipient across all classes. Cross-reference limit from `Common Service Parameter field` (see `6.6.2.7`). `NOTE 18`: maximum open Sequences between two `Nx_Ports` equals the sum of `Concurrent Sequences` reported in `PLOGI` and `LS_ACC` of the `PLOGI`. Class behavior: in `Class 1` and `6`, `SEQ_ID` ranges `0` to `L` inclusive, where `L` is the `Concurrent Sequence` field; in `Class 2` and `3`, `SEQ_ID` ranges `0` to `255`; in `Class 2`, `P_BSY` may be returned if resources unavailable. Content types: response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 6.6.5.9 end-to-end Credit
nodes.summary: Defines `Nx_Port End-to-end Credit` in `6.6.5.9 end-to-end Credit`: `word 2, bits 14-0` gives the maximum number of `Data` frames an `Nx_Port` may transmit without receiving accompanying `ACK` or `Link_Response` frames. Covers minimum value `one`, relation to available buffers for holding `Data_Field` and processing it, and applicability limits: not applicable to `Class 3` because `ACK` frames are not used. Integrity constraint: `Nx_Port End-to-end Credit` is a `15-bit` field while `SEQ_CNT` is a `16-bit` field, ensuring credit never exceeds one-half of maximum `SEQ_CNT`; `Bit 15` shall be `zero`. Content types: completion/exception conditions (minimum, not-applicable case, bit constraint). No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 6.6.5.10 Open Sequences per Exchange
nodes.summary: Defines `Open Sequences per Exchange` semantics for `word 3, bits 23 - 16`: maximum Sequences open at the Recipient at one time between a pair of `Nx_Ports` for one `Exchange`; value `X+2` specifies maintained `Sequence Status` instances in the `Exchange Status Block (ESB)`; value `X` limits link facility resources for error detection and recovery; `X` is specified in `bits 23-16` (see `FC-FS-2`). Includes `NOTE 19` on retaining at least one “good” Sequence and race/timing margin. Valid only for `PLOGI` and `PLOGI LS_ACC`. Content types: timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions. Adjacent carry-over anchors mentioned: `Nx_Port End-to-end Credit` valid values `1` to `32767`, `0` reserved; next section starts `CR_TOV` (`word 3`, `1 ms` increments, `0000000Ah`, `Class 1`, `Class 6`, `FLOGI LS_ACC`). No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 6.6.5.11 CR_TOV
nodes.summary: Covers `CR_TOV` encoding/meaning in `6.6.5.11 CR_TOV`: `CR_TOV` value (`word 3`) is a count of `1 ms` increments; example `0000000Ah` = `10 ms`. Scope/applicability: `CR_TOV` usage references `FC-FS-2`; applicable to `Class 1` and `6`, not applicable to other Classes; only meaningful in `FLOGI LS_ACC`. Nearby context on the same page also names `Nx_Port End-to-end Credit`, `Open Sequences per Exchange`, `Sequence Status`, `Exchange Status Block`, `PLOGI`, and `PLOGI LS_ACC`, but this section itself is specifically about `CR_TOV`. Content types: `timer/negotiation`. No tables. No figures.

Section `6.6.5 Class Service Parameters` covers applicability and layout of `Class Service Parameters` for `N_Port` and `Fabric Login`, including `PLOGI`, `FLOGI`, `PLOGI LS_ACC`, and `FLOGI LS_ACC`. `6.6.5.1 Applicability` states `Class 1` and `Class 6 Service Parameters` occupy words `9-12`, `Class 2 Service Parameters` words `13-16`, and `Class 3 Service Parameters` words `17-20`; word numbering is relative to the specific class service parameters field, and `Default Login Value` applies prior to a successful `Login`. `Table 155 – Class Service Parameters Applicability` defines parameter applicability by class/message and lists fields including `Class Validity`, `Service Options`, `Intermix Mode`, `Stacked Connect-Requests`, `Sequential delivery`, `Simplex dedicated connection - obsolete`, `Camp-On - obsolete`, and `Buffered Class 1 - obsolete`. Content types: `message/frame format`, `timer/negotiation`.

nodes.title: 6.6.6 Vendor Version Level
nodes.summary: Core topic: `6.6.6 Vendor Version Level` defines the `Vendor Version Level field` (`words 25-28`) as vendor-specific information and states when it is valid. Validity is controlled by the `Valid Version Level bit` in the `Common Service Parameters field` (`word 1, bit 29`): if set to one, the `Vendor Version Level field` contains valid information; if set to zero, the field is not meaningful. Nearby anchors on the page: `CR_TOV`, `FLOGI LS_ACC`, `Services Availability`, `Multicast Server`, `Clock Synchronization Server`, `FFFFF5h`, `FFFFF6h`. Content types: response/branch handling (bit set to one/zero determines interpretation). Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 6.6.7 Services Availability
6.6.7.1 Introduction
nodes.summary: Section `6.6.7.1 Introduction` indexes the `Services Availability` field: it returns the Fabric’s ability to route to defined well-known addresses and is meaningful only for `FLOGI LS_ACC`. Exact anchors: `Services Availability`, `FLOGI LS_ACC`, `word 30`, `bits 10 - 3`, `Word 29`, `bits 31 - 11`, `bits 2 - 0`, `FC-FS-2`. Scope/constraints: only `bits 10 - 3` of `word 30` are meaningful; `Word 29` and `bits 31 - 11 and 2 - 0 of word 30` are reserved. Nearby routed-address detail introduced by this section’s context: `Multicast Server` at `word 30, bit 10`, identifier `FFFFF5h`; `Clock Synchronization Server` at `word 30, bit 9`, identifier `FFFFF6h`. Content types: `message/frame format`, `response/branch handling` (meaningful only for `FLOGI LS_ACC`).

nodes.title: 6.6.7.2 Multicast Server
nodes.summary: Defines the `Services Availability` indication for `6.6.7.2 Multicast Server`: the `Multicast Server bit` at `word 30, bit 10` in `FLOGI LS_ACC`. Core meaning: when set to one, the Fabric supports routing to the well-known `Multicast Server` address identifier `FFFFF5h`; when set to zero, the Fabric does not support routing to that identifier. References `FC-FS-2` for the server specification. Relevant exact anchors: `Services Availability`, `Multicast Server`, `Multicast Server bit`, `word 30, bit 10`, `FLOGI LS_ACC`, `FFFFF5h`, `FC-FS-2`. Content types: response/branch handling (bit value `one` vs `zero` support indication); message/frame context (`FLOGI LS_ACC`). No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 6.6.7.3 Clock Synchronization Server
nodes.summary: Core topic: `6.6.7.3 Clock Synchronization Server` defines the `Clock Synchronization Server bit` in `word 30, bit 9` within `Services Availability`, meaningful for `FLOGI LS_ACC`, indicating whether the Fabric supports routing to the well-known `Clock Synchronization Server` address identifier `FFFFF6h`. If set to one, routing to `FFFFF6h` is supported; if set to zero, routing is not supported. Exact anchors: `Clock Synchronization Server bit`, `word 30, bit 9`, `FLOGI LS_ACC`, `Clock Synchronization Server`, `FFFFF6h`, `FC-FS-2`. Content types: response/branch handling (bit value `one` vs `zero` support indication). Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 6.6.7.4 Security Key Distribution Server
nodes.summary: Defines the `Security Key Distribution Server` capability bit in Fabric service capability signaling: `word 30, bit 8` indicates whether the Fabric supports routing to the well-known Key Distribution Server address identifier `FFFFF7h`; set to one = supported, set to zero = not supported. Core anchor is the `Security Key Distribution Server bit`; nearby continuity text also names `Alias Server bit` (`word 30, bit 7`, `FFFFF8h`), `Management Server bit` (`word 30, bit 5`, `FFFFFAh`), `Time Server bit` (`word 30, bit 4`, `FFFFFBh`), `Directory Server bit` (`word 30, bit 3`, `FFFFFCh`), plus `Login request`, `LS_RJT`, `LS_ACC`, `Payload Bit`, `Login Extension Data Length`, reason code `Logical error`, and reason code explanations `Invalid Payload length` and `Login Extension not supported`. Content type: response/branch handling.

nodes.title: 6.6.7.5 Alias Server
nodes.summary: Core topic: `6.6.7.5 Alias Server` capability bit semantics for Fabric routing support to the well-known Alias Server address identifier. Defines that the `Alias Server bit` at `word 30, bit 7` indicates whether the Fabric supports routing to `FFFFF8h`: set to one = supports routing; set to zero = does not support routing. Nearby anchors appearing in the provided text: `Security Key Distribution Server bit`, `word 30, bit 8`, `FFFFF7h`; `Management Server bit`, `word 30, bit 5`, `FFFFFAh`; `Time Server bit`, `word 30, bit 4`, `FFFFFBh`; `Directory Server bit`, `word 30, bit 3`, `FFFFFCh`. Content types: response/branch handling (bit set to one/zero meaning), completion/exception conditions absent, procedure/flow absent, message/frame format absent, state machine absent, timer/negotiation absent.

nodes.title: 6.6.7.6 Management Server
nodes.summary: Core topic: `6.6.7.6 Management Server` defines the meaning of the `Management Server bit` in `word 30, bit 5` as a fabric-routing capability indicator. If set to one, the Fabric supports routing to the well-known `Management Server` address identifier `FFFFFAh`; if set to zero, the Fabric does not support routing to `FFFFFAh`. References `FC-GS-5` for the server specification. Exact anchors: `Management Server bit`, `word 30, bit 5`, `FFFFFAh`, `FC-GS-5`. Content types: response/branch handling (bit value `one` vs `zero` meaning supported vs not supported); completion/exception conditions absent; procedure/flow absent; message/frame format absent; state machine absent; timer/negotiation absent. Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 6.6.7.7 Time Server
nodes.summary: Core topic: `6.6.7.7 Time Server` defines the meaning of the `Time Server bit` in `word 30, bit 4` as a Fabric capability indicator for routing to the `well-known Time Server address identifier` `FFFFFBh`. When set to one, the Fabric supports routing to `FFFFFBh`; when set to zero, it does not support routing to that identifier. Key anchors: `Time Server bit`, `word 30, bit 4`, `well-known Time Server address identifier`, `FFFFFBh`. Content types: response/branch handling only in the sense of set-to-one vs set-to-zero interpretation; no procedure/flow, message/frame format, state machine, timer/negotiation, or completion/exception conditions. Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 6.6.7.8 Directory Server
nodes.summary: Defines the `Directory Server` capability bit in Fabric service advertisement: `word 30, bit 3` indicates whether the Fabric supports routing to the well-known `Directory Server` address identifier `FFFFFCh`; `one` = supports routing, `zero` = does not support routing. References `FC-GS-5` for the server specification. Contextually adjacent anchors on the same page include `Security Key Distribution Server` (`word 30, bit 8`, `FFFFF7h`), `Alias Server` (`word 30, bit 7`, `FFFFF8h`), `Management Server` (`word 30, bit 5`, `FFFFFAh`), and `Time Server` (`word 30, bit 4`, `FFFFFBh`). Content types: identifier/bit definition, routing support indication; no procedure/flow, no message/frame format, no response/branch handling, no state machine, no timer/negotiation, no completion/exception conditions.

Defines `Services Availability` in `FLOGI LS_ACC`: fabric routing availability to well-known addresses, meaningful only for `FLOGI LS_ACC`. `6.6.7.1 Introduction` says only `word 30, bits 10 - 3` are meaningful; `word 29` and `word 30 bits 31 - 11 and 2 - 0` are reserved. `6.6.7.2 Multicast Server`: `word 30, bit 10`; `1` means routing supported to well-known `Multicast Server` address identifier `FFFFF5h`, `0` means not supported. `6.6.7.3 Clock Synchronization Server`: `word 30, bit 9`; `1` means routing supported to `Clock Synchronization Server` address identifier `FFFFF6h`, `0` means not supported. Content types: message/frame format, response/branch handling. Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 6.6.8 Login Extension
6.6.8.1 General
nodes.summary: Login Extension general error handling and interoperability requirements for unsupported `Login request` / `LS_ACC reply Sequence` payload or extension usage. Covers rejection behavior when `Payload Bit` is set to one and unsupported, when `Login Extension Data Length field` is non-zero and unsupported, and when an unsupported `page code` is received. Required responses: reject with `LS_RJT`, `reason code` = `Logical error`, with `reason code explanation` = `Invalid Payload length` or `Login Extension not supported`; for unsupported `LS_ACC reply Sequence` with `Payload Bit` set to one, perform explicit `Logout`. Content types: response/branch handling; completion/exception conditions. Named identifiers/codes present: `LS_RJT`, `LS_ACC`, `Payload Bit`, `Login Extension Data Length field`, `page code`, `Logical error`, `Invalid Payload length`, `Login Extension not supported`, `Logout`. No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 6.6.8.2 Login Extension Data Length
nodes.summary: Covers `6.6.8.2 Login Extension Data Length`: semantics of the `Login Extension Data Length` field (`word 31`) in `FLOGI/PLOGI/ACC` payloads, when a `Login Extension` follows the normal payload, and the rule that the `Payload Bit` (see `6.6.2.4.19`) shall be `one` if this field is non-zero. Also introduces `6.6.8.3 Login Extension format`: `Login Extension Page` sequence length equals `Login Extension Length field`; first page starts at `word 64`. Message/frame format; completion/exception conditions. `Table 171 – Login Extension Page format`: defines page layout with `Page Length (n)`, `Reserved`, `Page Code`, `Page Code Specific Data`. `Table 172 – Page Code Definitions`: defines codes `00h - EFh`, `F0h`, `F1h - FFh`. `Table 173 – Vendor Specific Page format`: shows `Vendor Specific Page`, `Page Code (F0h)`, `Vendor Identification Data`, `Vendor Specific Data`.

nodes.title: 6.6.8.3 Login Extension format
nodes.summary: Defines the `Login Extension` field structure for `FLOGI/PLOGI/ACC payload`: a sequence of zero or more `Login Extension Pages`, whose total length in words equals the `Login Extension Length` field; `Word 0` of the first page, if present, is `word 64` of the `FLOGI/PLOGI/ACC payload`. `Login Extension Page` format uses `Page Length (n)`, `Reserved`, `Page Code`, and `Page Code Specific Data`; `Page Length Field` includes the whole page. `Page Code Field` values: `00h - EFh Reserved`, `F0h Vendor Specific`, `F1h - FFh Reserved`. `Vendor Specific Page` format includes `Vendor Identification Data` and `Vendor Specific Data`. Tables: `Table 171 – Login Extension Page format` (generic page layout); `Table 172 – Page Code Definitions` (code meanings); `Table 173 – Vendor Specific Page format` (vendor-specific layout). Content types: message/frame format.

Login Extension general behavior for `Login` / `LS_ACC` capability mismatches and rejection rules. Covers `procedure/flow`, `response/branch handling`, and `completion/exception conditions` for ports handling a `Login request` with `Payload Bit` set to one, a non-zero `Login Extension Data Length` field, or an unsupported `page code`. Required outcomes: reject with `LS_RJT` when unsupported; reason code `"Logical error"` with reason code explanation `"Invalid Payload length"` if `Payload Bit` set to one is unsupported, or `"Login Extension not supported"` if non-zero `Login Extension Data Length` or unsupported `page code` is received. If a port does not support an `LS_ACC reply Sequence` with `Payload Bit` set to one, it shall perform an explicit `Logout` with the replying port. No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 6.6.9 Clock Synchronization Quality of Service
6.6.9.1 N_Port Login
6.6.9.1.1 Applicability
nodes.summary: Applicability of `Clock Synchronization Quality of Service (QoS)` in `N_Port Login`: the `Clock Synchronization QoS` field in `PLOGI ELS` or `LS_ACC` (`words 62-63`) is only meaningful when sent to or received from the `Clock Synchronization Server` (`FFFFF6h`). Meaningful only if `Clock Synchronization Primitive Capable` (`word 1, bit 20`) = 1 or `Clock Synchronization ELS capable` in an `N_Port Class Service Parameter Recipient control field` (`word 1, bit 19`) = 1; otherwise field shall be zero (`FC-FS-2`). Defines `CS_QoS_Request`, `CS_Accuracy`, `CS_Implemented_MS`, `CS_Implemented_LS`, `CS_Update_Period`. `Table 174 – N_Port Clock Synchronization QoS`: field layout/format. `Table 175 – FLOGI/PLOGI CS_QoS_Request`: request values `00h`, `01h`, `02h – FFh Reserved` and whether QoS parameter fields are meaningful. Content types: message/frame format, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 6.6.9.1.2 CS_QoS_Request
nodes.summary: Section `6.6.9.1.2 CS_QoS_Request` defines the `CS_QoS_Request` field in `N_Port Login` Clock Synchronization QoS for `PLOGI` and `FLOGI Request`, and states `PLOGI LS_ACC` handling. Core rule: in `Word 62, Bits 31-24`, `00h` means `CS_Accuracy`, `CS_Implemented_MSB`, `CS_Implemented_LSB`, and `CS_Update_Period` are not meaningful in the `FLOGI/PLOGI Request`; `01h` means those fields contain the requested Quality of Service Parameters; `02h – FFh` are `Reserved`. `PLOGI LS_ACC` makes this field not meaningful and it shall be set to zero. Table `175 – FLOGI/PLOGI CS_QoS_Request` defines the value-to-meaning mapping for `CS_QoS_Request`. Content types: `message/frame format`, `response/branch handling`, `completion/exception conditions`.

nodes.title: 6.6.9.1.3 CS_Accuracy (Mantissa and Exponent)
nodes.summary: Section `6.6.9.1.3 CS_Accuracy (Mantissa and Exponent)` defines the `CS_Accuracy` field within `N_Port Clock Synchronization QoS` for `PLOGI ELS`, `LS_ACC`, and `FLOGI/PLOGI Request` context, specifically splitting `word 62` into `CS_Accuracy_Mantissa` (`bits 23-21`) and `CS_Accuracy_Exponent` (`bits 20-16`). Closely tied anchors: `Clock Synchronization Server (FFFFF6h)`, `CS_QoS_Request`, `CS_Implemented_MSB`, `CS_Implemented_LSB`, `CS_Update_Period`, `00h`, `01h`, `02h – FFh`, `table 174`, `table 175 – FLOGI/PLOGI CS_QoS_Request` (defines when `CS_Accuracy`, `CS_Implemented_MSB`, `CS_Implemented_LSB`, and `CS_Update_Period` are meaningful or zero/requested). Content types: message/frame format, timer/negotiation, response/branch handling.

nodes.title: 6.6.9.1.4 Clock Synchronization Implemented MSB
nodes.summary: Section `6.6.9.1.4 Clock Synchronization Implemented MSB` defines the `Clock Synchronization Implemented MSB` field in `word 62, bits 13 - 8` as a 6-bit value, with `bits 15-14` reserved and set to zero. Core topic: how this field is used with the `Clock Synchronization Server (FFFFF6h)` during `PLOGI` to request or report the most significant implemented bit position in the 64-bit `Clock Count` field of the `CSU ELS Payload`. Includes `NOTE 20`: `Clock Count` wraps to zero on overflow from the `Clock Synchronization Implemented MSB`; example value `110111b` means the MSB of byte 1 is the highest meaningful bit. Content types: message/frame format, negotiation, completion/exception conditions. No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 6.6.9.1.5 Clock Synchronization Implemented LSB
nodes.summary: Defines `Clock Synchronization Implemented LSB` for `word 62, bits 13 - 8` as a `6-bit` field, with `Word 62, bits 7-6` reserved and required to be zero. Scope is the field-level definition of `Clock Synchronization Implemented LSB`; nearby context also names `Fabric`, `FLOGI`, `Clock Synchronization Server (FFFFF6h)`, `CS_Accuracy`, `CS_Accuracy_Mantissa`, `CS_Accuracy_Exponent`, `T_reference`, `PLOGI`, `Clock Synchronization Implemented MSB`, `Clock Count field`, `CSU ELS Payload`, and `NOTE 20`, but this section text itself only states the `Clock Synchronization Implemented LSB` field size/bit placement and reserved bits requirement. Content types: `message/frame format` (field layout/bit assignment). Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 6.6.9.1.6 Clock Synchronization Update Period
nodes.summary: Covers `6.6.9.1.6 Clock Synchronization Update Period` and start of `6.6.9.2 Fabric Login`: semantics of `Clock Synchronization Update Period` (word 63) when sent to or received from the `Clock Synchronization Server` `FFFFF6h`, in `PLOGI`, and the rule that it is not meaningful for `FLOGI` and shall be zero. Also includes preceding `Clock Count` least-significant-bit-position meaning for the `CSU ELS Payload`. For `Fabric Login`, defines applicability of `Clock Synchronization Quality of Service` based on `Word 1, bit 20 - Clock Synchronization Primitive Capable` and `Word 1, bit 19 - Clock Synchronization ELS capable`, else zero per `FC-FS-2`. `Table 176 – Fx_Port Clock Synchronization QoS`: shows `Fx_Port Clock Synchronization Quality of Service` layout/fields (`CS_Transfer_Accuracy`, `CS_Implemented_MSB`, `CS_Implemented_LSB`, reserved words 62-63). Content types: message/frame format, response/branch handling, timer/negotiation.

Core topic: `6.6.9.1 N_Port Login` defines `Clock Synchronization Quality of Service` usage in `PLOGI ELS` / `LS_ACC` (words `62-63`) for `N_Port Login`, including applicability to the `Clock Synchronization Server` (`FFFFF6h`) and the `Clock Synchronization Primitive Capable` bit / `Clock Synchronization ELS capable` bit gating whether the field is meaningful; otherwise it shall be `zero` (`FC-FS-2`). Field anchors: `CS_QoS_Request`, `CS_Accuracy`, `CS_Implemented_MS`, `CS_Implemented_LS`, `CS_Update_Period`, `CS_Accuracy_Mantissa`, `CS_Accuracy_Exponent`. `PLOGI`, `FLOGI Request`, and `PLOGI LS_ACC` handling: `00h` = other QoS fields not meaningful; `01h` = requested Quality of Service Parameters; `02h–FFh` reserved. Table `174 – N_Port Clock Synchronization QoS`: defines word/bit layout of the QoS field. Table `175 – FLOGI/PLOGI CS_QoS_Request`: defines request code meanings. Table `173 – Vendor Specific Page format`: shows `Vendor Identification field`, `Vendor Specific Data field`, `Page Code (F0h)` format. Content types: message/frame format, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 6.6.9.2 Fabric Login
6.6.9.2.1 Applicability
nodes.summary: Defines applicability of the `Clock Synchronization Quality of Service` field for `6.6.9.2 Fabric Login`: meaningful only when `Word 1, bit 20 - Clock Synchronization Primitive Capable` in the `Common features field` = 1, or `Word 1, bit 19 - Clock Synchronization ELS capable` in the `Recipient control field` = 1; otherwise the field shall be set to zero (`see FC-FS-2`). Covers the `Fx_Port Clock Synchronization Quality of Service field` layout reference and links forward to `6.6.9.2.2 CS_Transfer_Accuracy`. `Table 176 – Fx_Port Clock Synchronization QoS`: shows word/bit allocation for `word 62`, `word 63`, `CS_Transfer_Accuracy`, `CS_Implemented_MSB`, `CS_Implemented_LSB`, and `Reserved`. Content types: message/frame format, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 6.6.9.2.2 CS_Transfer_Accuracy
nodes.summary: Section `6.6.9.2.2 CS_Transfer_Accuracy` defines the `CS_Transfer_Accuracy` field for `Fabric Login`, specifically `CS_Transfer_Accuracy_Mantissa` (`word 62, bits 23-21`) and `CS_Transfer_Accuracy_Exponent` (`word 62, bits 20-16`). Core topic: Fabric-maintained clock-sync transfer accuracy passed from the `Clock Synchronization Server` to clients, expressed as accuracy bounds around `(T_server + T_fabric_delay)` using `(0.5 + CS_Accuracy_Mantissa * 2-4)* 2 (CS_Accuracy_Exponent-30)`. Includes surrounding applicability/QoS context: `Clock Synchronization Quality of Service`, `Word 1, bit 20 - Clock Synchronization Primitive Capable`, `Word 1, bit 19 - Clock Synchronization ELS capable`, `FC-FS-2`. Table `176 – Fx_Port Clock Synchronization QoS` shows the `Fx_Port Clock Synchronization QoS` layout, including `CS_Transfer_Accuracy`, `CS_Implemented_MSB`, `CS_Implemented_LSB`. Content types: message/frame format.

nodes.title: 6.6.9.2.3 Clock Synchronization Implemented MSB
nodes.summary: Core topic: `6.6.9.2.3 Clock Synchronization Implemented MSB` defines the `Clock Synchronization Implemented MSB` field in `word 62, bits 13 - 8` as a 6-bit indicator of the highest meaningful bit position in the 64-bit `Clock Count` field; `Word 62, bits 15-14` are reserved and shall be zero. Scope note: this describes Fabric transfer capability for clock synchronization values received from the `Clock Synchronization Server` (`FFFFF6h`) to clients, not server capability. Nearby context includes accuracy terms `T_server`, `T_fabric_delay`, `CS_Accuracy_Mantissa`, `CS_Accuracy_Exponent`, and examples using `CS_Transfer_Accuracy Mantissa`/Exponent. Also references adjacent field `Clock Synchronization Implemented LSB` in `word 62, bits 5 - 0`, with `Word 62, bits 7-6` reserved. Content types: message/frame format, completion/exception conditions. Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 6.6.9.2.4 Word 0, Bits 7-0 Clock Synchronization Implemented LSB
nodes.display_title: 6.6.9.2.4 Word 0, Bits 7-0 Clock Synchronization Implemented LSB
nodes.raw_title: Word 0, Bits 7-0 Clock Synchronization Implemented LSB
nodes.full_title: 6.6.9.2.4 Word 0, Bits 7-0 Clock Synchronization Implemented LSB
nodes.summary: Defines `Clock Synchronization Implemented LSB` for `word 62, bits 5 - 0`: a 6-bit field giving the least significant bit position in the 64-bit `Clock Count` field that contains meaningful information; example `001000b` means the LSB of byte 6 is the lowest meaningful bit. Also states `Word 62, bits 7-6` are reserved and shall be set to zero. Scope/capability note: this field describes Fabric transfer capability for a clock synchronization value received from the `Clock Synchronization Server` (`FFFFF6h`) to clients, not the server’s own capability. Named related identifier in surrounding text: `Clock Synchronization Implemented MSB`. Content types: message/frame format, completion/exception conditions (reserved bits set to zero). Tables: none. Figures: none.

Fabric Login clock-synchronization QoS for `Fx_Port` during `PLOGI`/`FLOGI`, tied to Clock Synchronization Server `FFFFF6h`. Applicability: `Clock Synchronization Quality of Service` is meaningful only if `Word 1, bit 20 - Clock Synchronization Primitive Capable` in the Common features field or `Word 1, bit 19 - Clock Synchronization ELS capable` in the Recipient control field is `1`; otherwise it shall be `0` (see `FC-FS-2`). Defines `Fx_Port Clock Synchronization QoS` layout and `CS_Transfer_Accuracy` semantics, including `CS_Transfer_Accuracy_Mantissa` (`word 62, bits 23-21`) and `CS_Transfer_Accuracy_Exponent` (`word 62, bits 20-16`), plus `CS_Implemented_MSB`/`CS_Implemented_LSB`. `Table 176 – Fx_Port Clock Synchronization QoS`: shows words `62`/`63` field mapping. Content types: message/frame format, timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions.

Covers `6.6.9 Clock Synchronization Quality of Service` for `PLOGI ELS`, `LS_ACC`, and `FLOGI` `N_Port Login`, specifically Clock Synchronization QoS in `words 62-63` when communicating with Clock Synchronization Server `FFFFF6h`. Defines applicability of `Clock Synchronization Quality of Service (QoS)` based on `Clock Synchronization Primitive Capable` (word 1, bit 20) or `Clock Synchronization ELS capable` (word 1, bit 19); otherwise field shall be zero per `FC-FS-2`. Names and semantics for `CS_QoS_Request`, `CS_Accuracy`, `CS_Implemented_MS`, `CS_Implemented_LS`, `CS_Update_Period`, plus `CS_Accuracy_Mantissa` and `CS_Accuracy_Exponent`. `Table 174 – N_Port Clock Synchronization QoS`: field layout/format. `Table 175 – FLOGI/PLOGI CS_QoS_Request`: request code meanings for `00h`, `01h`, `02h – FFh Reserved`. Content types: message/frame format, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

6.6 Service Parameters: defines the Service Parameters context for Login-related payloads, specifically the `ELS` and `LS_ACC` Payload formats and parameter applicability for `PLOGI`, `FLOGI`, `PLOGI LS_ACC`, and `FLOGI LS_ACC`. `Table 149` defines the Payload format for the `FLOGI` and `PLOGI ELSs` and the `LS_ACCs`. States that there are no separate `Class 6` login Service Parameters; `Class 1 service parameters` are used instead. Includes `NOTE 13` that the Link Service may further limit values supplied during Login as specified by individual Upper Level Protocols. Content types: `message/frame format`, `timer/negotiation` (service-parameter applicability/limits). Figures: none. Tables: `Table 149` — Payload format definition for `FLOGI`/`PLOGI` and `LS_ACC`.

structure.summary: Covers Login and Service Parameters introduction: how an `Nx_Port` establishes its operating environment with a Fabric or peer `Nx_Ports`, including long-lived Login scope, concurrency limits, and lack of one-to-one relationship with Class 1 dedicated connections. Describes explicit vs implicit Login, with exact anchors `FLOGI`, `PLOGI`, `D_ID`, `S_ID`, `ELS`, `LS_ACC`, `Service Parameters`, `Payload`, `N_Port_ID`, and point-to-point topology. Explicit Login uses a new Exchange carrying initiator `Service Parameters`; `LS_ACC` returns responder parameters. Implicit Login provides equivalent functionality by unspecified methods; Implicit Fabric Login does not require support for `FLOGI` request/reply. Also lists how `Nx_Ports` obtain native `N_Port_ID` and collect Address Identifiers. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, timer/negotiation. Tables: none. Figures: none.

structure.title: 7 Process Login/Logout

nodes.title: 7.1 Process Login
7.1.1 Introduction
nodes.summary: Core topic: Process Login (PRLI) introduction—PRLI ELS request establishes the operating environment between related process groups/images at originating/responding `Nx_Port`; defines `Implicit Process Login` vs `Explicit Process Login`, `image`, `image pair`, `Process_Associator`, `D_ID`, `S_ID`, `Responder Process_Associator`, `Originator Process_Associator`, relation to `N_Port Login`, `PRLO`, `LS_ACC`, `ABTS`, and when PRLI is used/left in effect. Covers combinations: `Nx_Port`↔`Nx_Port`, `Nx_Port`↔image, image↔image; Service Parameters exchange, modifying established image pairs, and failure/non-impact rules. Figures: `Figure 4 – Image/Group of Related Processes` shows an image/group behind an `Nx_Port`; `Figure 5 – Image pairs` shows paired images across a link. Tables present only as extracted fragments: `table_0189` (`Table fragment on page 197`) and `table_0191` (`Table fragment on page 198`), showing figure/table fragment content. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 7.1.2 PRLI/PRLO Relationships
7.1.2.1 Introduction
nodes.summary: Core topic: `7.1.2.1 Introduction` to `7.1.2 PRLI/PRLO Relationships`; defines that `Process_Associator (PA)` images may exist in multiple relationships, and that any such relationships may be established as a default condition using mechanisms not specified by this standard. Exact anchors: `PRLI`, `PRLO`, `Process_Associator (PA)`, `PA images`, `Nx_Port`. This section is a scope/setup note for the PA relationship model, not a detailed rule set; it introduces the relationship concept before cases such as `PA not supported` and `PA required by originator, supported by responder`. Content types present: timer/negotiation (relationship/default establishment context). Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 7.1.2.2 PA not supported
nodes.summary: Section `7.1.2.2 PA not supported` defines the PRLI/PRLO relationship when `Process_Associator (PA)` support is absent on one or both ends. Core rule: if either `Originator` or `Responder` does not support `PAs`, then no valid `PA` is specified in any frame of any exchange. It states that `PRLI`/`PRLO` should allow control over enabling/disabling the specified services for the entire `Nx_Port`. Nearby context on `PRLI request` establishment and retries mentions `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `P_BSY`, `F_BSY`, `P_RJT`, `F_RJT`, `LOGO ELS Sequence`, reason code `Unable to perform command request`, and reason code explanation `N_Port Login required`, but this subsection itself is specifically the no-PA relationship case. Content types: timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 7.1.2.3 PA required by originator, supported by responder
nodes.display_title: 7.1.2.3 PA required by originator, supported by responder
nodes.raw_title: PA required by originator, supported by responder
nodes.full_title: 7.1.2.3 PA required by originator, supported by responder
nodes.summary: Section `7.1.2.3 PA required by originator, supported by responder`: PRLI/PRLO relationship where the `Originator` requires `PA` and the `Responder` supports it. Core rules: `Originator` communicates only when a valid `PA` is included in the initial `Association_Header`; `Responder` provides a final `Association_Header` and other `Association_Headers` as required. `PA` establishes routing to a particular `Originator` process (or related group); no relationship exists between `PAs` on different `Nx_Port`s of the same host. `PRLI` request has valid `Originator PA` values and invalid `Responder PA` values, informing `Responder` of `Originator` requirements/capabilities for each `TYPE` of `FC-4`. `PRLI`/`PRLO` may control access per `Originator Process` to `Responder Nx_Port`. Communication with unestablished image pairs is not allowed. Content types: message/frame format, negotiation, completion/exception conditions. Tables: none. Figures: none.

nodes.title: 7.1.2.4 PA required by responder, supported by originator
nodes.display_title: 7.1.2.4 PA required by responder, supported by originator
nodes.raw_title: PA required by responder, supported by originator
nodes.full_title: 7.1.2.4 PA required by responder, supported by originator
nodes.summary: Covers PA usage when the Responder requires PAs and the Originator supports them: valid `Responder PA` must appear in the initial `Association_Header`; the Responder returns a final `Association_Header` and other `Association_Headers` as required; PA routes to a specific Responder process and is per `Nx_Port`. `PRLI` request pages carry valid `Responder PA` and invalid `Originator PA`; `Responder PA` may be learned via informative `PRLI`. `PRLI`/`PRLO` may enable or disable access of each Responder Process to the Originating `Nx_Port`; communication with image pairs not established is not allowed. Also includes adjacent rules for `7.1.2.5`, `Establish Image Pair bit`, `Informative mode`, and `Binding mode`. Content types: message/frame format, response/branch handling, negotiation, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 7.1.2.5 PA required by originator and responder
nodes.display_title: 7.1.2.5 PA required by originator and responder
nodes.raw_title: PA required by originator and responder
nodes.full_title: 7.1.2.5 PA required by originator and responder
nodes.summary: Covers PA-required communication when both originator and responder require valid `PA` values. Core rules: communication should occur only when valid `Originator PA` and `Responder PA` are specified; `PRLI` request pages shall contain valid `Originator PA` and `Responder PA` and set `Establish Image Pair` bit to one to create image pairs in a binding manner. `Responder PA` values may be learned via informative `PRLI`. `PRLI` and `PRLO` pages with complete PA information enable/disable the specified image pair; incomplete `Originator PA`/`Responder PA` information in `PRLI` is invalid. Communication with unestablished image pairs is not allowed. Content types: procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions, timer/negotiation (`Informative mode`, `Binding mode`, `Establish Image Pair`). No tables. No figures.

Covers PRLI/PRLO relationship rules for establishing and using Process_Associator (PA) images, especially `7.1.2.1 Introduction`, `7.1.2.2 PA not supported`, and `7.1.2.3 PA required by originator, supported by responder`. Defines cases where one or both `Originator`/`Responder` do not support `PAs` (no valid `PA` in any frame of any exchange), and where the `Originator` requires `PAs`, including valid `Originator PA` and invalid `Responder PA` values in `PRLI` request pages, use of `Association_Header`, `TYPE of FC-4`, and per-process access control via `PRLI`/`PRLO`. States that communication with image pairs not established is not allowed. Content types: timer/negotiation, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions. No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 7.1.3 Mode of operation
7.1.3.1 Informative mode
nodes.summary: Core topic: `7.1.3.1 Informative mode` in `7.1.3 Mode of operation` defines PRLI operation where `Service Parameter` information is exchanged to enable later image pair negotiation, contrasted with `7.1.3.2 Binding mode`. Exact anchors: `PRLI`, `PRLO`, `Association_Header`, `Responder PA`, `Originator PA`, `PA`, `Nx_Port`, `FC-4`, `PRLI request pages`, `Establish Image Pair bit`, `Informative PRLI`, `Binding PRLI`, `Directory Services`, `image pair`. Includes surrounding rules from `7.1.2.4 PA required by responder, supported by originator` and `7.1.2.5 PA required by originator and responder`: valid/invalid `Responder PA` and `Originator PA` usage, complete vs incomplete PA information, and prohibition on communication with image pairs not established. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions. Tables/figures: none.

nodes.title: 7.1.3.2 Binding mode
nodes.summary: Core topic: `7.1.3.2 Binding mode` defines PRLI-based binding operation where exchanged information explicitly establishes relationships between processes in communicating `Nx_Ports`; only communication types/paths established by `PRLI` are allowed. Exact anchors: `Binding mode`, `PRLI`, `Binding PRLI page`, `Informative PRLI`, `PRLI request page`, `Establish Image Pair bit`, `Originator`, `Responder`, `PAs`, `Nx_Ports`, `Directory Services`. A `Binding PRLI page` requires the `Originator` to have precise knowledge of `Responder` `PAs` and capabilities, obtainable via `Directory Services`, out-of-scope configuration, or an `Informative PRLI`. `Binding` versus `Informative mode` is determined by the setting of the `Establish Image Pair bit` in the `PRLI request page`. Content types: timer/negotiation.

Covers `7.1.3 Mode of operation`: the two PRLI operating modes for process/image-pair setup and how subsequent communication is constrained. `7.1.3.1 Informative mode` exchanges `Service Parameter` information to enable later negotiation for image pair establishment. `7.1.3.2 Binding mode` exchanges information that explicitly establishes a relationship between processes in communicating `Nx_Ports`; no communication types or paths other than those established by the `PRLI` are allowed. Mode selection is determined by the `Establish Image Pair bit` in the `PRLI request page`. Binding use requires precise knowledge of `PAs` and capabilities in the `Responder`, obtainable from `Directory Services`, configuration outside FC scope, or an `Informative PRLI`. Content types: timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 7.1.4 Protocol
7.1.4.1 PA required by originator and responder
nodes.display_title: 7.1.4.1 PA required by originator and responder
nodes.raw_title: PA required by originator and responder
nodes.full_title: 7.1.4.1 PA required by originator and responder
nodes.summary: Section `7.1.4.1 PA required by originator and responder`: rules for `PRLI request page` and `LS_ACC response page` when both `Originator PA validity bits` and `Responder PA validity bits` are set to one and valid `Originator PA` / `Responder PA` values are specified. In requests, `Service Parameters` `requirements` reflect the combined understanding expected to be agreed to by the `Responder`; `capabilities` are those of the `Originator` only. In responses, `requirements` are those agreed to by the `Responder`; `capabilities` are those of the `Responder` only. Each page identifies a `Binding mode PRLI operation` between one image at the Originator `Nx_Port` and one image at the Responder `Nx_Port`. `Error responses` are possible. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions. No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 7.1.4.2 PA required by originator, supported by responder
nodes.display_title: 7.1.4.2 PA required by originator, supported by responder
nodes.raw_title: PA required by originator, supported by responder
nodes.full_title: 7.1.4.2 PA required by originator, supported by responder
nodes.summary: Section `7.1.4.2 PA required by originator, supported by responder` defines PRLI `Binding mode` behavior when the `Originator PA validity bit` is `one` and the `Responder PA validity bit` is `zero`. For each `PRLI request page`, a valid `Originator PA` is specified; `Service Parameter requirements` reflect the combined understanding expected to be agreed to by the Responder, and `Service Parameter capabilities` are those of the Originator only. For each `LS_ACC response page`, the `Originator PA` is returned; `Service Parameter requirements` are those agreed to by the Responder, and `Service Parameter capabilities` are those of the Responder only. `Error responses are possible`. Content types: procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling, timer/negotiation. No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 7.1.4.3 PA supported by originator, required by responder
nodes.display_title: 7.1.4.3 PA supported by originator, required by responder
nodes.raw_title: PA supported by originator, required by responder
nodes.full_title: 7.1.4.3 PA supported by originator, required by responder
nodes.summary: Covers `7.1.4.3 PA supported by originator, required by responder`: PRLI/LS_ACC page semantics when the Originator supports but does not require PA, and the Responder requires PA. In each `PRLI request page`, `Originator PA validity bit` = zero, `Responder PA validity bit` = one, and a valid `Responder PA` is specified. In each `LS_ACC response page`, `Originator PA validity bit` = zero, `Responder PA validity bit` = one, and the `Responder PA` is returned. `Service Parameter requirements` are those expected to be agreed to / agreed to by the Responder; `Service Parameter capabilities` are those of the Originator in requests and the Responder in responses. Includes response/branch handling: `Error responses are possible`. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, message/frame format, negotiation.

Protocol rules for PRLI page semantics in Binding mode, covering how Service Parameters act as requirements vs capabilities and how `Originator PA validity bit`, `Responder PA validity bit`, `Originator PA`, and `Responder PA` are carried in `PRLI request page` and `LS_ACC response page`. Anchors: `PRLI`, `PRLI request page`, `LS_ACC response page`, `Originator`, `Responder`, `Originator Nx_Port`, `Responder Nx_Port`, `Binding mode PRLI operation`, `Service Parameters`, `Originator PA validity bit`, `Responder PA validity bit`, `Originator PA`, `Responder PA`, `FC-4`, `FC-SB-3`, `FCP-2`, `Nx_Port`. Subcases: `7.1.4.1 PA required by originator and responder`; `7.1.4.2 PA required by originator, supported by responder`; `7.1.4.3 PA supported by originator, required by responder`. Content types: procedure/flow; message/frame format; response/branch handling; timer/negotiation; completion/exception conditions (`Error responses are possible`).

Defines **Process Login (PRLI)** in section **7.1 Process Login**: the **PRLI ELS request** / **PRLI ELS Sequence** establishes the operating environment between related process groups at originating and responding **Nx_Port**s. Covers duration until process logout, separation from **N_Port Login**, and the two types: **Implicit Process Login** and **Explicit Process Login**. Names key identifiers/fields: **Process_Associator**, **D_ID**, **Responder Process_Associator**, **S_ID**, **Originator Process_Associator**, **N_Port_ID**; defines an **image** and **image pair**. References **figure 4** (Image/Group of Related Processes; shows image/group behind an **Nx_Port**) and **figure 5** (image pair; cited as the identifier combination example). Includes **table_0189** / “**Table fragment on page 197**” (table fragment present; content not described). Content types: procedure/flow, message/frame format.

nodes.title: 7.2 Process Logout
nodes.summary: Section 7.2 `Process Logout`: defines use of the `PRLO` ELS request to invalidate the operating environment between an image at the initiating `Nx_Port` and an image at the recipient `Nx_Port`, freeing resources from prior `PRLI`. Covers `TYPE-specific Service Parameter` reset and image-pair removal semantics, scoped by `Originator Process Associator` and `Responder Process Associator`. Response/branch handling: removal/reset takes effect only on `LS_ACC` reply Sequence receipt/send; nonexistent image pair or `FC-4 TYPE` still accepted with `LS_ACC` if no link errors; `P_BSY`, `F_BSY`, `P_RJT`, `F_RJT` permit retry; no valid response means action status cannot be assumed, retry is recommended/vendor unique. Completion/exception conditions: after `PRLO`, no `Information Units` for that image pair/`FC-4 TYPE`; active `Sequences` and `Exchanges` terminated via `ABTS`. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

structure.summary: Covers `7 Process Login/Logout`, specifically `7.1 Process Login` / `7.1.1 Introduction`: purpose and scope of the `Process Login (PRLI) ELS request` and `PRLI ELS Sequence` for establishing the operating environment between related processes at originating and responding `Nx_Port`s. Names and identifiers present: `PRLI`, `Process Login`, `Process Logout`, `N_Port Login`, `Implicit Process Login`, `Explicit Process Login`, `Process_Associator`, `D_ID`, `Responder Process_Associator`, `S_ID`, `Originator Process_Associator`, `image`, `image pair`, `N_Port_ID`, `Nx_Port`, `N_Port`, `FC-4`. States/conditions: PRLI remains in effect until process logout; concurrent logins depend on `Nx_Port` facilities; explicit vs implicit branch. Figures: `Figure 4 – Image/Group of Related Processes` shows image/group behind an `Nx_Port`; `figure_0001`, `figure_0002`, `figure_0003`, `figure_0004` are figure crops on page 197. Table: `table_0189` (`Table fragment on page 197`) shows related process/image fragment. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

structure.title: 8 Virtual Fabrics N_Port Support

nodes.title: 8.1 Overview
nodes.summary: Covers Virtual Fabrics N_Port support overview and initial enablement context: `VFT_Header` (see `FC-FS-2`) tags frames with `VF_ID` so tagged frames from different Virtual Fabrics can share one physical link; support may exist on `N_Ports`, `F_Ports`, and `E_Ports`. Includes `FLOGI` negotiation via the `Virtual Fabrics bit` in `FLOGI Common Service Parameters` (see `table 150`) and `FLOGI LS_ACC` behavior indicating whether the Fabric requires `EVFP` (`see 4.2.43`). `Table 177 – Virtual Fabrics Bit Usage` shows request/configuration/response combinations for `Virtual Fabrics Bit = 0b/1b` and whether Virtual Fabrics are allowed. Also notes `D_ID = 000000h` in `FLOGI LS_ACC` when EVFP is requested, no `N_Port_ID` assignment, and use of `FFFFF0h` as `S_ID`. Content types: timer/negotiation, response/branch handling, procedure/flow.

nodes.title: 8.2 Enabling VFT Tagging on N_Ports
nodes.summary: Covers enabling VFT tagging on `N_Ports` during `FLOGI`/`LS_ACC` negotiation and when `EVFP` must be initiated. Key negotiation fields/codes: `Virtual Fabrics bit`, `Security bit`, `D_ID = 000000h`, `N_Port Controller Well Known Address = FFFFF0h`, `S_ID`, `VF_ID`, `N_Port_ID`, `N_Port_Name`, `Fabric_Name`, `VFT_Header`. `Table 177 – Virtual Fabrics Bit Usage` defines request/`F_Port` configuration vs `LS_ACC` behavior for `Virtual Fabrics Bit = 0b/1b`. `Figure 6 – Logical N_Port Initialization` shows initialization flow for Virtual Fabrics and Logical `N_Ports`. State machine content: `P1: FLOGI`, `P2: AUTH_ELS`, `P3: N_Port`, `P4: Process EVFP`, `P5(k): FLOGI`, `P6(k): AUTH_ELS`, `P7(k): Logical N_Port`, `P8: Isolated`, plus transitions `P1:P2`, `P1:P3`, `P2:P3`, `P1:P4`, `P2:P4`, `P4:P5(k)`, `P5(k):P6(k)`. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, state machine, timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 8.3 Configuration Information
nodes.summary: Section 8.3 Configuration Information: defines VF capable N_Port configuration parameters used for VFT tagging and Virtual Fabric operation: `Tagging Administrative Status`, `Port VF_ID`, and `Locally-Enabled VF_ID List`. Page also includes adjacent state-machine/flow anchors for link behavior: `Transition P5(k):P7(k)`, `Transition P6(k):P7(k)`, `State P7(k): Logical N_Port`, `Transition P4:P1`, `Transition P4:P8`, `State P8: Isolated`; protocol/message anchors `FLOGI`, `FLOGI LS_ACC`, `Security bit`, `EVFP`, `Link Reset`, `Link Initialization`, `Exchange Virtual Fabrics Parameters ELS (EVFP)`. Key branches/completion conditions: `Security bit set to zero`, authentication completes successfully, EVFP determines tagging/not-tagging and matching/non-matching `Port VF_ID`. Tables: none. Figures: none. Contains: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, state machine, negotiation, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 8.4 Exchange Virtual Fabrics Parameters Processing
8.4.1 Overview
nodes.summary: EVFP protocol overview for VF capable `N_Port`/`F_Port`: negotiates `VFT Tagging` mode, checks `Port VF_ID` consistency, and establishes operational Virtual Fabrics. Procedure/flow + timer/negotiation + response/branch handling + completion/exception conditions + state machine. EVFP transaction roles: `EVFP Initiator`, `EVFP Responder`; unique `Transaction Identifier (T_ID)`; phases `EVFP_SYNC` then `EVFP_COMMIT`; uses `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, code `'EVFP collision'`. Figure `7 – A Generic EVFP Transaction`: shows `EVFP_SYNC / T_ID=Q`, `LS_ACC / T_ID=Q`, `EVFP_COMMIT / T_ID=Q`, enabling reception/transmission of VFT tagged frames. Named fields/identities: `Tagging Administrative Status`, `Port VF_ID`, `Locally-Enabled VF_ID List`, `Core N_Port_Name`, `Core Switch_Name`. Special identifier/code: `Control VF_ID`, `FEFh`. Branches/states: `P1`, `P4`, `P5(k)`, `P8`, `Isolated`; references `table 126`; NULL operational VF_ID set handling and simultaneous-start collision resolution.

nodes.title: 8.4.2 Changing the VFT Tagging Mode
nodes.summary: Section `8.4.2 Changing the VFT Tagging Mode` covers the EVFP procedure/flow for changing link VFT tagging mode after a management change to `Administrative Tagging Mode` on a VF-capable `N_Port` or `F_Port` that supports the `EVFP protocol`. `EVFP_SYNC` initiates; `LS_ACC` responds. Message carriage rule: if currently performing tagging, EVFP messages are tagged with the `Control VF_ID`; if not performing tagging, EVFP messages are untagged. `EVFP_SYNC` carries updated `Tagging Administrative Status`, `Port VF_ID`, and `Locally-Enabled VF_ID List`; `LS_ACC` carries responder `Tagging Administrative Status`, `Port VF_ID`, and `Locally-Enabled VF_ID List`. Response/branch handling uses `table 126` to decide whether `VFT Tagging` changes; if not, terminate EVFP on exchange completion; if yes, the `EVFP Responder` performs link initialization and the `EVFP Initiator` participates. Contained types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

nodes.title: 8.4.3 Adding or Removing Virtual Fabrics
nodes.summary: Covers EVFP behavior for adding or removing Virtual Fabrics when a management action changes the `Locally-Enabled VF_ID List` on a tagging `N_Port` or `F_Port`. Procedure/flow and response/branch handling: all EVFP protocol messages are tagged with the `Control VF_ID`; the `EVFP Initiator` sends `EVFP_SYNC` carrying `Tagging Administrative Status`, `Port VF_ID`, and updated `Locally-Enabled VF_ID List` (see `4.2.43.2.4`); the `EVFP Responder` replies with `LS_ACC` carrying its `Tagging Administrative Status`, `Port VF_ID`, and `Locally-Enabled VF_ID List`, then acts depending on the resulting operational `VF_ID List` (see `4.2.43.2.4`). Anchors: `EVFP`, `EVFP_SYNC`, `LS_ACC`, `Control VF_ID`, `Locally-Enabled VF_ID List`, `Tagging Administrative Status`, `Port VF_ID`, operational `VF_ID List`, `N_Port`, `F_Port`. No tables. No figures.

nodes.title: 8.4.4 Changing the Port VF_ID
nodes.summary: Section `8.4.4 Changing the Port VF_ID` covers management-triggered behavior when the `Port VF_ID` is changed on `N_Port` or `F_Port`, distinguishing tagging vs non tagging ports and `Isolated state` handling. For a tagging `N_Port` or `F_Port`, no changes are applied to the link. For a non tagging `N_Port` or `F_Port`, the `FC_Port` shall perform a link initialization. If the `Port VF_ID` changes while the `N_Port` or `F_Port` is in `Isolated state` (`state P8`), the `FC_Port` shall go to `state P1` (see `8.2`). Content types: state machine, completion/exception conditions. Tables: none. Figures: none.

Section `8.4 Exchange Virtual Fabrics Parameters Processing` / `8.4.1 Overview`: overview of the `Exchange Virtual Fabrics Parameters ELS (EVFP)` protocol between a `VF capable N_Port` and a `VF capable F_Port`. Core topic: EVFP is used to negotiate `VFT Tagging` operational mode, verify consistency of the two `Port VF_ID`s, and establish the list of `operational Virtual Fabrics` across the link. Exact anchors present: `Exchange Virtual Fabrics Parameters ELS (EVFP)`, `VFT Tagging`, `Port VF_ID`, `VF capable N_Port`, `VF capable F_Port`, `operational Virtual Fabrics`. Content types: `timer/negotiation` (mode/VF negotiation), `procedure/flow` (protocol purpose/processing overview). Tables: none. Figures: none.

structure.summary: Covers Virtual Fabrics support on `N_Ports`: `VFT_Header` tagging with `VF_ID`, shared-link tagged-frame use across VFs, and how Virtual Fabrics are enabled during `FLOGI`. Includes `procedure/flow`, `response/branch handling`, and `timer/negotiation` content. Key anchors: `Virtual Fabrics bit`, `FLOGI`, `LS_ACC`, `EVFP` transaction (`see 4.2.43`), `FLOGI Common Service Parameters`, `table 150`, `D_ID`, `N_Port_ID`, `N_Port Controller Well Known Address`, `S_ID`, `000000h`, `FFFFF0h`, `Authentication transaction`. Explains negotiation outcomes when the `Virtual Fabrics bit` is `0b` or `1b` in request/reply, including when the Fabric requires `EVFP` and returns `LS_ACC with Virtual Fabrics Bit = 1b`. `Table 177 – Virtual Fabrics Bit Usage`: shows request/configuration/behavior combinations for Virtual Fabrics allowed/not allowed.
